Trying these on in Southern California I will say they are very warm but got them for camping season. No smell color is GORG and maybe the slightest stretch but not much. I will say I tried them on in store and they weren’t the loose fit (different colors) and I was going to be an XL Tall so the loose fit and regular fit make a difference!!!!!

Perfect in every way I have such a hard time finding a 38” inseam so when I saw these I had to jump on them and I’m so glad I did. I absolutely love everything about these! They are true to size, so comfortable and so cute. Also can’t beat the price! I will definitely be ordering more of these.

The (short) length is lovely! I’ve been going back and forth with these. They are comfortable. The lining inside is soft and cozy and the leggings are stretchy and the waistband has give that make them lovely for relaxing or just hanging out. I had no problem with sheerness (at least in the lighting I checked in lol). I ordered the short size, because I’m 5’1 and short and the length was great. It’s super hard for me to find comfy and/or activewear bottoms that are the correct length so this was a treat. This may sound nitpicky, but I just really wish they had pockets. I almost always purchase leggings with leg pockets and these don’t have them. The inclusion of a side pocket would make them a five-star for sure. The other reason for deduction is my concern about how they’ll hold up over time. I have machine washed/dried them a couple times and they held up fine, but the material just seems moderate and not great so I think they may end up piling fairly early on. I’ll update over time on how they hold up, but that’s my main quarrel. For the price the material is fair, but I’m taking into consideration since there are other options in a similar price point with less piling-inducing fabric. Otherwise though, I do like these and can’t emphasize enough how amazing it is to find pants like these with the right length!

Super comfortable with a slightly dressy look I ordered the X-Large / 24” inseam Petite, and these fit perfectly. For reference, I’m 5'1", about 195 lbs, and usually wear a large or XL. The length is ideal; they hit about an inch above my ankle bone, which is exactly what I want in a petite pant. The fabric is smooth, stretchy, and very comfortable, with a nice weight to it. They feel thick and high quality without being too hot, so the temperature control is excellent. The waistband is comfortable and flattering, not tight or restrictive. Very flattering from the back! The zippered pockets are very functional; the zippers are small, but the pockets themselves are surprisingly roomy, which I really appreciate. The pants also have a slightly dressy look, making them easy to wear casually or dress up a bit. Overall, these are comfortable, well-made, and thoughtfully designed. I would definitely buy these again and highly recommend them.

Fit great and nice and comfy! I sized up because sometimes I wear a 4 nowadays but some sizes run small in jeans and I kind of expected that with these jeans so I went for a 6 Regular. So I am short and only 5’3” but these jeans aren’t really that long plus I want to tuck them into boots in the winter as it’s getting cold out this time of year. The fit is not too loose maybe kinda baggy which I like. A size 4 probably would have been okay but definitely not practical for Thanksgiving dinner and sitting for long car rides to visit family for the Holidays.This time I am definitely all about comfort and even though these are “Mom jeans” they actually look pretty good on my 54 year old bod! Husband approves so I’m happy with them!

Beautiful Boots! These are really nice boots! I was surprised at the quality for the price point. They run true to size - I normally wear an 8.5 and often struggle to find boots that fit both my feet and calves. I also have one foot that is slightly larger than the other and the front of my feet is very wide compared with my heels. Finding regular shoes is also a struggle because those that fit my heels (and don't rub) often pinch my toes. I've even had a podiatrist suggest that I consider purchasing custom made shoes due to the shape of my feet. I decided to take a chance and order these in my normal size and was pleasantly surprised when they fit perfectly. No pinching my toes and no slipping in the heels! I was also a bit skeptical about whether I would be able to get them on without a zipper that runs the full length of the boot, but there are no issues there either. They are very stylish and can be dressed up or down, as the occasion requires. They are very nicely made and seem to be great quality. I've seen similar boots priced much higher so the value for the money is good. The only reason that I removed a star is because I feel like they would have been even better with more padding for the sole of the foot. The heel height puts quite a bit of pressure on the ball of the foot and more padding would have made them even more comfortable. I plan to wear them mainly to church in the fall/winter for a couple of hours on Sundays, so I don't expect it will be too much of an issue for me. But I don't think I would be able to wear them for extended periods standing on my feet. Of course, I am nearly 60 years old and the padding on the soles of our feet degrades with age (I guess it moves up to our midsection - LOL!). Younger wearers will probably find them just fine.

Fits awkward body type! I have diabetes, and am insulin resistant. Because of this, my stomach bulges like a pregnant lady. It’s hard to find pants that fit right because they are always too tight in the waist and loose on the legs. If I get them to fit my waist right, the legs will be very loose and look so bad and frumpy. I am 5’7 and 175lbs, much of it carried in my stomach area. I chose 12Long but may have been able to size down 1 size, but they would be very snug. If you are my size and body type, I’d say stick with 12L, but if you prefer tight pants go down 1. If you have a big booty, go up 1. These pants fit my stomach AND legs properly. I have longer legs and the length covered my ankles very well and did not rise throughout the day like high waters, even with me bending and stooping down a lot at work. They were very soft and comfortable while still holding me in and giving me a nice shape overall. Made me look like a woman instead of an Oompa Loompa. I have only worn them once, but I have a cat and chinchilla so my black clothes always hold hair, but the hair did not stick to these. 5 stars! Will buy again! FYI: The back pockets are not real pockets.

High-Quality, Comfortable Golf Joggers with Secure Zipper Pockets (Great for Petites) I was very impressed with the quality of these pants. The fabric feels great and the stitching is well done. I especially love the zipper pockets, which give the joggers a unique and practical touch. This feature is essential for me, especially when traveling through the airport—I like keeping my ID and phone in my pockets, and the zippers give me peace of mind that nothing will fall out. These pants do not have an adjustable drawstring at the waist, which initially concerned me. However, the elastic waistband fits comfortably and didn’t need any adjustment at all. I also really appreciate the length options. I chose the 27” inseam because I felt the 24” would be too short, and I prefer a longer length. As a petite woman, it’s hard to find pants that fit well, so I love that this brand offers options specifically for petite sizing. I followed the size chart, and it was very accurate. Overall, I love these pants and find them perfect for everyday wear and errands. I just wish they came in more color options.

Comfortable I'm so happy! I've always had issues finding long enough anything. I'm 5'8 140 lbs. I have a shorter torso and longer legs (31.5 from inner top thigh to ankle bone). I usually buy 34 in inseam because I don't like pants to ride up too far above my ankle when sitting. These are perfect. They're light weight, not winter worthy, and breathable. I have thinner legs, thigh is 21.5 in. around, so they aren't tight but fitting and not overly baggy. My waist/ hips area widest point is 39 in. and medium fits well. I do have animals and while hair attaches, it does not stick. If I see any I can easily shake it off. I always wash in cold and line dry so I cannot attest to the dryer and any shrinkage issues. This is my first full day of wearing and if anything changes I'll update.

Love these leggings! Overall, these leggings are great! The material is buttery soft while still feeling supportive, and the stretch makes them incredibly comfortable for all-day wear. I especially love the high-waisted band—it offers just the right amount of tummy support without feeling too constricting. The hidden scrunch and V-back design are subtle but flattering, giving a natural, lifted look. They’re perfect for life on the go or a workout at the gym. For reference, I’m 5’3” and 116 lbs wearing an extra small. I chose the 28” inseam, and while they’re slightly long for my height, I personally like having that little bit of extra length.

Very cute, lace is pretty and has some stretch These are very cute very trendy pants. I got them for my daughter who is 5’7” and very thin, she wears usually a size 2 to 4 in women’s pants. These came down exactly to her ankles in length and she really liked them. The shorts are actually a nice length as well, they are very short, but they aren’t showing butt cheek like I thought they were going to, so that’s a relief. The lace top layer helps with a little bit of coverage there too, but the point of these is to look a little provocative, I guess. The material is good quality and they made pretty well, they don’t feel cheap and the lace has a little stretched to them too so they don’t feel stiff or like they’ll tear easily. I feel like you can actually dress these up or down. Overall, I think they are a cute choice for a party or to go out and I would recommend them.

Super comfy! These pants a super soft and comfy! They have so many pockets; 2 normal pockets and then 2 more, one on each leg. The waist hits right at the belly button and is adjustable with drawstrings. The fabric is stretchy and seems like a really nice quality. I am 5’11” with a 36” inseam and weigh 150lbs. I got the size large tall. The only reason I didn’t give 5 stars is because the tall size is still too short on me. It may just be my preference but I like my pants to touch the ground when barefoot and these do not. Most people will not have this issue but just a heads up for the tall girls. Anyway, there are great pants and I would definitely recommend.

Most comfortable work pants ever ! I finally found a pair of work slacks that really feel like yoga pants. These pants are lightweight, breathable and with the right amount of stretch. As you move they feel like a second skin. Perfect for hot humid days. I got a M- gray, the color I got is elegant and the fit is very comfortable. The slacks have an elastic waistband at the back and deep front pockets that fit my S23 Ultra, plus two back pockets that enhance my rear. At 5'5 the length is about 29" falling right at my ankles. Their high waisted style provides me with a smooth, flattering look fit, without a muffin top. I love this material because its 20% stretch makes it perfect for the office, and I don't have to worry about wrinkles, bending, or ironing. These are professionally styled and I'm happy I got them. At that value you should get a few colors.

Almost Perfect I honestly don’t know what magic they used to make these sweatpants, but the material is amazing. They are soft and stretchy with a velvety feel that’s comfortable to wear. I’m usually a size 8 and about 5'6", and I went with the medium in the 29" inseam, which ended up fitting really well. They look more polished than other sweatpants and I also appreciate how they don’t wrinkle easily, which makes them great for travel. The only thing I’m not a fan of is that the outline of the pockets shows through, and that does take away from the elevated look, especially because the pants feel high quality otherwise. Still, aside from that detail, I am really happy with these and love wearing them.

SUPER soft and comfortable! Super comfortable, well-fitting leggings! These are very, very soft. Sometimes the leggings that are described as “buttery” are kind of slick, but these a just super soft. I love the bright red color. The pockets on the side are great for holding my cell phone. The length is perfect and they fit all around (I’m 5’5” and about 125 lbs). It’s high-waisted and feels very comfortable around my waist, not too tight but enough stretchiness to fit snugly. I am wearing black underwear in my pictures so you can see they aren’t see through. If I had to make one complaint, I don’t really like the seams that run up the front of the legs, but definitely not a deal-breaker. I know I’ll wear these often!

Good Quality Jeans!! These are so good. I bought a blue wash and the black wash, and they’ve become my two favorite pants that I wear as often as I can. The style is great, goes with all my shirts, can be dressed up or dressed down. I’m 5’3”, 120lbs, and I usually buy size Small/size2-6 on jeans. Too tight, too long for golf pants. I usually order the size Amazon recommends because it usually fits. In this case it did and it didn’t. The fabric in these pants is stretchy and I think if I had ordered the next size up they would have been loose in the waist, especially as I wore them through they day. But, the legs were very tight, long and ended up being super scrunched. I need golf pants as I only have warm weather golf clothes but these definitely will not work. If the pants were made a bit wider and not as long they would work. As they are now, they don’t for me. I will be returning them. I am 5’3, 113 and ordered XS in Gray. The color is good and pockets and material seem fine, other than tightness in way they are designed.

Thank you reviews that recommended sizing down First off, I LOVE these jeans! Secondly, the reviews I read on here were a huge help in sizing. It’s weird because this are definitely Women’s jeans, but the sizing is in Juniors. For reference I am 6’0, 170-175 lbs, and a solid Women’s size 10/30, which would translate to a Juniors size 11. Per the suggestion of previous reviews, I reluctantly sized down to a 9. The last thing I wanted was for the jeans to fit for an hour, only to stretch beyond repair. I ordered a size 9 with 34” inseam. I am SO happy I did! When I pulled it out the bag I thought to myself, there’s NO WAY this is gonna work. But I squeezed myself into the initially doubtful fit. Wisely I decided to wear them around the house for a couple hours, w/o removing tags (just in case.. again, because of reviews). And to my pleasant surprise this wonderful pair of jeans decided to form to my figure in the best ways possible. So if you’re in doubt and you got some junk in the trunk, size down and trust the process. Because I could totally see if I had bought these in an 11, I wouldn’t like them now. Also the 34” inseam does almost hit the floor for me, but the next pair I order will be a 36”, just so I have some extra length.

Good Color and Fit for 5’5” These were a good purchase. I am 5 feet 5 inches and weigh 219 lbs. I got a size 18W short. They are the perfect length for the shoes I will typically wear them with. They are a nice deep (not faded) black color. They have good stretch. I normally wear a size 20W but an 18W fit well with the stretch. They are very comfortable. The only thing I can’t on is the washing. Typically I buy one pair of black jeans a year and have to replace them due to fading after washing. I am going to try and keep these ones from fading if I can.

I love the look and feel of the fabric, and the style of the cargo pockets. I ordered these in dark gray size large. I usually wear bottoms in numerical size US12. The inseam measured 31 inches. For me (5' 4" height) these are perfect if I am wearing shoes that aren't completely flat. They work with my athletic shoes which have some height to them (see photo).I love the look and feel of the fabric.Thes do not have an elastic-banded waist, the crossover style waist stretches due to the stretchy fabric they are mode from. In my photo you can see that I measured the unstretched waist of the pants (flat on the floor) at about 14 inches side to side. My waist is in the mid-thirty inches and these were comfortable. But I would not want anyone to be able to see every bump and bulge of my waist area while wearing these, so for sure I am wearing a longer length top that covers my stomach.These pants are pretty form fitting through the stomach and thighs until they flare out and are wide around the calves.I really like the style and interest that the cargo pockets add.

In my photo you can see that I measured the unstretched waist of the pants (flat on the floor) at about 14 inches side to side. My waist is in the mid-thirty inches and these were comfortable. But I would not want anyone to be able to see every bump and bulge of my waist area while wearing these, so for sure I am wearing a longer length top that covers my stomach.These pants are pretty form fitting through the stomach and thighs until they flare out and are wide around the calves.I really like the style and interest that the cargo pockets add.

Comfy and Cute! I got the 'Forest Camo' and they're perfect! The fabric is like a soft jean material and very comfortable, especially with the elastic waistband. It has a decent bit of give to the fabric so it's not stiff. I LOVE the elastic at the ankles, if they didn't have them I would probably have to hem the pants. The size guide is accurate in my opinion, I ordered an XXL to get the oversized look. I am about 5'2" and 175lbs. I am very happy with these pants.

True to size perfection, if you’re shorter like me choose Petite. Just wow, these pants are seriously the most comfortable, beyond soft and I want all the colors now. I am so happy I ordered them, I actually hesitated as I thought they might be too warm for the almost non existent winter months where I live. Besides the buttery comfort factor, they fit amazing. The cargo style takes the traditional sweatpants up a notch giving them a cool vibe. The semi wide, straight legs isn’t so wide they look clownish and the U Seam Stitching across the backside really emphasizes that area, doing what it should. I have zero complaints. I chose my regular size SP and found the fit comfortable. The elastic waist does not dig in, the drawstrings are functional so you’ve got wiggle room if you prefer a more cinched waistline. Pockets are deep enough for everyday essentials and my iPhone Pro Max. For reference I am 5’ tall and weigh approximately 124lbs with measurements 34DD/27”W/36”H I’ve attached a few photos for you. The color Ivory is beautiful, a really soft tone that’ll be perfect for the months ahead.

Lifesaver Fit: I needed stylish slacks for an event. I read many reviews, and saw that many women much heavier than I am were raving about the fit of size medium. Since I am 73 years old and not interested in snug fitting clothing I knew that medium would be perfect for me. I am 5'3" and 120 lbs. I chose the 29" inseam.

A good inseam fit for up to 6’1” women!! I gave these pants a shot and at medium they were too loose annnd way too long, so here is what my sister said: THE Perfect inseam length for women over 5’9”. I would suggest ordering your true size because they’re stretchy but I would not go up a size. Even if you gain 30-40 lbs they will still fit, I promise. The side pockets are nice and lay flat. The seams are well finished & they are incredibly comfortable. I am 20 weeks and all belly & thighs so these are appropriate for work, church, running errands, or going out to lunch.

Beautiful!!! Sexy! My 18 yo daughter LOVES these. They are gorgeous, quality is amazing and they are surprisingly comfortable. The wide calf boot is great because we have LARGE calves and the boots fit both of us. The white is so bright and vibrant, it's amazing. Great value in my opinion.

Beautiful!!! Sexy! My 18 yo daughter LOVES these. They are gorgeous, quality is amazing and they are surprisingly comfortable. The wide calf boot is great because we have LARGE calves and the boots fit both of us. The white is so bright and vibrant, it's amazing. Great value in my opinion.

No complaints, dare I say perfection! I absolutely adore these! Honestly they are so comfortable it almost feels like nothing at all. They drape lovely on the body with the front pockets large enough but yet without adding additional bulk. Nice a smooth to touch and look. There’s a fair amount of stretch for enough wiggle room if you’re carrying a little extra water weight. The waistband is smooth without rolling down. And love that we have the choice to choose our inseam. I am almost always a size small, wearing a size 4us at 27”W/36”H at 124lbs. I chose size small petite. For these, if I could have tried on first I would have chose XS Petite. So something to think about ladies if you’re close to my sizing. The Small Petite is fine, and I’ll make them work. It’s my own fault for not looking closely at the size chart, it actually suggests I order XS. Please look closely prior to ordering. The coffee brown color is a great choice going into fall. I will be wearing these on repeat and think I’ll need another color! I can easily style these for a smart casual look with a sharp white button down, or a simple tee with a coordinating sweater tied around my neck. The length is nice too. I’m barely 5’ but with sneakers or a little kitten heels or ankle boots they hang perfect. Seriously worth the try with versatile styling options, comfy fit and front pockets make these a great value in my opinion.

Cozy, comfortable and good quality I am very happy with these pants. The 29” inseam length hits the top of my feet on my 5’4” frame. The waistband has just enough stretch and the fleece lining makes them extremely comfortable to wear. The outside has a nice sheen to it that drapes well and the overall silhouette is flattering. The only pocket in these pants are on the center back but it spans 2/3 of the way across the waistband and is deep enough to fit my phone. I’m glad to have this as the cooler weather is coming and I think these will be very cozy.

Red has some pink, but base your fit on the size chart and your measurements Really cute wide leg jeans. I got the red in size 8, based on the sizing chart. The measurements on the chart are more accurate than what you think your size is. I’m a 6 in Banana and Crew but 8 fit me better in this pant. I am 5’5 135# for reference. LOVE the wide leg and unfinished hem, about 30” inseam, I should be able to wear a low or mid heel boot or sandal with these. I do love the red but it has a lot of pink in it and not a true RED red, like the photos might suggest.

IMPRESSED. 5'0", 160 pounds, Medium petite No regrets! And I've tried a lot of scrubs... A lot of my fat is stored in my thighs and waist so its a little snug around there. BUT I really dont mind because the material is breathable and stretchy so I can still move around comfortably! The rest of the leg is NOT super baggy like most scrubs or joggers. The length is perfect. I wish the elastic at the bottom was just a little tighter but its not so loose that it looks weird.

Super comfortable pants! These pants are very comfortable! You could even wear them to bed. I wore these for a very long day at work, and they were not binding around the waist, plus the legs are loose and flow nicely when walking. The style length and nice dark green color match a wide variety of footwear. When I washed these, I tossed them in the washer on cold and then in the hot dryer for about 4 minutes. Then I hung them to dry, and they look great - no ironing needed! These do have a drawstring, but I just let it hang because the elastic was fine for my fit. I am going to be wearing these every week. For your reference, I am 5'7" (170cm) and 140 lbs (64kg) in the pictures, and I usually wear a size 2 in pants. UPDATE: These pants do NOT collect dog hair! My dog sheds a lot, but I can sit on my carpet, couch and bed without using the lint roller before I go out in these pants! It's just amazing! Even my shiny track pants pick up some dog hairs, but not these. UPDATE: I bought the navy blue on sale, and they are fabulous too!

The perfect hiking pants Im 6ft tall with a 36" inseam and have never been able to find light weight hiking pants until these. They are super comfy and long, no exposed ankles when you sit down! Great fit and wear. This is my favorite athletic-wear brand right now. I love a good matching set and have six or seven from Pavoi. Pavoi needs to sponsor me because I need more. Please and thank you.

I am 5'3" and 135lbs and the small fits well

Comfy yoga scrubs These yoga scrub pants are super comfortable and have tons of pockets for all of your nursing needs. They're stretchy and easy to move around in. There is a zippered cargo pocket to make sure all of your items are secure. The one pocket has a badge loop on it. My only complaints about these pants would be that there are no back pockets, which is a feature I typically like on my scrub pants. And second is I got this in the ceil color, depending on if your facility requires a certain color, the ceil color that these came in is a bit of a richer of a blue than was standard at hospitals I was required to wear ceil blue at. I don't think it's hospital approved ceil blue. On the plus side, these are comfy enough to Casually wear around the house, or even if you wanted to wear them as like gym pants they are stretchy enough and comfortable enough to do. The fabric is thick and stretchy. With scrubs, I typically get a small in bottoms, as I like a more fitted look, and the smalls fit well for me. I am 5'4, and the length on these pants is perfect

Love the longer length on these, high quality and warm! I am 5'7" and 160lbs with a 31" waist, I normally wear between a size M and L. I ordered these in a size L, 31" inseam. The waist was slightly tighter than I would have liked but the length was great, maybe even slightly longer than expected. Love the length on these, I have long legs for my height and always struggle to get long enough inseams on joggers and pants.These are a thicker fleece lined jogger that is incredibly soft on the inside and very cozy and warm. These wash up great without any shrinkage or pilling. The hand pockets on the front are a big size and zippered with nice quality zippers. These doesn't grab a lot of pet hair or lint and you don't get a lot of fluff sticking to them in the wash.Really no complaints about these, they are great quality and I would recommend!

Very cute and trendy denim capris; true to size and great weight I've recently lost 45 pounds and have realized most of my staple wardrobe items are way too big. It's not capris weather, but I wanted to get ahead of the season and make sure I'm ready for the warmer days ahead. I don't really like wearing shorts, but capris are a go-to pant for me.I am still trying to figure out what size I should be wearing, and based on the measurements provided in the description I picked a size 12. A SIZE TWELVE!!! I am still having a hard time believing it. The dark blue wash is perfect for me. The capris are very well made; the fabric is sturdy but still lightweight. Soft and stretchy. The distressing and frayed hem are on trend. I like that there are functional front and back pockets, a must for me.For reference, I'm 5'11" and weigh about 180. I have a very straight figure and have a hard time with pants fit, but these fit well enough without any areas where they are too tight or loose. These will definitely be a staple item for me in the months ahead. The price point is a little higher than I like to pay for casual pants, but these should last for a long time. Definitely recommend these and I'm considering getting in another wash.

Gorgeous but Hard to Zip Thigh Highs These are very cute and stylish knee-high boots. The shoe’s outer material is a semi-smooth faux suede and the heel is thick and durable. The sole is made of firm rubber about a centimeter thick, and the inner sole is lightly cushioned. I typically wear a size 6-6.5 in shoes, and got these in a 6.5. My feet are slightly wide, and these boots are more narrow. They squeeze my feet a bit, but the part of the boot that I have the biggest issue with is the zipper. They’re very difficult to zip— easier on the right side, but a real struggle on the left. My feet aren’t wide enough that I’ve ever had an issue zipping my shoes— and I’m even able to zip the shoe a bit first and then get my feet in. I really have the most difficulty zipping the left boot above my ankle. I have thin ankles, so it really doesn’t make much sense. The zipper dug into my finger and calloused it from trying to zip the boot. The fabric on the right shoe came together easily, the zipper just wouldn’t zip, but on the left side the fabric seemed too short and particularly restrictive. I knocked off a star for that, as they were a struggle to get on. Once they’re on, they’re comfortable for the most part (with the left boot feeling tighter than the right). They squeeze my feet, but not enough for me to constantly notice it. They’re not the easiest to walk in. I can walk around in them, but they keep my arch pretty high. They’re the kind of boots that I’d wear if I knew that I was only going to be walking to and from the car and then sitting down. If you’re better at walking in heels or wear higher heels often, you may not be bothered by the heel height. If, like me, you tend to wear shorter heels or stick to wedges and ones with a platform when you wear much higher heels— these could be uncomfortable. I’m 4’11” and they go about 3 inches above my knee, and look really cute. Overall, these are gorgeous boots, but they’re more for fashion than practicality. I love the style and the way they look on me, but I’m not a fan of the zipper and narrow fabric near the ankle of the boot.

A little cheap but still great! The material is a little odd, but that is both a pro and a con. Con: it's a little thin, a thicker material would make it seem fancier. Watch out for VPL. Pro: it shrunk length in the wash. This was a pro for me because i would have needed to alter it so it wouldn't drag on the ground. I'm 5'6.5, 175-180 lbs, just had a baby 4 months ago. Pre-pregancy i had 30-33 waist and 40-44 hips, now i have about 36 waist and 45 hips (self measured which is inaccurate sometimes) and had a hard time ordering things the right size so i aimed high and ordered a 2x. Fits great, like leggings, the top cinches the waist so if you wear a tight top, it will create an indent in your silhouette, and your tummy pouch will show on crop tops (i don't mind it). Leg is wide, doesn't feel as pajama-y as palazzo pants, but not as nice as dress pants. An easy way to upgrade your outfits and add pizazz. A little more of a blue green than a yellow green. I included a picture and a video to show movement!

Great for the price These flare pants are a soft cottony materiel. I wouldn’t say they are thick but they aren’t super thin or see through. Sooo Comfortable!! These boots are so comfortable. I was in the market for some new ones when my old ones just gave out on me. I usually order from a department store but decided to go this route. So glad I did. I read the reviews and I was sold. I literally wore these all day with no issues. I like the fact that they don't make a lot of noise when walking on tile (an issue I had with my last boot). Everyone heard me coming...lol! I will say the reviews are pretty accurate on the wide leg gap. Even the models on the site show large gaps. However, for me it wasn't that big of an issue. I'm 5'7" and weigh about 167 pounds and the leg gap was not an issue with a standard legging as you can see in the picture. Jeans would probably fill in the gap more. All in all, a great boot that is comfortable, stylish and durable.

90's Style Sweatpants I bought these 90-style sweatpants for a costume: Rachel from Friends "the one with the football". It worked out perfectly. As far as the quality goes, these are good quality in my opinion. They are thick material, the black hasn't faded after multiple washes. My only complaint(s), and they are minor, is that they seem to attract every hair and every lint imaginable, way more than any other piece of clothing I own. That and the elastic at the legs are very tight and it can get uncomfortable after a while. For reference, I am 5'3", 112 lbs, and ordered small.

Amazing pants! Absolutely recommend! I really like these pants! I’m petite and it’s difficult to find and order pants online, because in most cases they are too long for me. But these have 28” inseam option, so that’s great! Material: so good that I was positively surprised. Doesn’t look cheap at all! So don’t worry about that. It’s important to know that it’s not that stretchy, so make sure to have that in mind before you choose your size. Size: I’m 138 pounds. My regular size is M in bottoms, but even though the Amazon recommended size S for me (because of my measurements), I purchased size M because I wanted it to be more flattering look than tight. I could probably fit in the size S, but this was the better option. Length: I’m 5 ft 4, and it’s changing to order pants online, but I ordered 28” inseam of these, and it was the perfect choice! You can see in the picture that the length is just perfect for me!

Not for taller people My first impression when putting these pants on were regarding the fabric. Buttery soft and very comfortable. I actually kept them on all day because they feel like yoga pants. When it comes to wearing them as slacks, a few things to know…they have a working button, zipper and pockets. So they feel like legit work pants. But much comfier since I could just slip them on without unzipping. They have a lot of stretch to them which is great. I think they are more meant to be worn with a shirt tucked in because the pockets are high up on the high waist rather than on the sides. So they’d be trickier to access with a shirt untucked. The inseam was perfect on me when I pulled them up to the waist. But then the hem was too high for me at 5’7”. And pulling them down to make them longer made the crotch too low. So maybe they’re better for someone a few inches shorter. But with the right shoe, it probably wouldn’t matter. Overall they’re a great pant. Just wish they were longer. I’ll be wearing them with an untucked shirt just because the fabric does hug the body enough to show your shape and I’m not crazy about mine. For reference, I am 165 pounds. Waist is 35” and hip is 42” and I got an XL.

Peach and Sparkly! I love these peach-colored sparkle tights! They are soft and comfortable, and the sparkles are well-affixed. After wearing a handful of times over the past few months, no sparkles have come off and been lost, and the tights haven't gotten a single run. I am 5'5 and about 108 lbs and got the XX-Small-Medium, which is a perfect fit with room to grow. I'm not a fan of high waisted bottoms, so I just fold the waistband down around my hips and they stay there comfortably. I've worn them with shorts and skirts, and even by themselves (with a top) to teach my belly dance fitness classes, and I receive tons of compliments and exclamations of "Where did you get those??!?!" every time I wear them. For the quality, the price is more than fair and treated with care these tights should last a good, long time!

Perfect Length! I have a 35" inseam. I wear 12/14 jeans. I ordered the XXL so they'd fit loosely, but an XL would've worked as well. The fabric is comfy for a full workday. I love that they reach the soles of my feet, so whether I wear flats at work or stilletoes afterwards the length is perfect! The seperate sash is cute, too! And the belt loops can hold a wide belt if I want to jazz it up!

Nice Stretchy Wide-leg Pants Great little pants. I’m 5’6” and weigh 165, large fit great BUT I have long legs and wish I selected the longer pants. Having said that, the large are 31” inseam and come all the way down to your shoes. These pull up, zipper/buttonless jeans have decent pockets. The material feels good and is stretchy—doesn’t seem like denim. They are comfy. These will look good with office shoes or a bit dressier with heels. I have extra wide hips, please see attached pictures to see how this looks. When I ordered these, they were $42. Today there’s a coupon on it, making it an okay value.

Size 10, white I love these chinos. They’re cut really well and very cute. I got them at a great price. I usually am a size 6 in stretchy jeans and 10 with no stretch/100% cotton, but in the Amazon essentials chino shorts I’m a 10 so I ordered these in 10 but these are definitely cut roomier than the shorts. Still, they don’t fall down at all throughout the day and my only other white pants (jeans) are butt tight. These as with the shorts have a little stretch. I showed close up of how long they fall on me: 5’9, long legs. I wore black underwear with them and although you could tell a little my husband said you couldn’t really unless you were staring. Don’t do what I did and cut the tag out carelessly! I cut a hole right in the fabric (not on seam!) and had to sew it. It is tiny and no one would notice but I mean I do. The one criticism I could maybe have for Amazon brand chinos (these and shorts) are that the button seems to be sewn in flimsily. I’d get these if went on sale in another color, but would probably get an 8. I’ll dry these in dryer since they’re big and come back to update if the fit changes drastically. They’re very comfy and I even took a long, hot walk in them. They don’t get too wrinkled (I didn’t steam or iron these I just wore them out of the package; I know they’re not perfect but good enough for the day I wore them!). Edit: I tumble dried these on low heat and they shrunk a TINY bit, just enough that the size 10 is now perfect. Not too short or too tight after the dryer. As long as I hung them up immediately, no ironing needed.

Love!!!!🤎 Worth every penny ! I am a bit thicker on the bottom weight around 140-145 height is 5’1 a bit long yes but nothing crazy so a large fit just perfect not tight and not huge perfectly comfortable

Super comfy and warm! These sweats are exactly as described. The material is a slick feel on the outside that I am not familiar with but they are definitely fleeced lined on he inside giving that warm feeling. I wore them with an oversized sweatshirt and sneakers and even though I was in a baggy look; I felt super cute and comfortable. My inseam is a 33. For reference. I am in the process of losing prednisone weight but still deal with an arthritic inflammatory disease. I’m 5’8 and 188#;. Longer legs with a shorter torso, a perfect fit is large or size US 12. Weight is mostly in tummy area now; 13# left to go. I still go one size up for extra looseness in clothing but it’s almost too big now to do so. Shoe size is about a 7 wide when inflamed. 7 medium when normal. US shoe size 7-8 is about normal depending on shoe. FYI: online purchases I try to keep rating simple. If I was in a local store and could hold and view it; 5 star would buy and take it home, one star would put it back on shelf and pick something else.#OnlyJesusSaves.

A pass for petites My height of 5’1” dictates I purchase cropped pants in regular sizes. With these, the legs expectedly were long. I would have to trim off the bands to accommodate my inseam (29”).In the smallest size offered, an S, I am swimming in these joggers. Sure, I have the help of the elastic waistband and lace. But, no matter, my buttocks are sad in these. I meant, they sag.The fabric is good for our cooler seasons. Pockets are zipped and roomy. In summary, petite chicks, you may want to pass on these pants.

Excellent pants with some caveats I'm 5'8", about 120 - 125 lbs, but have a really short torso and the inseam of someone closer to 5'10". I first ordered these pants in size medium in white (pictured) and I loved them so much that I also ordered them in green. For reference, I'm wearing the pants pulled up to my natural waist and wearing 3" heels. I thought the medium fit great and looked ok on my butt, until I tried on the size small in green which looked way better on my butt than the medium, and better all-around. The problem with the green is that they are about 1.5" shorter than the white, despite the product page saying the inseam on small and medium are the same. The picture speaks for itself - clearly they are not the same length. Someone else made this same comment with a photo about the white vs. blue, only she ordered the exact same size and the white were a lot longer. I did not find them to be see-through at all as others have mentioned, but perhaps that's because I didn't buy a skin tight fit. I received many compliments on the white pair and will keep them, but unfortunately any other color seems to be too short, so the green pair is going back. Update: I ordered the blue in medium and they are as long as the white medium so it seems as if the color doesn’t matter, but the small is a lot shorter than the medium even though the product page says the inseam is the same.

Sized up for a looser fit I normally wear a size small but sized up to a medium to ensure that these had a baggy fit on me. They were perfect. For size reference I am 5’3, 140 lbs and my lower measurements are 27-36. Lengthwise they were great for my height and the little bit of hang that I like. These pants are linen material and very well made. They aren’t see through at all and have such a comfortable fit. I love them and would recommend them.

Soft, Warm, and Great Fit for Tall Sizes These fleece-lined yoga pants are some of the softest and most comfortable I’ve owned. The fleece lining makes them cozy without being too heavy, and they’re perfect for colder weather. I really appreciate the option to choose inseam length—at 5’9” with more room in the thigh and booty area, the XL 33” Tall fit me perfectly. They have a nice high rise that stays in place and a wide waistband that smooths without digging in. The back pocket is bigger than most yoga pants and can easily hold my phone and keys, which is super convenient. The straight-leg cut also makes them versatile enough for lounging, errands, or even casual wear outside the house. If I had to point out one thing, it’s that they’re definitely warm, so they might feel too heavy for warmer days—but for fall and winter, they’re perfect.

I wasn’t sure on the size, as I usually a size 29/30 in most jeans. I got a 28 and I’m glad I did. They are snug but also stretchy. I'm instantly nearly 3" taller without feeling like I'm going to fall! I have been so eager to receive these platform knee high boots. Just in time for the fall and winter weather! I'm not all that tall...I like to say I'm 5'5" but with age it appears I'm now 5'4" which poses a problem when I go to concerts which are always standing room and everyone is so tall I cannot see the stage. This elevates me nearly 3" and the whole platform is nearly 2" meaning the angle of tilt on my heels and the subsequent pain that shoots up my body is diminished, because the angle of the high heel part for me in only about 1.5". So I get to be taller without the pain of traditional high heels. I am usually a 7.5 but I think with age my feet spread out so I got a size 8 and it's plenty roomy. Once I put my orthotics in (gosh, I'm showing my age, aren't I?) these fit perfectly. I can easily go all day wearing these. I love the traditional boot style, clean, rounded toe box, goes all the way up to the knee, the zipper is high quality and even fits on over jeans. Bring on the colder weather and cooler fashion. And the concerts too!

Love them!! These are actually better than I was expecting. The fabric is pretty decent quality, and really smooth and soft to the touch. I am 5’3 and 175 lbs, and I got the XL with the 28” inseam. They fit really well, not too tight at all. There’s a slight tummy tuck quality, but it still doesn’t make as much of a difference for a tummy like mine. The length is perfect. I would say if you’re any taller than 5’4, then definitely get a longer inseam or you’ll be dealing with high-waters. The pockets are decently sized, they do bunch up a bit and look a little strange since the pants are skin tight, but they’re not too bad. I think these are great for the price, I’m definitely considering getting more colors…

Waist was way too big for a 0. Cute jeggings and affordable I'm 5'1" 106 pounds and went with the 0 short. For the most part these fit perfectly except for the waist. They ran really big in the waist for a size 0. The legs and hips were very tight but the waist had a huge gap. I would've deducted 2 stars but since these were so affordable I decided to only deduct 1 because for the price i can deal with the waist area. I just won't be able to wear cropped tops with these. The length was just right. These are a legging jean material a little stretchy and comfortable. The front pockets are fake but they do have back pockets. They seem durable and not see through. These look really cute with a sweater and high heel boots. Definitely recommend. I also got these in the black as well. The black was a little better in the waist but still too loose for a size 0. Looks exactly as advertised and love them.

So Comfortable! These viehunt Womens Straight Leg Stretchy Dress Work Pants really surprised me. They feel so comfortable, but don't look it at all. The fabric is thicker than leggings, and heavier, but it also feels almost buttery soft like some leggings. The pants have a lot of stretch to them, but the straight leg cut makes them hang in a way that doesn't look it. They are a thick, slick but not shiny material that I find often used for dresses and jumpers. They have a button and zipper, like any dress pant, but could absolutely be pulled on as well - that's how stretchy and forgiving they are. I am normally a 12-14 and I am 5'9". I ordered the XL because I was concerned about length - and I am glad that I did. They are just about the perfect length, though I can make them appear longer if I slide them down a bit. At the waist, that is comfortable, but the crotch does feel over low when I try to scoot them down to add length. Overall, with dress shoes, them being a titch too short won't matter or be obvious at all. These are definitely pants I will wear for stuffy settings - no one will ever be able to tell how comfortable they are!

Finally pants that fit me!!! I’m not that tall, I’m 5’7” but every time I buy sweat pants they shrink and always become 2”- 4” too short. These pants I purposely got them a bit longer (32 inches inseam) and after the wash and dry they became the perfect fit! I’m so glad to finally have pants that reach the floor. Very comfortable and not too thin or too thick. Will definitely get more pairs.

Innacurate size description I'm 40 years old, 5'2", and typically weigh between 109-111 lbs. My measurements are 34B bra, 27-inch waist, 35.5-inch hips, 11-inch biceps, 21-inch arm length, short torso, and a 29-inch inseam pants. Ordered size 2 short. This is definitely not 29inseam. It’s more like 27 inseam which I don’t like specially for this type of pants. This pant were too big for me and too short. I would have easily fit in a size 0 regular snd even tho that one might be too big also due to the stretch however there is not size 0 available. Not my favorite in general.

Love these The fit is perfect! I ordered an XS at 5’1 & 118lbs and was very pleased. They’re just a little long but otherwise they fit even better than I hoped! Stretchy, soft & movable. Exactly what I need. Definitely buying more but maybe in XS Petite for shorter pant legs.

too long, (way) too wide at the hips What I love about these jeans is that they truly are high-rise, straight-legged, and fit me well around my waist. I'm 5'4" and weigh around 176, so I got a women's 20 petite. What I dislike is that these seem to be made for a height of someone who is around 5'6". In addition, though perhaps it's hard to see here, the hips are way too large, and I doubt going down a size would make much difference. While I understand that these are to be relaxed fit, these are more like no-fit. Nothing like the photo in any case. Having said that, I know Lee is a good brand, it's hard to find black, high-rise jeans so I'm having them altered by having them taken up a good inch or so and having them taken in at a cost of around $35.00 (which really isn't too bad).

Not a WORK PANT These are basically a painter's chino trouser. The material is a soft cotton/stretch blend, comfy but hardly seems 'durable.' I was surprised by the fit - I ordered a 2 Short, I'm 5'4", 115lbs. They were slightly loose, sitting below my true 27" waist at 31" on hips. I like the color and am hoping a hot wash/dry will shrink them some even if they stretch again during wear (like a good pair of cotton denim jeans) but not fade. Was looking at the new Pierce model in-store, but received a 2023 version of this.

BEST HIKING PANTS EVER THESE PANTA ARE INCREDIBLE!!! I’m a backpacking guide in Utah and I ordered these pants with low hopes that they would fit right because I have a 34 inch inseam which is nearly impossible to find in women’s clothing. Not only are these ample long enough but they are so flattering and do not restrict range of motion AT ALL. I’m 5’8 about 130 lbs and I got a medium and they fit perfect. These blow all the expensive brands out of the water, such good quality!!!

Runs GARGANTUAN! Should've read the reviews first because these pants are huge!!!! I ordered a small but they felt like they were twice the size. They are also tall, so if you're say 5'7 and under you might be safer ordering petite or just stick with rolling the pants leg up.

Comfy & cute Was looking for jeans that would be comfy for traveling & these are great. The elastic waist and slightly stretchy fabric made them comfy even on the plane and for a long car ride. I gave them 5 stars because they seem well made with a nice weight denim & fit according to the size chart. They did not shrink noticeably after washing according to directions & hanging to dry. My only criticisms are the front pockets are on the smaller side so I have to put my phone in the back pocket. Also, although they did fit me, if you have a larger backside you may need to size up. I'm 5'7/170cm tall and generally wear a US size 16 in jeans, and I bought these in size XL.

Great for mid-size short, curvy girls Super happy with these pants! I wore them on a recent trip to Europe in VERY hot weather. They are lightweight, stain resistant and flattering. I am 5'3.5" and 158lbs and normally a size 12 curvy. Size large short was perfect in BEIGE (the off-white was a bit see through). They have washed nicely, and I hang to dry. Will be ordering more in other colors because they are so comfy.

Stretchy but not as flattering as hoped. Material is on the thinner side but still very soft and stretchy. Compared to my Halara pants, the fabric is thinner and more stretchy. The Halara version holds its shape better in the legs. These are a little more drapey and looks more baggy. There wasn't a sizing chart so I ordered my normal size and it fits just fine (runs larger than Halara if that helps). The 29" inseam did wear a little longer than I'm used to. I normally can wear 29" inseams with flats but this is a little too long and would drag on the floor a bit. I would wear this with a slight heel to keep the pants from touching the floor. The pleats in the front are not the most flattering. If you don't have a flat belly, the pleats will make your pooch more accentuated. It's not horrible but it doesn't lie flat as it does in the photos on the models. The cut and style of the pants look nice enough to be slacks but the fabric kinda gives it away as loungewear. The black does hide it pretty well and I could get away with wearing into the office with the right blouse but if you are trying to dress to impress, don't wear this to a business function.

The best pants I love these pants, I wear them all the time. They’re great for wearing around the house or dressing up and going out. I got my first pair in white from Marshall’s, then I got 2 more pairs from Amazon in black and navy. Out of al the linen pants I own, these are the most comfortable and easiest to care for. They’re a little too long when I first get them, but once I washed and dried them, they slightly shrank, which is perfect for me since I’m 5’3”. If you’re taller and it fits great, maybe air dry and iron .

Tall Girl Win 20/10 love the fit, feel and length! I am 6 ft tall, 160-165 lbs, athletic build. These fit AH-MAZING. I am FLOORED. I ordered a Large with the 36’ inseam. Praise these pants for TOUCHING MY FEET! It’s a small miracle. I’ve been nothing but legs my whole life and this is soooo satisfying. They are light but not cheap feeling, comfortable, breathable. Very affordable. I’m obsessed with the pockets, I’m going to need someone to bring back pockets to women’s clothing, because.. WOW! I could see this for lots of outdoor adventures, I bought mine for fishing (lighter khaki color). But I could totally see how these would be super great to hike in. I may be investing in quite a few more pairs! Totally recommend!

Gorgeous! These are beautiful quality brown faux suede boots with a lot of design detail. They look a lot more expensive than what they really are. Even though I am 5’8” these boots reach to a little over the bottom of my knees. They have an extra tall shaft so if you are a short person just know they have an 18” shaft so for me 18” reaches a little over the botyom of my knee and I have long legs because I am 5’8”. Now as much as I like these fabulous chocolate brown boots, I have a wide foot and they are too snug. So if you have a boxy wide foot like me these are not going to be comfortable.

Look and Feel Great These are wonderful pants for summer. They are lightweight but no see through and the khaki color is perfect. They are true to size - small for 5;4”, 120 pounds. They would go great with a linen blouse or just a nice tee top. I washed them and they look as good as when I took them out of the package. They’re exactly what I wanted.

Order a size down/ they are very thin. The pants fit perfectly but they are extremely thin. Which doesn’t bother me, but might bother you. ( I took a photo holding them up to the light to show how thin.) I’m 5’7 153 lbs and I ordered a small tall.

Comfortable and Reliable Bibs for Women I bought these 2 sizes up, because the XL was on closeout (for reference, I'm 5'4 and about 180 lbs. I am wider at the hips and smaller at the waist, more pear shaped). I knew I'd have some alterations to do, but it ended up being as simple as sewing on four buttons 1.5 inches in on each side. For the price and minimal effort of taking it a few sizes back down, I'm very happy with these. The value for money can't be matched. Mobility is good, the zipper on the big pocket works nice and the smaller pockets at the front make for convenient and secure storage. These bibs are unlined, which will work great for offroading or any general outdoor activity on cold mornings or evenings. The thickness of the material is comfortable enough that wearing a pair of leggings and a hoodie underneath will allow for different comfort levels. On the photos, you can see where the first 3 are the fitted version, after the buttons have been sewn on. The other 3 are of the original product, wearing it with the buttons that come on the bibs.

Very boxy…but very very soft I got the clear blue sky in a size small, and I am 5’4 and 125 lbs. Let me say that the fit is nothing like the pictures. It’s boxy. You can see in one of the photos I took how much the fabric stands away from my body. From the pictures on the model I judged it to be a more close-fitting cut, and longer. The neckline is also wider than it looks in the pictures, and the sleeves are a bit loose and long. That said, the color is spot on, and it is so super duper soft. Supima cotton for the win. I’ve washed and tumble dried it already, and no shrinkage (or very slight). I recommend sizing down. I’m only keeping it because it’s a hassle for my dad to do returns.

Cute! Shrank but still fits. I am 5’9” and weigh 190 lb. I typically wear a US size 12-14 and ordered an XL. The photos were taken before they were washed, and one leg was slightly shorter than the other, maybe 1/2-1”. The first time I washed them I only let them dry about 15 minutes on low temp then hung them up to finish drying. They needed to be ironed afterward and did not shrink. The second time I washed them I forgot about them, and they went through a full dry cycle on low temp. They came out wrinkle free but shrunk 2-3” in length. They also shrank in width but still fit. They wear like hot pants from the 60s. The material is a stretchy twill and I’ve received numerous compliments on them. They also hold their shape well during wear. My only caveat is that it has no pockets. I’d love a hidden pocket in the back. Overall, I think these are a good value for the money and will order more.

Really warm, unique In the item description it mentions a 2-in platform, there is no platform hidden in these boots, they actually feel like slippers and there's just a small bit of cushioning between you and the ground.(The sole measures 1 in) I'm actually very happy for this because I did not want a platform but I took a chance on them anyway because they were so unique looking. When I open the package they did have a unpleasant odor, it wasn't overpowering but I'm also not sensitive to these things. The odor dissipated after I left them out of the packaging for a couple of days. I have thicker calves (16.5 in) And I am normally a US size 8, I ordered these in an 8.5 and I'm glad I did because they are perfectly snug on my foot with a pair of thin socks and I'm afraid the eight might have been too tight. I did have to struggle to get them up my thick calf but once they got there they were actually comfortable and didn't restrict, but I would not suggest this for anyone with calves thicker than mine. Also they are very warm, but I imagine they would be terrible in rain or snow since they are just a faux fur fabric. The soul is a brown rubber with treads. To give you an idea of height I am 5'3 And these came up to right under my knees.

Buttery soft & sooo comfy, but boho Sooo I am extremely torn over these leggings. The color is absolutely perfect, matches the photo exactly and is just what i wanted! Not to mention, the material of these leggings are next level. Soooo buttery soft, stretchy and comfortable. I would say they are on the thinner side but not see through from what I could tell. I bought the beige/khaki color in size S/M. So the only actual issue I have with these is that, in my opinion, they do run bigger in size. Mind you, I am pretty petite (5’3, 100lbs; basically no butt sadly) .. and i consistently have trouble finding pants that fit me perfectly. I would honestly love if they had an XS/S sizing option. These were just slightly baggy in the waistband area and upper thigh/hip. I was able to roll down the waistband which did help make them appear less loose and more form fitting, so with that being a possible way to wear these (and the possibility/hope they may shrink after washing & drying in high temps) I am conflicted on returning these or not. I mean these truly are the softest leggings I’ve ever felt and the pricing was great. Curious if anyone else felt these ran bigger than expected?

Petite Small vs. Petite Medium I found the sizing chart to be somewhat confusing, so I have notes on both Petitie Small and Petite Medium! I posted a picture of me wearing the Petite Small and the other picture is me in the Petite Medium. It's difficult to tell by the photo but the Medium is looser. Petite Small (actual pants measurements): Waist 30" circumference Hips 38.5" Calf 13.5 Hip to crotch: 11.5" approx Petite Medium (actual pants measurement): Waist 31" Hips: 40" Calf: 16" Me: waist 28" Hips 35" Calf 13" The small fits on me, but snug in the calf. The medium also fits. The waist band is thick 2.5" but does have stretch. The actual material of the pants does not have stretch. Overall, the medium is looser like sweat pants but looks fine with a fitted top! I wear both the small and medium! A lot of my coworkers wear these in a tighter fit especially if they are pear-shaped and these pants look like leggings on them. :)

NOT SHORT GIRL FRIENDLY!!!!!!!!! NOT SHORT GIRL FRIENDLY. I was to start this review with saying do not order if you’re a short girl. I, thankfully, know how to sew and was able to hem the pants myself. For the average shirt girl, I would strongly avoid, unless you can sew or want to pay a tailor to alter them. I am 5’2 and had to cut off five inches from the bottom. You can see in my photo that I have it pinned in one, I was measuring for the hem. The length look ridiculous, honestly it looked like I was wearing scuba fins with how much fabric there was at the bottom. To GTP: either shorten the length to adjust to the average woman’s height (5’3-5’5) or provide a petite option because it truly was ridiculous. The pros are that they are stretchy and indeed comfortable. The quality is as expected from TGP, as always it’s not great but it’s not bad. They look cute, but they don’t look like real jeans either.

Surprisingly Petite Approved! I’m petite but these work for me! I’ve been on the hunt for low rise baggy/wide leg jeans that are also fitted on the glutes and I’m very happy with these! For reference I’m 5’0 ~110-112lbs, waist is 25” and hip measurement is 37”. I’m usually a 0-00 depending on the brand. In Abercrombie I’m a 24 short in the Curve Love line. My inseam is 27” and these are definitely on the longer side for me but they suit the baggy look and will pair really well with chunky sneakers, heels, heeled boots etc. The material is thick and stretchy and they are extremely comfortable. Quality is similar to other premium brands - I’d say fabric thickness and stretch is on par with Good American. I was sent the dark first by mistake but liked them so I just kept them and went back for the light. Both are great but the light is just a tad bit more flattering :)

Tighter Than Expected, Overall Nice Fit and Fabric I'm 5'4" and 38"x33"x39". According to the size chart the Size 12 would fit best at my waist, but would have been baggy at the hips. I decided to purchase a 10 and a 12 to make sure of the fit. The 12 was obviously tight looking while still in the package, so I tried them on first—never even opened the size 10. The 12 fit very tight across the hips and thighs, but are not tight around my waist. Waistband rolls when I sit, but I don't tuck my shirts in as I did for the photo. Thick fabric for a stretch denim. Regular inseam length hits right above the back of my foot. Front pockets are very shallow, 2" deep. Followup note: These pants are difficult to pull all the way up. I have to grab the waistband and hold it while I bend over to pull them upward up to 3 times. Once up they stay pretty well, but I wish the waistband was a little tighter. Basically, every slim-cut stretchy denim pant I have tried has this same tight hips and loose waist fit problem.

Perfectly loose, comfy, and colorful I have these pants in 4 different designs. The pair I’m wearing in these pictures are my favorites, but they all are very cute and colorful. I had ankle reconstruction surgery and wanted pants that were comfortable, soft, cute, easy on and easy off, and would allow easy access to my ankle and foot during PT. They also had to be long enough and loose enough to cover my shoes…I have to wear 2 different shoes because my surgical foot and ankle is so swollen I had to buy a tennis shoe 2 sizes larger and wider than I normally wear. These check all the boxes. They are perfect. I will wear them even after I heal. I’ve worn them to PT, dinner, church, and during a day out with my husband. I’m getting my moneys worth from these pants. Wash on cold, gentle and hang to dry. No ironing needed. They will pack and travel well. I did return 1 pair because the material seemed different, almost plastic like. But the other 4 I have are great. 5’4” tall. 132 lbs. Size M, which fit me a bit loose. My others are a size S and the fit is a little snugger, but still very comfy. There is some stretch and no shrinkage. The tank top is ANA from JC Penney.

These check all the boxes. They are perfect. I will wear them even after I heal. I’ve worn them to PT, dinner, church, and during a day out with my husband. I’m getting my moneys worth from these pants. Wash on cold, gentle and hang to dry. No ironing needed. They will pack and travel well. I did return 1 pair because the material seemed different, almost plastic like. But the other 4 I have are great. 5’4” tall. 132 lbs. Size M, which fit me a bit loose. My others are a size S and the fit is a little snugger, but still very comfy. There is some stretch and no shrinkage. The tank top is ANA from JC Penney.

Comfortable and Cute Pants Comfortable pants. I love the way they feel on. The fabric feels soft, stretchy and breathable. I love how they look which is very flattering on my booty. The front and back pockets are usable. I chose my size based on the size chart. The pants are a little tight around the waist which I knew because my waist is 33" my weigh156 lbs. The size chart for L waist is 30 to 32. They are not uncomfortable because the pants have stretch. I have washed and dried these pants several times. No shrinkage at all but they do wrinkle if they are not hung up immediately. I wear these pants to the office and then to the gym.

Soft but can be too short for some These pants, as others have mentioned, have a wonderful yoga pants-like texture, with LOTS of stretch all over (both in the waistband and in the legs). That being said, as others have mentioned, they are in fact not tall girl friendly and will run too short if you’re anything above 5’6”. If I keep them where it’s comfortable on my waist, they are a tad bit short but I am able to style it well overall. They were able to be steamed with no issue and had minor fraying that did not impact the integrity of the pants. For me, they’re a great compromise because I expected a much rougher texture than what they actually are, and are incredibly comfortable to wear. They look great but if you are self conscious about your stomach, you may want to pair with some shapewear since there’s no compression or shaping in these pants.

Excellent quality joggers These Sevego tall inseam joggers are really nice. I'm 5'11" so it's hard to find long joggers and the 33" inseam is perfect. They fit true to size with a fitted look. The joggers have a somewhat shiny smooth athletic outside and a soft short fuzz on the inside. They are stretchy, comfortable, warm and flattering. They have a nice weight to them and a light/medium thickness. Great fitting and quality joggers for taller people.

Comfy but inseam is short Why did you pick this product vs others?: I wanted full panel "over the belly" jeans because the other pair I have no longer fits me properly. So far these have been fine, my only problem is they aren't quite long enough. I am 5'8" and normally wear a size 4 when not pregnant and the medium fits well but the inseam is a couple inches too short! It isnt too noticeable while standing, but while sitting it is obvious and it looks terrible. Maybe an option to select a different length as well as overall size would be nice. Other than that, these are comfortable and look fine. A little boxy due to the "straight leg" with no taper and no flare, but I'm ok with that. I have not had any problems with these slipping down, the panel rolling down, or anything. It all stays in place which is perfect for me! Just keep in mind the inseam length if you have longer legs!

Well made, very high heel, smaller calf, possible pinch point I’ve obtained several pairs of boots for the winter. These run smaller than others, and are smaller through the calf as well. They do still fit but may cause issues if worn for long periods. I also noticed that the edge of the top of the boot comes rich at the bend in my knee. I noticed that as soon as I sat down with them on. I would prefer not having that hit at that spot and worry that it will rub or pinch. I’m not sure but that may just be an issue for someone at my same height (5’2’) or shorter. They look very nice and have the look that many like with the larger than usual soles and the sole pattern. These are very high. They feel good - soft and smooth with nice lining. I’m not sure I would pay this much for another pair considering the smaller calf and height at the knee bend.

Super cute sexy and so very flattering. My 2nd pair. 30 inch inseam was just right. I'm 5'1" 114 pounds and went with my normal size of small which was true to size and a perfect fit. This is actually my 2nd time purchasing these pants. The first time I went with a 28 inch inseam and they were the right length but due to the tight fit it caused the legs to raise just a little and it wasn't exactly where I wanted them to be. This time I went with the 30 inch inseam and they were absolutely perfect. These are made of a good quality stretchy soft comfortable polyester material. They are tight fitted but have plenty of stretch. The buttocks has a scrunched fitted design to help shape and make your butt more flattering and sexy which it definitely does. I've had several women ask me where I bought these. I use these for work so I do wish they had front pockets. That's the only downside to these pants. They do have two back pockets which can be buttoned up to keep your items, especially cash or your debit card, from falling out. My phone is too big to button up but the pocket still holds my phone. I use the other back pocket for my keys and vape. This is where the front pocket would come in handy because I also have to carry an ink pen with me and when I wear these I have to clip my pen to the neck of my shirt which constantly pokes me in the face and my makeup gets all over it from rubbing my neck. If these had even a simple small front pocket I could use it for my ink pen. Aside from that these are absolutely perfect and I love them even more now that I have a longer length. They are super flattering to the legs thighs and butt and very sexy. Highly recommend. I do wish these were a little cheaper though. It gets expensive trying to buy enough for the work week at $30 a pair.

Follow the size guide. Not reviews. If I followed the size guide I would need a XXXL because I typically wear a size 16-18. true chocolate brown, roomy, stays up, and comfortable! For reference, these are my true measurements: bust - 38” waist - 31.5” hips 46”, and I’m 5’6 around 185. I hold a good amount of weight in my hips/glutes, and my stomach is on the natueal flatter end, with a natural lower belly protruding. I purchased a Large! PROS: I really like the pants and truly underestimated them! For someone with a hourglass shape/bigger waist to hip ratio, I was looking for wide trousers that had room around my leg but cinched at the waist, and these pants do! It has elastic in the back and a button/zipper in the front, with belt loops all around. With buttoning the pants, the elastic in the back allowed for a secure & tight, yet still comfortable fit around my waist—with and w/o shirt tucking. I am closer to 5’6.5 and a half than 5’6, with my torso & leg being of equal length. I ordered the 31” inseam pants, and wore them with a pair of 1/1.5 inch platform loafers; they are the perfect long length without dragging on the floor. CON: the only issue I had is the loose pouch that I got around my crotch area (see pic). Pretty typical for my body type with any loose-fitting pants, just because of its tailoring for straighter figures. Unless I pose a certain way, it is slightly apparent when walking. But doesnt bother me too much—as long as you wear an appropriate shirt that compliments the bagginess, it won’t pull in as much attention. I will be ordering in different colors!

👠 Easy slip on Design, Flexible Soft Fabric and Petite Inclusive Makes These A WINNER ! For Reference I am : 168lbs , 5’4” Size Large I'll be the first to admit, my initial reaction to these pants was skepticism. As I unboxed them, I thought to myself, "These are going to be a disappointment." The fabric, while incredibly soft to the touch, has a clingy quality to it that I wasn't sure I'd be comfortable with. But, as I decided to give them a fair shot, I slipped them on and immediately Changed me mind about them. Moving around in the clingy fabric, which I had initially seen as a drawback, actually is quite flexible and comfy. I received these in my normal size Large in the color Dark Gray. The fantastic part of all this is that they actually come in a petite length which is so wonderful for a person like me who is on the shorter side. The fact that I don’t have to worry about my pants dragging on the ground or having to pay to have them hemmed is FANTASTIC ! The petite length is perfectly proportioned and hits me at my ankle. As per my measurements the inseam is XXX” and not to mention that when I am wearing pants that are the right length, I instantly feel I look more put together and polished. I don't have to worry about tripping over my own hem, or not being able to show my shoes. Another feature that really stands out to me is the waistband. It's like a little piece of heaven no annoying, tight elastic that digs into my skin, just a smooth, flat design that stays put all day. I've had issues with waistbands rolling down in the past, but that's not a problem here. The only downside I've noticed is that the pockets could be a bit roomier. They're not tiny or anything, but if you've got big hands or like to carry a lot of stuff with you, you might find them a bit cramped. Also, because the fabric is on the thinner side, when I put my hands in my pockets, it can look a bit awkward - my hands kind of bulge out and it's not the most flattering look (check out the picture I've included to see what I mean). Despite those minor quibbles, I'm really enjoying these pants. The comfort level is top-notch. In closing, I have to say that these pants might not be everyone's cup of tea, and I'm still on the fence about whether they're really my style. However, The softness of the fabric is unparalleled, Whether I'm running errands, lounging around the house, or just need something to throw on to walk my pooch these ft the bill. I think they look best with sneakers or sandals, and can easily be dressed up or down depending on the occasion. They are being offered in 10 colors , in tall, petite and regular lengths and 6 sizes. They are machine washable And dryer friendly.

8 1/2 shoe has 16” shaft height & not a good fit for short thick legs. I ordered these in black and gray in size 8.5 regular. They are nice boots, true to size as far as the shoe part. The heel is a nice height and the material is soft & leather like. The shaft height on the item description says 14.56 inches but does not include the shoe size with that height. When I measure both pairs, the shaft height is closer to 16 inches on an 8.5 size shoe. I’m a 5’2” with thick legs & very muscular calves which always cause an issue with boots and shaft size. The design of the shaft in this boot is curved. Since my legs are short, my calf muscle is at the very bottom part of this curve where they just begin to widen, resulting in a tight calf fit, while the top of the shaft is big and gaps a bit. The shaft WIDTH at the top is 16” (8” X 2 laid flat in the pics). Where the biggest part of my calf starts is 6 inches below the top, where it is only14 inches (7” X 2) so it’s really tight there, and hard to zip until you get to the part that’s wider. So if I switch to wide calf they will really be gaping and sloppy at the top. The gray was too tight to be comfortable, but the black was a little bigger (but snug) & I could zip it. The top of both pairs hit just below my knee cap. A 14 -15 inch shaft fits much better and is more flattering, but good luck finding a pair that height in an 8 1/2 shoe. They need to change that shaft height in the description, that’s the reason I ordered these. I’m returning the gray and on the fence with the black. I wish I could find a 14 inch shaft height.

When I measure both pairs, the shaft height is closer to 16 inches on an 8.5 size shoe. I’m a 5’2” with thick legs & very muscular calves which always cause an issue with boots and shaft size. The design of the shaft in this boot is curved. Since my legs are short, my calf muscle is at the very bottom part of this curve where they just begin to widen, resulting in a tight calf fit, while the top of the shaft is big and gaps a bit. The shaft WIDTH at the top is 16” (8” X 2 laid flat in the pics). Where the biggest part of my calf starts is 6 inches below the top, where it is only14 inches (7” X 2) so it’s really tight there, and hard to zip until you get to the part that’s wider. So if I switch to wide calf they will really be gaping and sloppy at the top. The gray was too tight to be comfortable, but the black was a little bigger (but snug) & I could zip it. The top of both pairs hit just below my knee cap. A 14 -15 inch shaft fits much better and is more flattering, but good luck finding a pair that height in an 8 1/2 shoe. They need to change that shaft height in the description, that’s the reason I ordered these. I’m returning the gray and on the fence with the black. I wish I could find a 14 inch shaft height.
