Pants in a pinch I purchased the lite khaki and grey. They are okay in terms of fit. I’m 5’2” at 130lbs and bought size small. You’ll need blouses that cover the camel-toe and undies lines. They feel super smooth and the thick band holds the tummy a bit. Black undies will show through the lite khaki but the grey seem to mask it pretty well. If you need pants you can just throw on, then these are good for the price. The camel-toe, somewhat sheer, and show of lines is what bugs me about them.
The fit was good, but the length is a little long for them being "short". Size 16 short, 5'3"

Flattering and also wrinkly I got a medium size as someone who is 5'5, around 28 in around the waist. When I got the pants, it was wrinkly. The material felt cheap as it's 100% polyester, I have not yet washed it, but I feel it will also be wrinkly. I love the waist, and it is super cute when I tie it near the waist. The length of the pants fits me just right, and the fabric doesn't feel see-through.

Perfect fit! These Under Armour ankle leggings are fantastic! They’re true to size and have a great fit for shorter folks around 5’2”, hitting at the perfect ankle length without bunching. The fabric is soft, stretchy, and comfortable for workouts or casual wear. I also love the deep pockets—perfect for holding my phone, keys, or cards while on the go. Overall, they’re stylish, functional, and flattering. Definitely a must-have for anyone looking for quality leggings that fit well and feel amazing!

Just what I’ve been looking for. As a curvy person who works in the animal medical industry, I have somewhat struggled to find a good bottom for work. Of course I want to be comfortable, but also require a quality material. I have tried scrubs in the pants, and I’m sure there are good scrub bottoms out there but when you try the Greys Anatomy brand and have them get easily damaged in the thigh area, you give up. (Especially for the price) To make a long story short, these pants seem to be what I’ve searching for. (I haven’t had them for very long but so far so great!) they are very comfortable and feel a bit more professional than pant jogger sweatpants. They’re also a bit fashionable, if you pair them with a nice sneaker. I am 5’5 and a size 16, XL shirt. I don’t agree that they are a bit long, but I typically wear high waisted items and if you wear the pants up high, it’s not an issue. (And keep them tied) After I’ve wear them more and the don’t fail me, I will order more. I’m leaving this review so early to hopefully help out someone that is searching for pants like myself.

Great fit and comfortable set! I’m 5’7 and weigh 140. I bought a size large-tall and it’s perfect!! I love the feel and comfort of this outfit. I used it for travel. I just ordered a different color because I love it so much! I’m so glad it comes in tall size and is long enough for me!

Great pants These are great comfortable hiking pants. They are high waisted. I cinched the waist a bit with the drawstring. Since they are thin I I’ll also wear them for lounging. I am 5’ 7” and 130 lbs. the size M 30” length are perfect. I’m going to order the black too.

Classy casual pant Bought these pants because the reviews were so good. So adding about great review of my own! Love these pants!!! They are so comfy and perfect for my job. I feel a little dressed up while being casual at the same time. The waistband is a little snug fitting but I didn’t mind the fit while wearing. If you’re someone who likes a looser fit in the waist maybe size up. I am 5’4” and usually wear a 2/4. These fit great. The length of the pant is exactly what I like and the fun barrel fit looks great if that’s the look you’re going for! Plus the material of the pant is not too thin so I feel covered.
Bought for a cruise Look pretty good, a little large and long. I'm 5'4", 126 lbs and got a small. Should be great on our cruise though.

I rarely give only 3 stars, but these "jeans" were a disappointment for me. First the material is not denim. It looks like it, but it definitely has a polyester feel. I love jeans with stretch, but these are more like stretch pants made to look like jeans. And even though I ordered the longer length - and I am only 5'6" - these pants feel short to me, like they hit just below my ankle bone, which would be okay if they were "skinny jeans", but having the wide legs, it looks like I am wearing the wrong size of pants. The wide waist band is nice, but the drop from the top of the band to the crotch area is too big for a medium so as I wear them it gets are crunched up and though they are not tight, I get that "smile" look in the front. I'm just not a fan.

Cute Lace Pantyhose Looks just like the seller's photos. I like the floral pattern of the lace. The price was good and they have stretch but they are lace so they are basically like a netting. I've seen some styles that have the lace pattern on top of a traditional pantyhose, this is not that. It is all lace and no other feature. There isn't any compression either, it's lace pantyhose and then a half inch waistband at the top. The waist came up to my natural waist but I am short. This may not be long enough for anyone over 5' 5". I've only worn these twice and have been gentle with them. I don't expect them to last longer than a season b/c they are all lace with no reinforcement anywhere. Next time I might look for a similar pair but that have a traditional nylon with the lace for durability and a reinforced toe (this pair has no reinforced toe).

These are not like the cheap ones I have. Very well made and not thin. I love the fabric these are made of. Heavy and cozy. I need to shorten them as they are really long and I am 5'8". The picture shows I need to shorten about 5" overall.

Very comfortable, fit great! At 5'5",183 lbs I am currently in a size 14 and these definitely fit true to size. I have more of a straight up and down figure, but they were not cutting in on my tummy. I do have it tiny bit of a muffin top, but not so much that I felt like I needed to go a size up. These pants are super comfortable. They are thick like jeans, but stretchy like leggings. I intentionally showed them with one boot in and one out so you can see that even though I would consider them a skinny jean cut, the pant leg has enough room to wear over the top of my ankle boots. They are also cut small enough, they could be worn tucked inside boots. If you wanted to wear them with taller boots, they would be nice and comfy because they would not be bulky. Overall, I gave these mid-rise jeans five stars because they are not only comfortable, but they fit nicely and are versatile. You can dress them up or dress them down according to your own style. You should definitely give them a try!

Glad they're not actually linen These linen-like pants are my new go-to for comfort. I ordered up and am glad I did. I usually am at the top end of a small, but didn't want the pants to be too tight, so I ordered a Medium. They fit great. They hang to the perfect length, have some flow in the legs, and don't hug my seat too much. At 5'5" and 140lbs (roughly), they're a great fit and a great buy! I love the green. I'm so glad the material is not actually linen. They do look like linen, but are not 100% linen, which is great for washability. I have not tried them in the dryer yet, so I don't know if they will shrink. The wrinkles do eventually hang out, and the price is exceptional for excellent quality!

My favorite scrubs - petite and has many pockets. These are my favorites!! Very comfortable and breathable. Quite utilitarian. Fit is good, although I have athletic thighs so I might want to size up next time to a petite large for a little extra breathing room. I am 5’2 and 145 lbs, and very grateful for this shorter length! It is very hard to find petite scrub pants with these many pockets. I hope they never change this cut, fabric, size and style!

Run really big but still look cute on I'm 5'1" 110 pounds and went with my normal size of small which ran really big on me. I wish they offered an xsmall. These are wide legged and meant to run bigger but these are really big to the point I could barely tie them tight enough to stay up. Luckily they still look cute but I have to roll them one time. These are made of good quality comfortable material. The length is longer on me because I'm so short. I have these high waisted. Aside from the size these are as advertised. I'm not sure how they fit the model so well and still look slim considering I'm smaller than the model. I wouldn't size up. Some may even need to size down if a smaller size is available. I do recommend these even though they run big because they still look good.

The reason for a missing star is that they do create a bit of "camel". I tend to wear a longer top that covers that area anyway. But maybe the front seam could be done a bit differently

Straight leg jeans look, pull on comfort Can't even describe how much I love these jeans. They look especially baggy in this picture, but in reality from the front they are surprisingly slimming at the waist and very very comfortable. The fabric is soft and stretchy, but the button at the top makes them look like they're regular jeans. I'd take a pic of the front to add to this one, but the fact is they are perpetually in the wash because I wear them constantly. As a taller woman (5'8") I especially appreciate the 31" inseam, and the dark wash makes them conservative enough that I can get away with wearing them to the office with a nice blouse or sweater. Big picture: They look like nice jeans but feel like sweatpants. Get them.

A great, practical boot for daily us I'm writing this based on my wife's experience with these boots. She has found them to be a practical option for daily wear. The main positive she reports is comfort. She has worn them for full days at a time without any issues or breaking-in period. The flat heel and round toe seem to work well for her. They have also held up in wet conditions. She wore them in the rain on one occasion and mentioned that her feet stayed dry and the boots showed no immediate damage. For sizing reference, she is 5'2". The shaft of the boot hits her right below the knee, which was the fit she was looking for. One thing to note is the tread. It's fine for paved surfaces like sidewalks and floors, but she felt it wouldn't provide enough grip for uneven or rugged terrain. Overall, based on her use, these are a solid and functional boot for general daily and urban use.

Lightweight, Comfy, and Long Enough For Me I’m 5'9" with a 27" waist and 37" hips, and I ordered a small (usual size) and the Tall inseam. Honestly, I’m relieved it worked — but just barely. These fit me perfectly at the ankle, but if I were even half an inch taller, we’d be entering “accidental cropped pant” territory. Length aside, these pants are fantastic. The fabric is lightweight, stretchy, and breathable, and they somehow manage to feel like lounge pants while looking polished enough for errands, travel, or even a casual round of golf. The zip pockets are a small but mighty victory — finally, a place to stash keys or a phone without worrying they’ll escape mid‑stride. The high rise and tapered leg make them flattering without being restrictive, and they pack down beautifully without wrinkling. They’re the kind of pants you put on for a hike and then forget to change out of because they’re that comfortable. If you’re on the taller side, definitely go for the Tall inseam — and if you’re taller than 5'9" check your inseam measurements.

Size down! Good stretch Final review! 11/5/2022 So, like I feared, the waist was tight when I went down two sizes. I posted pics labeled size 2. I LOVE how they fit my butt and thighs, but the waist was too uncomfortable. I the pictures you can see that there is give if I pull it, but when it sits naturally on me it is uncomfortable. I tried these on at the END of my day to truly feel how they felt after a day of normal eating. These are made for women who have a small waist and large butt and hips. Not for my body type. I brought it down to 4 stars instead of 5 I love these. I left a video with details too. I got a size 6, but can definitely go down a size. I'm 5'6 about 133lbs and have a 32in inseam. These do not flatten the butt. Good stretch ALL around. The button is flimsy and seems like plastic. But I'm not too concerned because of how well they stretch. I gain weight in my mid section. Gonna buy more in different styles and colors. Hopefully this company is consistent with their sizing. UPDATE FOR SIZE 10/28/2022 So I did it. Went down a size from 6 to 4. I added photos with the size labeled. I did return them to try a size 2. As you can see from the pictures. It was still a bit big. I was very on the fence in returning them for. Smaller size. I decided to walk around my house and wear them a bit to see how they felt while I was moving naturally in them AND I did NOT like how they felt to loose in the butt area. I'm crossing my fingers to see if the size 2 are better. My worry is that the waist will be too tight and the butt/hip too loose still. But I will update for everyone

Order up if you want to wash! I gave these pants five stars because I like them a lot once I figured out what to buy. I usually wear a size 2/4 depending on the brand and am 5’6” tall, 120 pounds. The first pair I ordered were on this site called “ The millennium”. I ordered a small but received a medium and didn’t realize it. (they might’ve sent it to me because Company knew they were small ?) They were a little big when I tried them on and then I washed them and they fit perfect! So order up.I bought some more colors which I thought were the same, but weren’t. They were called “Point Pants”. Same brand and looked the same in the picture but weren’t the because they had more nylon in them than the millennium. I ordered a small and they fit perfect and didn’t really shrink. They were much thicker which would be good for winter, but it is spring and I wanted a thinner pant so I am keeping the medium size millennium pants so I can wash them. I hope this makes sense because these pants are great for winter and spring and summer. And they are also a great price!PS. I would’ve liked to have them a little shorter, but they were all out of the “short”pants.

*EDIT *SOME HAVE NO BACK BELT LOOPS *** UPDATE *** (posted by my daughter) it took three tries but third times a charm as they say right? Finally a small size WITH back belt loops! I was close to giving up but so glad I tried again. I am 5’0, about 122-25lbs, size S, or 2-4 in most things, 26” waist (maybe vanity sizing, ha!) and these fit perfect in length and on my waist (though a little snug, however medium were too big all around through legs and looked like balloon pants so kept small). The thick black, with back belt loops seem to be looser than the other 2 pairs w/o loops I tried. Also will be keeping a thin brown pair with the hidden front closer. Overall the material is noticeable polyester, however they are the style I was after. If you’re petite then I’d try them out for sure! ORIGINAL POST- I have ordered 2 pairs of SMALL thick short trouser and NEITHER have back belt loops. However when I ordered size Medium (which were too big and returned) the size M short HAVE belt loops. So frustrated!! Not sure if I’ll keep or not.

Wonderful dressy maternity pants I hesitated on these at first, because I’d gotten a pair of “wide-legged work pants” for another brand and they were awful! These are pretty much the same material as those, but they used a different pattern to make these, and they’re actually very flattering on me! Not to mention that this material is extremely comfortable. I was also a little concerned about them being too short. I have another pair of maternity pants from this company, G4Free, and the description of those said the regular was a 31” inseam (which is what I normally need,) and those were the perfect length. These state that the regular is a 29” inseam, but when I tried them on, they were plenty long! So I’m glad I didn’t order the Tall this time. Just for reference, I’m 5’6” and was 165 lbs in a size 10 before pregnancy. Now I’m six months along and a few lbs heavier, but my pre-pregnancy size fit perfectly. (With this company, a women’s 10 is a Large.) You probably can’t tell in my photo, but this material is nice and smooth once you put it on. There’s a little split on the sides at the bottom of the pant legs that adds a nice touch to the overall look. I really like these, and can pair a dressier top with them and look really nice when I go out. I highly recommend these.

Good uniform pants I bought a size 4 & 6 for my 5’4” 125lb daughter to wear for her school uniform. The size 6 was too big and the 4 fits perfectly. She is happy with the fit and they look like the fairly standard khaki pant. The pants are a “brushed” fabric khaki and not a smooth weave (which I would have preferred). They look like real jeans! I had surgery about 5 weeks ago and can't wear real jeans for a few months to protect my incisions. I was getting really sick of wearing nothing but joggers and pj pants so I decided to try these. I got the size small in the long/33inch inseam because the regular length felt a little short/flood-y on me. These are PLENTY long on me! In my pictures I'm wearing boots with a 3.5 inch heel and I'm 5'6 and they are perfect length with the heel. The waistband offers a little compression type support which was actually perfect for my post surgery recovery. It felt really nice to be able to have a cute outfit to wear to an early Thanksgiving dinner yesterday and feel put together for the first time in weeks! The waistband didn't roll at all, and these didn't bunch or sag or become loose during the day. The fabric is stretchy and soft and they held their shape perfectly and I'd definitely recommend to anyone healing from an abdominal surgery or for people who just don't like wearing jeans. If you're a leggings girl-you'll love these. The "claw marks" in the description is just the worn look "whiskering" at the hip area. These have a cross over yoga-pant style waistband. I prefer real jeans, but these are definitely great for me for the time being!These have real pockets too!They did have a slight funky smell right out of the package but not nearly as bad as another brand I tried! And the smell goes away if you wash with oxyclean + detergent.

Easy, Breezy, Comfy & Cool As a plus-size girly it is HARD to find palazzo pants that fit and also flow. But these are the ones! I love these pants so much that I bought two more pairs (chocolate & black) in the same size. I’ve worn them to church, date night, around the house, and running errands. They are as soft as your favorite sleep shirt but not see through. Flowy enough that you won’t get hot in the summer but don’t show panty lines. The ruching across the midsection is strong enough to pull high to natural waist or down around your hips and it’ll stay there. Some slight pilling after several washes but nothing big enough to be bothersome. (And my only other complaint is that, while your cell WILL fit in the pockets, excess weight WILL pull the waistband down a bit. Not all the way, just feels weird). If you are looking for breezy summer trousers, buy these. Edit: I am 5’8” and weigh 275lbs

Different than the pictures and video, but the zipper is an interesting touch I was a little disappointed in this shirt/dress. I carefully looked at all the details and watched the video to see what to expect and it arrived different than the listing. For one, the inside is unlined and not black like the video. Second, the zipper is silver and not black. I don’t wear silver, I wear golds so it’s a not my first choice. Also the fabric doesn’t drape like the video- it’s a stretchy polyester so it hangs and does not billow open when I walk. I had a hard time getting it to open at all to show my leggings underneath. It’s also big- I’m 5’3” and 125 lbs. The waist is way too big for me and does not form to any of my curves. Fortunately, it’s at a good price and is unique with the zipper opening. I’ll have to try to sew it to make it fit my frame.

Comparing NCL32 Cotton Leggings + HDL Active Flex What I ordered: NCL32 Cotton: - sizes: x-small & small -1in waistband HDL Active Flex: - sizes: small and medium -1in waistband My Measurements: - waist: 26in - hips: 37.5 - height: 5’3” - weight: 123lbs Here is a breakdown of the specs: HDL Active Flex: - 84% polyester, 16% spandex - made in Vietnam -buttery soft -sizes offered: small-3x -doesn’t shrink -unavailable in 1in waistband capri style with various sizing NCL32 Cotton: - 95% cotton, 5% spandex - made in the US -soft, but not nearly as soft as the Active Flex series -sizes offered: x-small–5x -available in 1in waistband capri style w/ sizing between x-small–3x Conclusion: I tried on both leggings and prefer the softer texture and fit of the Active Flex series. Since polyester in the Active Flex is more durable than cotton in NCL32, I wanted to actually size down to an x-small to achieve that smoothed out, perfectly slim look while still maintaining comfort and stretch. I’m disappointed in the company for not offering that size as an option in full-length or capri in the Active Flex 1in waistband. It seems the closest fabric blend being offered in a capri style is their Buttery Soft series at 92% polyester, 8% spandex, but they only offer One Size, One Size Plus, 1x–3x, and 3x–5x. I will gladly buy from this seller again if they make the Active Flex with a 1in waistband in x-small and begin to offer it in the capri style. For now, I’ll be sticking with Active Flex in size small to see how long it lasts.

Sleek and comfy and great choice of inseam lengths These pants are fabulous for either lounging at home or at work.. The material is 81% polyester and 19% spandex, so there’s plenty of stretch and this material needs no special laundering, comes out of the dryer wrinkle free. What makes these pants look a little dressier to me is that sharp front seam. Very classy looking! The waist clings much like a pair of leggings. I think this is actually designed to be that way to lend shape to your silhouette. I don’t find the waist overly tight or uncomfortable. I like the zippered side pockets for an athleisure flare.The big attraction for me was the choice of a 29 inch inseam because I am petite and have spent my life shortening pants! I ordered a size small with the 29 inch seam and I am 5 feet tall, 117 pounds. These fit perfectly and just drape over the top of my shoe. I will enjoy this alternative style compared to jeans, joggers, or leggings on occasion. Great quality and I highly recommend!

Different colors different fabrics I decided to try these because I was looking for high-waisted drawstring leggings for running. I have a lot of leggings I already love for lifting or for being comfortable, but they don’t always stay up well or are more compression than I want when running. I ordered the dark blue-green and the gray heather. The dark blue green arrived first and I happily took them for a test drive (run) shortly after. They worked great for a 5k, at no point did I feel the need to hike them up or adjust. I was also really happy with the material, they are smooth and soft and buttery with a nice sheen to them. Then the gray ones arrived. And I’m not UNhappy with them…but they are simply not as impressive. They are a thinner, cheaper feelings material. Maybe this is just the nature of heather print anything, and it’s not that it’s necessarily bad that they’re thinner…but following the material of the first pair of leggings, I feel pretty meh about them. I’m not going to return, because I’m sure they’ll still do the job with the high waist and drawstring, but certainly would not order another “heather” color. If the gray pair had been the first and only that I ordered to try, I would not have ordered a second pair. They are pretty thin and feel more like literal tights than standard leggings do. I also ordered racerback shirts from this brand, and the same can be said for those in the heather color vs solid colors - solids feel and look high quality, the heather is kind of scratchier and doesn’t fit/lay the same as the other material. So to summarize, fully recommend the solid color leggings. Personally wouldn’t buy the heather ones or recommend them. I would say they are both true to size, I wear a medium in leggings and the medium fit well (5’6”, 140 for reference). OH also - tie the drawstrings before washing, they are on the short side and I had to fish them out after the first wash.

Maybe this is just the nature of heather print anything, and it’s not that it’s necessarily bad that they’re thinner…but following the material of the first pair of leggings, I feel pretty meh about them. I’m not going to return, because I’m sure they’ll still do the job with the high waist and drawstring, but certainly would not order another “heather” color. If the gray pair had been the first and only that I ordered to try, I would not have ordered a second pair. They are pretty thin and feel more like literal tights than standard leggings do. I also ordered racerback shirts from this brand, and the same can be said for those in the heather color vs solid colors - solids feel and look high quality, the heather is kind of scratchier and doesn’t fit/lay the same as the other material. So to summarize, fully recommend the solid color leggings. Personally wouldn’t buy the heather ones or recommend them. I would say they are both true to size, I wear a medium in leggings and the medium fit well (5’6”, 140 for reference). OH also - tie the drawstrings before washing, they are on the short side and I had to fish them out after the first wash.

Get Ready Boots… Groovy. Retro. Spunky…and most of all, Comfortable. Sleek boots at a great price. Appearance is the primary feature of these boots. They elongate my leg which is definitely a plus, considering that I am 5’3. I feel comfortable walking and standing with these boots on. They also give me a confident stride. I ordered the lighter color, which has a hint of pink, depending on the light. They do attract scuff marks and dirt easily. That means I need to watch my step (lol) to keep the boots spotless. However, they do clean up easily with a white non-abrasive sponge. I’m not sure what type of material was used for these boots, but it’s amazingly buttery. Supple and flexible. The heel appears steep, however, if you measure from the top of the platform to the top of the heel, that’s the actual height of the heel. The inside of the boot is cushioned. The zipper is durable. The boots run true to size. The boots are sewn and not glued completely. Let me see. What else would you like to know ? Water resistant?- Yes. Slip resistant?- Very. The soles are made of textured rubber material. Easy to put on ?-Definitely. And that is due to the length of the zipper which provides a wide opening when unzipped. At the end of the day…”These boots are made for walkin’ .” (N.S.)

Need to decide between sizes - do you want a compression feel or not? Great pair of leggings... I actually ordered 3 sizes (M, SM, XSM). I thought I was going to settle on the Small but after trying on the XS, I decided that I wanted a pair of these as well. I have read many reviews (I have the capri version as well) and one thing that stuck out was the number of people who said size up and the number of people who said to size down. I thought that was weird, but I understand why. It boils down to body type and level of compression you are looking for; all 3 sizes DO fit me as they aren't that different (material does offer a good amount of stretch). For reference, I am 5' 7" and wiegh about 120# with a straight body type (think boy - no real curves). I can imagine that if you carry more weight in your waist vs. hips/legs that could make a difference on which size you order. And I will add this: I had purchased other leggings/capris for about $20 - $25. For a little more, I got some that I love vs the others that I liked. I think these are like 100% polyester and the others were not. So they are on the shiny side (that I like). I haven't worn them nor washed them enough to see the long term wear. They are thin. I don't know how warm they will be in the winter. You'd have to read other reviews for those aspects. And if you go with a size that fits you nicely but isn't super snug, chances are on the capris, they will bag and ride up. That is what bugged me, so going with a smaller size was right for me. P.S. I ADDED A PIC OF THE COLOR. IT IS MORE PURPLE THEN A TEMPERED STEEL (which to me means they are more grey/charcoal colored which these are not).

Good for the price I got this pair first, and then also the white one. First you see what they look like with straight bottom. They become longer after being ironed, out of the package. I'm 5'10", so it is an acceptable length. However, It's still a little short for me, when I walk and move around and I found them not very flattering. I had this idea. Purchased a pair of shoelaces in similar color; cut a small hole on the 'wrong' side of the bottom of each pant leg so that it is between the very bottom and the horizontal seam, and then I threaded a shoelace in each, to tie them around the ankle. And these pants become what you see on the last picture, with a tie on the outer side of the leg. This style is more attractive to me, and works better with this garment for my height. I did the same with the white pair. And I wear both frequently. I haven't had a chance to wash them yet, since I wear them gently and mostly for walks. I heard from other reviews that these pants shrink and pill. Shrinkage is the bigger worry. I have other linen pants that shrink and stretch with washes and wear. My plan with THESE pants is to iron them while wet after the wash, and stretch them out, then hang-dry. In my experience, this works with clothes that are prone to shrinking. At least for a while. For pilling/fuzz, I keep a fabric-shaving machine. It's been a great help for many years, and there's a great selection of them right here on Amazon. Hope this helps.

These tights are insane, I love them. This review is for LUCKELF Women's 80 Denier Soft Opaque Tights, Sparkle Rhinestone Tights in Bordeaux. I'll admit, I was expecting the rhinestones to be a little more subtle. Subtle is one thing they definitely are not! And so many of them! I was planning to wear these for teaching, but I think I'll only be able to wear them under LONG skirts or my class will be distracted by the flashy tights! I wish I'd had these for my trip to Vegas last month. Unfortunately, the exact look of the rhinestones doesn't seem to photograph (or even video, but I tried) well - they're iridescent, sparkling all different colors as the light hits them. The sizing is confusing, given that the sizes on the size charts don't match up to the sizes you can choose from. I'm a size 12 with 47" hips and a "Medium-X Large Tall" fit me perfectly. Even aside from the rhinestones, they're very nice tights. They're the thickness I wanted - just a tiny bit of sheerness but enough coverage that if I wear them under a short skirt it will keep me warm. The color is also perfect, a nice dark maroon. They're exactly what I look for in tights - plus very sparkly! As a side note - I love the feeling of running my hands up and down the rhinestones. If you're looking for clothes that double as a fun sensory stim/fidget, these are excellent.

My HOT new pants! The name “pull on pants” make me think of leggings, but these are completely lined dress pants - and they’re adorable! Great stretch and color (I bought them in red). These are great for us tall gals! I’m a size 14 in most pants - 5’10 and 190 lbs. I got these pants in XXL inseam 35” and they are fantastic! On me, they fit more like a L/XL and more like a 33”-34” and they are sleek enough but loose enough to look like dress pants and not cling to lumps/bumps. The material is like that sateen type of quick dry material that is stretchy but holds it’s shape and is thick enough to lie nicely on the booty and not show cellulite! They’re perfect for dressing up with heels for a nice evening out, and they’re also perfect for lounging around. They’ll be great for travel…they don’t hold wrinkles! This was a great find!

Soft, stretchy, flattering tall jeans—perfect everyday fit 🖤👖✨ These jeans deserve five stars. They are soft, stretchy, stylish, and genuinely comfortable right out of the package. The denim has a nice amount of give without feeling thin, and the fit is classic and flattering. 💖 I ordered the Medium Tall, and they fit me exactly how I like my jeans to fit—not too tight, not baggy, and no digging in anywhere. For reference, I’m 5'8", 127 lbs, with a 29" waist, and these are a great everyday fit. The length is perfect, and I love the dark blue wash with the subtle faded look—very versatile and easy to dress up or down. Construction is also excellent: smooth zipper, nicely stitched seams, and well-made pockets. The front pockets are big enough for your whole hand, and the back pockets are well placed and roomy enough for a large phone. For around $50, these are a great deal, and I know I’ll get a lot of use out of them. They are now the best jeans I own! 👏 Highly recommended! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Super soft and comfy! I love these jeans so much! I barely lost 80 lbs and thought I had to get a size 8 but I bought these size 6 short and they fit me perfectly. It was a little difficult to pull up since there aren’t any belt loops but it’s okay since I was still able to get them up all the way. I like how these seem to have a bit of tummy control without feeling like it’s pressing down too hard on me at all. These are really comfortable and end right at my ankle or at least the top portion of my ankle. I think these have some stretch in them so they don’t feel stiff like normally thicker jeans while still giving you a lot of flexibility. But because of this they may not feel very warm even while indoors. They are indeed skinny jeans and Make me feel nice and slim. I’m 5’2+3/4” and between 135-138 lbs.

***Not 35’ inseam*** ***Tall women beware*** These are not a true 35” inseam. Duped once again from another Amazon seller… they come to around 33.5”. The sizing leans somewhat large and the ‘grey/green’ color I ordered shifts a bit more blue than pictured. The fabric is super soft and if I was shorter, sized down and went with a different color, these would be great lol.

Short. Weird sizing. I’m 5’8” and 6 months pregnant. I got the black wash. I have other size 4/6 maternity jeans from old navy that fit great but they’re skinny style. These 4/6 (small) size are really short and tight up the crack. They’re also tight right along where my baby sits low. I first ordered medium (size 8/10) but they were really baggy in the butt and would slip down with only my phone in the back pocket. They also had a discolored spot. Even the medium wasn’t as long as I would like. Also weird that it’s $7 cheaper for the size small. Pre pregnancy I wore a 4/6. These were just disappointing bc it’s been hard to find an overall great fit and style for a reasonable price. They’re soft though. (pic in camo is of size small.)

Best maternity leggings for tall girls I’m 5’9” currently 39 weeks and about 225 lb. Pictured is around 37/38 weeks. I ordered these around 30 weeks and I wish I would have ordered these sooner! They are actually long enough and feel so soft! I do wish I would have ordered the ones with the pockets but I didn’t realize they made them with pockets. The fabric is great and just the right thickness for me. You can see my underwear lines with certain pairs of underwear but I normally wear a longer shirt anyways so it’s not a problem.

Comfortable work pants These pants easily became one of my new favorite work pants. As we all know, the comfier a work outfit, the better the day! They are the perfect blend of comfort and professionalism. I ordered a medium (150lbs / 5"6 for reference) and they fit snug like a glove. I love the fit especially with the high-rise waist. The hidden elastic waistband gives a smooth fit and makes them extremely comfortable for sitting at a desk all day. The straight leg cut is flattering without being too tight, and they look polished enough for work while luckily still feeling stretchy like yoga pants. Made from polyester and spandex, these are perfectly soft and breathable. I love that the pockets are not only actually functional, but really deep as well. The overall design is simple, versatile, and a perfect addition to any closet!

Some loose threads on pocket | Great Fit | Very Well Dyed These fit really well! There was little to no gapping in the back of the waist. They were also a perfect length. I am 5'7" tall, but struggle with finding pants that are long enough, and these did splendidly. They are very comfortable too with good streatch and the zipper and button were easy - no sticking or difficulty. They are overdyed and very black - they look fantastic, but take care when washing these! I always wash my blacks and grays together and didn't have an issue, but some of my black jeans look a little more black hahaha. I washed on cold with detergent and white vinegar and did not experience any shrinking or issues with seams. Overall, I am very impressed with these. Despite the small quality issue with the pockets, which was easily mended, these are an excellent pair of pants. Also, one last thing to note, these are straight leg - NOT skinny jeans, so they are open at the bottom just like the picture shows. Just a heads up if you are thinking these are skinny jeans.

See Pics and Review for XL Measurements I took measurements prior to wearing. On an XL the waist is 34” unstretched, rise is roughly 13”; cuff is 12” across flat and inseam is 30”. I ordered these in a 31” inseam and they’re only 30”. This length works for me with flats but not if I want to wear anything with a heel. I’m deducting one star for the missing one inch in length. I’m fairly long waisted and these still come up higher than my other jeans but that’s not a bad thing and actually goes a long way toward preventing the dreaded muffin top in my case. They fit fairly snugly through the waist and hips but because they’re so stretchy they don’t pinch or bind anywhere. Dark color wash is slimming. They feel like I’m wearing yoga pants as opposed to wearing jeans which means they’re pretty comfortable. I’d order them again but I really need a 31-32” inseam. Otherwise no complaints.

Great quality and very comfortable! I am 5-5 and 125lbs. Ordered size small in Orange. This is not the first time I have bought this brand and must say their clothes run very true to size. High waisted, flattering, nice material Wanted to make a matching set with the short sleeve top! I got it in the olive green color it’s better in person, it’s not as bright as the online image which I like. It’s a buttery soft stretchy material and flattering. Ordered my typical size XS in Sunzel bottoms. I enjoy the no front seam, butt scrunch detail (which isn’t noticeable when worn but gives a tiny accent lift), and the variety of lengths you can choose. Went with my usual 25” inseam to hit at the ankles as I’m 5’1”. Overall really happy with purchase and would get in more colors!

Gonna order more! All I can say is....where have these pants been all of my life?! They are so lightweight and comfortable that I feel like I'm not even wearing pants. These will be perfect for Florida weather, possibly even in the summer. I'm 5'9, pants size 14, and ordered the one size extra long, and they are plenty long enough. I'm definitely going to get more colors, but I will try the regular length inseam option because there are more colors/patterns to choose from. Update: I ordered a one size regular length. They are long enough, but just barely. The elastic just comes to my ankles and I have to wear them at my belly button, the the long version I could wear the rise up higher, which I really wanted to fold the waist over a bit with the long size anyway. With the long version I end up with a more puffy look at the ankles and she shorter version is not puffy. I think the long version could accommodate someone up to 6' tall but they may get the less puffy look at the ankles. Overall, with my height, I like both. I also like the more fitted look I get with this brand compared to other brands I have tried that are very big and oversized in the width of the legs. I do wish there were more color/pattern options (especially in long size!) because that's one thing that other brands have available that this brand does not. I did find that with other brands that the inseam listed in the product description was longer than the actual pants, sometimes the pants were shorter by 3". Both types I've tried of this brand have accurate overall length and inseam. I do recommend this brand!

you need these!!! so comfy and fit is AMAZING the run a size big!! i am 5’6 125 and normally wear to 2 but i need a 0 in these. the best work pants ever!!! i work for a farm that requires khaki pants but all i could find was slacks in store, so comfy, and stretchy!! my favorite pants ever…let’s just say from one blue cooler girl to another they fit all the right places

Cute and comfortable fall boot I ordered these knee high kitten heel boots in the chocolate suede option Fit: They are true to size. I ordered my usual size 8 and they fit perfectly. They are not too tight, just enough extra space to be able to wiggle my toes but would definitely be a little snug with a thick pair of socks. The pointed toe part is long enough to give space for your toes without them being crammed together. I’m 5’6 and the top of the boot comes to a few inches under my knee. They do not slouch down while you’re wearing. The calf part does not have stretch to it and does not have much room to spare. I do not have large calves and tend to not have any issues with the fit of boots (ie I don’t usually have to look for wide calf boots) So if you do, these might or be great for you. I can maybe fit one finger in the extra space at the most. You may be able to tuck thin pants in if you want, but may be a bit difficult with thicker jeans. Will definitely be best for pairing with skirts and dresses. Quality/look: They look exactly as pictured and are a true chocolate brown. The bottoms of the shoes are a softer plastic (not the super cheap black plastic that makes loud clacking sounds and is slippery) These are a step up from super cheap boots, I think around a $40 price range is fair for these. The zipper zips and unzips very smoothly and unzips to the very bottom so they are very easy to put on and take off. Comfort: They are surprisingly very comfortable! The little heel is thick enough to walk on easily, the arch is not extreme so after walking in them for a bit there were no issues. No hard parts poking into my ankle and no rubbing. Will be a great outfit staple, especially for fall. Overall I would recommend these boots! A cute, comfy wardrobe staple with a timeless look.

Great outdoor pants for tall girls! These pants are so cute, comfortable, affordable, and great for tall women! I so often have a hard time finding pants that fit long legs, but I am 6’2” and these pants fit me great length wise and in the waist. Definitely recommend.

Regret not buying sooner honestly I’m 5’4 and currently weighing 140 lbs. I was originally a size 0 in jeans pre pregnancy so I haven’t been able to comfortably wear jeans in months. I honestly regret not buying these sooner. They are SO COMFY and I love the straight leg jean. They belly bad is SO soft it feels like you’re wearing leggings and the stretchiness of the actual jean material is perfect it feels like a begging. Honestly could not be happier with this purchase. I put off buying maternity jeans for so long bc of the cost bc the comfort and confidence in not having the rubber band holding my pants together is a million times worth it.

Sparkly fun tights but might be a bit tight I am 6' and 230lbs. The XL-4XL Large Plus Tall just barely fits. I had trouble pulling it over my thighs and was sure I was going to rip it or all of the rhinestones would pop off. Neither of those things happened, and I am pleased b/c that means they are well made. The top of the thighs are pretty tight, but once I put on shorts I did not notice it, although I think I would be aware of it while wearing a skirt. Only a couple of the smaller rhinestones popped of throughout the day when I was adjusting, but I can't tell where they came from due to the randomness of the stones (which is a good thing). They are sparkly and fun and made my outfit stand out. My only issue was the 'ankle' where the rhinestones suddenly stopped. I wish that it had been gradual as it made it hard to make a shoe choice because the line of demarcation went well above my ankle and looked silly. I would probably only wear these with higher ankle booties as my Mid Jordan's did not cover it.

Really Cute and Will keep! First, the positive! They fit as I expected. I'd read that many other purchasers sized down. I did not. I am 5 ft 7 in, 148 lbs. I got a size 8. The wait band is pretty tight. Which doesn't bother me as it will hold my pants up! But any smaller and they wouldn't have fit! The fabric is thinner than denim, but stretches! Going into summer this is perfect! They're JUST long enough for me. And the small wear spots are flattering without showing any skin. These however, are not bellbottoms. At best, they are flares. Which I still love! But I was disappointed the flare wasn't bigger considering the description said bell bottom. Over all, I really like them, and likely will order again! It's been a long time since I found a good pair of flares!

Was surprised by the quality of the jeans pockets are deep. Maybe a little long for my liking but I’m picky about length. Definitely worth a try if you’re on the fence!

Wrinkle-free and comfortable This will be a great outfit for travel or lounging, but nice enough to wear when running errands. Fabric is medium weight and soft - I have seen it described as “scuba” fabric. I am 5’5” and 190, and XL fits perfectly. Pants are usually too long, but these are the correct length for a 28-29” inseam. The cuff on the pants helps it make the pant legs more trim. I bought the taupe color, and I would describe it as more of a dusty rose color. There were very few wrinkles out of the package and after a wash/dry, there will not be any wrinkles.

100% Awesome Cowboy Boots! This review is for the 'POYOIOR Cowboy Boots for Women Wide Calf Round Toe Chunky Stacked Heel Knee High Cowgirl Boot Heart Pattern Classic Embroidered Tall Western Booties Pull on.' I ordered these in Red, Size 10.I normally wear a 9 in all shoes, boots; however, I ordered these in the 10 so I would have room for socks and/or gel inserts. I am happy to say, it was a good decision; the boots fit exactly the way I hoped they would - indicating to me that they are true to size! The width of the ankle and thigh is perfect, not too skinny or too wide, and they are very easy to put on and take off. The Faux Leather is good quality bc they look like real leather (at least the red ones do), the color is a beautiful bright red, the stitching is even and pretty, and the heels and soles are sturdy and appear to be attached well. So far, they have been comfortable wearing around the house, breaking them in. I can't yet say how they will feel after wearing for an extended amount of time, but I'm not worried because I have room for gel inserts - LOL!I do have 3 'cons,' but they are personal preferences and in no way warrant a decrease in stars: 1) I'm not crazy about the hearts being white, I think it makes them look like Valentine's Day boots - so I'm going to paint or bedazzle the hearts; 2) I wish the heels were 3/4 to 1 inch higher - it just helps make your foot look a bit smaller; and 3) they are a bit high on me, I am 5'5" tall and my heel to inside knee bend measures 17" - the side seam of the boots measures 18" so on me, my knee is right in the 'V' of the boot which makes my legs look a little short. None of these are a game changer for me but I wanted to be thorough in my review.Overall, I recommend giving these boots a try; for the price, they are awesome boots!

5'4" 137lb Large Absolutely LOVE these camo pants! Tall girl friendly with a drawstring on the bottom so they dont drag if your short, just the right amount of bagginess. Super comfortable. Im 5'4" about 137lbs normally size medium but I sized up to a Large and Im happy I did, they fit perfectly

Thank you for including the talls! I'm 5'10", with a lot of that length in my legs, so finding long enough pants is a forever challenge. I'm very happy with the length of this pair. The pants themselves are comfortable and fit true to size. I appreciate the inclusion of the drawstring as well. They wash great, I always let my pants air dry to avoid shrinkage/losing any length. Theres's plenty of storage/deep pockets, even a pen pocket! The only thing I'd change is the brand name on the cargo pocket, I'd prefer them without it, but that's a small detail I can't hold against them!

THE JEANS ARE FABULOUS! Omg.....I freaking love these jeans! I've lost over 75 lbs and wasn't sure what size i wear now. So I went off of the reviews. I'm 5'2", 152lbs, have always had curvy hips. I ordered the large based on the size chart and reviews and they fit perfect!

Super comfy These are great joggers for exercise and lounging/running errands. I’ve had some weight fluctuations, and it’s nice to have comfortable clothes that work for different activities. The material isn’t too thick or too thin, and I really love that they don’t have that annoying swishing sound as I walk like a lot of woven athletic wears does. A medium could work, but I feel more comfortable with the little extra room of the large. I’m 5’4 150lbs

My favorite leggings I very much enjoy these. They are very stretchy and buttery soft, and flatter my body exceptionally well. For size reference, I bought a small and I have a 27-28” waist and 39” hips. I bought a medium about a year ago which held up nicely aside from 1-2 dangling seam threads here and there, but honestly they were always a bit too big on me, so I bought a small this time and definitely prefer it. My one complaint is that since I’m short (5’3”) the rise is very long on me, like the waist goes all the way up to my longline bra, but I’m ok with that and it’s not a design flaw whatsoever. Can’t have everything.

I think they could fit absolutely anyone Edit on edit: Well I notice quite a few ppl at work wear these jeans and they mold (mould?) to every body type. One woman has that round booty tiny waist, another wide hips, skinny legs, another average. Looks great on all. The darker color is a tad more rough and stiff. And there is a slight chemical smell (the dye?) Edit: I have since bought several colors and sizes and even bought a size 2. Since there is stretch they get loose throughout the day. 2 is perfect. A little tight at first, but they loosen. So I’d consider going down your normal size idk. Since skinny jeans are out and I’m too old for the baggy grunge look wanted straight leg jeans. Happened to see on IG and tried them. Love em. The light color is perfect for spring and cold summer days as the fabric is on the lightweight side. Def not thick lucky jeans or gap, and not as closely knitted or buttery as Paige. Has a very slight course feel to the touch. Almost as comfy as pjs because of the stretch and thinness. The stretch could allow for many different body types to wear I think. I’m 5’6” 120lbs w heavier thighs flat a$$ and wide hips and size 4 M length is loose perfect, goes over hips easily, I may even consider these high rise and length is perfect w a slight crease at ankle if wearing flats

Good fit. Shallow pockets. I’m 5-6, 150 lbs, size 12 is a perfect fit. The rise comes up to my belly button and the inseam is perfect (long enough to almost touch the ground). Legs are a straight fit. I’d give 5/5 for fit. The color is as expected and closure, seams and other details looks to be high quality, especially for the price. My only complaint - and reason for 4/5 stars - is the back pockets, although functional, are not deep enough to hold a phone. Clothing designers of the world, PLEASE, women’s clothes also need pockets!!!

Love, but watch the length and size So, ladies, here's the skinny... very much pun intended 😆. But seriously, I am 5'5, 150lbs, long legs shorter torso, 36C.. i got the l/xl in red. Whilst I am of average height, albeit longer legs, I still found these fall after movement, about mid to lower calf. I might imagine a bit tighter after even a cold wash. AND, if you get a color like I did, WASH IN COLD AND HANG DRY OUTSIDE OVER GRASS.. SEPARATELY from anything!!!!! You've been warned lol!!! The color is beautiful, vibrant red.. i can imagine the other colors, which i will be buying more of, in a larger size 😂. Oh, and it. Is. SOFT! O. M. G. Yummy. Like the velvety side of a 2 layer blanket, fabric. The pic is it next to my burnt orange blanket.. the underside 😁. I took 1 star for the length. It's borderline, and for the size, it's 1 size too small. I like them comfy, not restricting.

FINALLY A BOOT THAT FITS! I have ALWAYS had big calves. Im talking thicc with like 4 c's, even when I was tiny and weighed 120 pounds. So finding cute boots that I could actually wear was impossible. WHY DID NO ONE TELL ME WIDE CALF BOOTS ARE A THING?!!! I did almost panic when putting them on for the first time cause they /almost/ didn't zip up all the way, but once I adjusted the little strap, they zipped.

Nice in general, nothing overly exciting, zippered pockets a plus I got these in a medium/31 inch length and am happy with the sizing. 5'7" and about 138 lbs, so I feel sizing is accurate. I love the color, the fit was nice, I just wasn't thrilled about the band on the ankles. I wish it was tapered more as the leg is wide and then the band on the ankle is tight, some other joggers seem to have a smoother transition so it looks better. I am happy with zippered pockets. Having lost a car key fob before, I like to make sure things like that can't fall out. Nice thickness, a pair of thermals will fit underneath if I want to wear in winter outside. For the price and the ankle band, I would not get a second pair in a different color but I will definitely get use out of these while they last.

Flattering! These pants are super flattering and I love how they fit - comfortable and soft. They are bigger than I expected them to be based on sizing, so I would suggest to go down a size. They are also really long, and at 5’6” I am going to have to hem mine, so they are really meant for quite tall people! (That said I sew a lot so it’s not an issue for me). I just love the way these pants fit and hang on my body!

Comfortable, Functional, and Flattering—With a Small Fit Note for Petites These Yogipace pull-on ankle pants are a fantastic blend of comfort and utility. The fabric is lightweight with just the right amount of stretch, making them ideal for everything from travel and hiking to lounging or a casual round of golf. The zippered side pockets are a standout feature—secure, roomy enough for essentials, and sleekly integrated into the design. I also appreciate the two faux back pockets, which add a polished look without bulk. As someone who is 5'2", I did find the rise a bit high—the elastic waistband sits above my belly button, which may not be ideal for all petite frames. That said, a simple waistband roll gives me a more customized fit without compromising comfort or style. As you can see the length still reaches at the ankle Overall, these pants are a versatile wardrobe staple. Just be mindful of the rise if you're on the shorter side. I’d love to see a true mid-rise or low-rise option in the future, but for now, these are a solid pick for active days or travel.

Expect some shrinkage in length I have purchased several pairs of these jeans in different colors and they all fit great with plenty of stretch. Be aware that these jeans do vary slightly from one color to another. I noticed the darker colors seem to run slightly larger than the lighter ones. I am short (5’-2”) so I bought the short inseam (actually 30” not 29” as noted in description), as recommended by the size chart. Upon trying them on, the leg length fell perfectly at my heal. After a few washes the length is now above my shoes and looks awkward. I then purchased the medium inseam (actually 32” not 31” as noted in description) which was a few inches too long. I had to fold them so they wouldn’t drag on the floor or bunch up at my shoes. After a few washes, sure enough, they shrank and the length is now perfect. I would recommend purchasing the longer inseam if you are in between the heights noted on the size chart. Also, if you are like me and have bigger thighs, the material will pill quickly in the crotch area as well. I snapped a photo after a few months of wear, for reference. Overall, I am satisfied with the jeans and would purchase again.

Must have maternity jeans Best maternity jeans and super flattering. I’m just over 5 feet tall and weigh 125 pounds and I ordered the black in a size small. Couldn't ask for better I have other Yogipace pants and love them all. However, I wanted to review these 25-inch Small Yogipace joggers. I am 5' tall and 120 pounds. If you are short, you can relate to how hard it is to find pants that fit. I always bought cotton pants in the past but I feel very differently now. These joggers are very lightweight and feel very good on my skin. I wash them in a mesh bag and hang dry them. They dry fast. The joggers are beyond comfortable. A very flexible waist and a flattering fit. I posted a picture of the navy and black ones I bought and a picture of the navy's waistband. I've happily worn them in temperatures between 40 and 70 degrees. I have fleece-lined Yogipace pants for winter. While they are a bit pricey, all of my Yogipace pants are wearing beautifully. To me, they are very worth the investment.

Great length… nice fabric I am very happy. Being 5’8” I struggle getting sweats that are long enough. Phone fits in pocket with no issue. Pleased with my purchase.

Comfortable Stretch Jeans – More Bootcut Than Skinny I’m not tall, but I have long legs and a short torso, so I usually buy tall sizes in pants to get the right length (32" inseam.) These jeans were perfect in the inseam – finally a pair that actually reaches my ankles without looking cropped. The fabric feels more like jeggings material than traditional denim, with lots of extra stretch and softness. They’re very comfortable for all-day wear and move easily without feeling stiff. The description says skinny leg, but on me the fit is closer to a slim bootcut, which I actually ended up liking for a more relaxed, everyday look. For sizing, I’m a 32 waist and followed the size chart, ordering the XXL because I expected a tighter fit. I definitely could have sized down – these run true to size, not small like many stretch jeans. If you’re between sizes, I’d recommend choosing the smaller one for a more fitted feel. Overall, these are comfy, flattering, and great for anyone who needs extra length with plenty of stretch. These also have a good quality and seems it would be durable enough to last for years to come. I’d order again, just one size smaller next time.

Love these jeans I really love the way these fit. I’m 5 foot 8 and 173 pounds. Since these are juniors I bought the 14 plus. Unfortunately, the stitching on the pockets is very frayed after 1 wear and 1 wash. However, I have a few pairs of these jeans in different colors and I do love them. Also, in some colors I need long and other colors I need regular. It seems the darker the favric the longer they are. They’re very comfortable and hold shape pretty well through the day.

Cute decent fall boots! Exactly what I was looking for. Cheap knockoff without a dramatic heel that will last likely 2 seasons. The chunky heel is great because it adds height without the stark footbed angle that causes pain like other high-heeled boots. I ordered the burgundy and the brown and decided to keep both because they both gave such different vibes. I am 5’2” and they are still true calf boots, not over my knee thank goodness. The bed feels like memory foam, very comfy! And the toes don’t squish, they are rounded enough to mostly fit them all. The colors are pretty but I imagine will be easily scuffed. Though I can’t confirm that yet, most boots of this price range and quality are not meant to be long lasting. I usually wear my boots with some small scuffs, and get up to 3 years before they are just embarrassing or peeling. As for sizing, I am about an 8 (sometimes 7.5 but I lean towards more room), and they fit true to size for size 8 and not squished at all, especially with my wool blend socks I use in the colder months. If you wear thin socks only and are between sizes you might be able to go with the smaller size. So far, I would recommend. I’ll try to update after some constant wear in the fall/winter.

Otherwise, they fit so good.

“I’ll only wear these for Halloween” lol *adds more to cart* I LOVE these jeans!! I actually bought them for my Halloween costume (Joyce-Stranger things) and thought for the price and wearing them one day it was fine. Little did I know I would now be coming back to buy more in other shades. I am 5’4”, 155 lbs, and got a size 12 based off measurements I took. I was on my period so I do think a 10 would fit me as well, because there is comfy stretch to these pants. They are truly high waisted but I love that. Classic mom jeans win with extra stretch and comfort. After wearing them several hours walking around trick or treating, they did loosen like most jeans too, so that’s another reason I want to try a size 10. But with my weight fluctuations these will remain a commonly worn pair of pants. Hard to tell in the photos how high the waist is. But you can see there’s some looseness in the legs after 3 hours of wearing them when the pic was taken. I also felt the regular length was fine for someone on the shorter side. Classic 80’s fit, which is perfect for what I was looking for.

Big rips on the mom jeans These look so cute on the model and honestly, I like them for my body shape too. I am 5-2, 120 pounds with a short torso. I usually wear hip huggers below my stomach but I wanted to try something different. Price was good. I got the medium and found it fit really well in the legs, but tight at the top. Sizing is tough Figuring out sizing was not for the faint of heart. I have a 32.5 inch inseam. I ordered these in a 10 and the inseam was 31 even though it’s listed as 32. I don't care for is the faux button on the pants either -- it dangles there loosely and looks a little sloppy. The photo makes it look like it's actually against the fabric. Not sure if this is a manufacturing defect or expected -- but it's nothing major. She's said she'll be looking for more pants in this style, so overall I think this one is a good find.

**TRUE PETITE SIZING** Okay sooo……. I am super pleased with these scrubs. I am a new grad RN, I am 5’0” tall, and 95 pounds. I wear a 00 in jeans. I have been on the hunt for some TRUE petite sized scrubs. So far I have tried white cross, barco, Adar, and these HeartSoul. None of the others have even come close in comparison to how good these scrubs fit me! I got the XXS petite in the pants and the XXS in the top. I do have a little booty for my size, and these pants actually kind of show it off 🤣 which I never can say in scrubs because in all other brands i get the absolute smallest size and they’re still baggy on me. I may could have gotten just an XS petite in these pants and they would still have fit me good. Very pleased with the true petite fit. I haven’t wore them for a shift yet so I’ll update later on their durability, but so far the quality seems good.

Stylish comfort I ordered these capri denim jeans to fill out my summer wardrobe. The medium-weight denim is 59.8% cotton, 27.9% polyester, 10.6% viscose, and 1.7% elastane. The fabric is very stretchy and breathable. They are soft to the touch. They are lightly distressed, adding to their style. I ordered my usual size 16, and the fit is very good. Being 5’2”, I often shorten pants. These hit me at mid-calf. I like them at this length and will skip the alteration. They are comfortable to wear. The stretch has a lot to do with the comfort. They fit well around the waist. There is no gap in the back of the waistband. I wore them all day on a busy Saturday, and not once did I consider changing out of them. After a day of housework and errands, I changed to a dressy top and stilettos and wore them out to dinner at an upscale restaurant. The workmanship is excellent. All seams are straight and free of gaps, puckers, and hanging threads. The button is secure and slides easily in and out of the buttonhole. The zipper is installed properly and operates well without catching on any wayward fabric. Stress points have reinforced stitching. The large front patch pockets will hold even the largest of phones. There are also two smaller patch pockets in the back. I machine-washed them with similar items in warm water and dried them on low. They came out ready to wear without any noticeable fading or shrinkage. These are likely to last a long time.

That did, I think they'll look cute in the spring/summer with some sandals or wedges. Sorry pics are blurry. I'm 5'4", 136 lbs, wearing the size 6. Price point is fair.

Denim and Stitching Color & Pocket Size As much as I like the style and fit of these - Sidefeel Women's Wide Leg Jeans High Waisted Stretchy Big Pockets Capri Denim – jeans, I am disappointed with this purchase. The color of the jeans that I received differs from what is shown in the photographs in the listing, as is the stitching. The thread in all the photos looks to be white. The pants I received, the thread is orange. I had ordered the “Bluing” color of denim pants which, in the photos appears to be more navy blue than what I received which looks closer to the “Ashleigh” color. The bigger disappointment is in the size of the front pockets which are much smaller/shorter than the pockets that are pictured in all the listing photographs. The fit is fine although they are shorter than I thought they were going to be and I’m only 5’4” tall too. Because of all of the discrepancies, I dropped a few stars. For sizing reference; my waist measures 38”, and my hips measure 48”. I purchased a size 18.

You either like them or you don’t These cropped high waisted pants are straight-lined. They don’t have any stretch but are comfortable…if they fit comfortably around your middle. I can wear these with boots, sandals, or other casual shoes /heels. I can dress them up or down. The size is true to size and the color is a dark blue wash, pockets included. The only drawback to these pants is that if you’re a leaner build, the pants will sit straight down your frame, but if you have a full figure, you’re going to look fantastic. Recommendation: I’m a size large, tall at 5’8” and 160 lbs. personally I didn’t like how they look on me but others have told me that they were cute-looking. If you like the cropped look, then this pant is for you.

They're just way too big and baggy for me. I am 5'1 and about 118lbs. They fit nicely around my waist but not my knees and they do the weird ruffle thing when I sit or bend. They are also very stretchy so even though they fit nicely on my waist I can still stretch them out.

Fits well and tall girl approved. I just got these jeans so I might update as I actually start wearing them day to day but so far I like the fit. I am 5’9 174 lbs and I ordered a 14 long. I wear a 12 usually but I’m glad I sized up based on the reviews. The waist does have stretch to it so they’re comfy. The length is perfect for my height, goes to my ankles, not too short or long. I keep trying to wear different silhouettes of jeans to be more current with today’s trends, but my millennial self just seems to look and feel best in skinny jeans. These are definitely reasonably priced as well.

Highly recommend! I was VERY skeptical of these jeans due to the cost, let's face it, Levi's for under $20 is VERY rare! I ordered the "Totally Shaping Bootcut Jeans" in a size 6 short (I am 5'3, USA) and while they are a bit loose at the waist, they are super comfortable! PROS: VERY comfortable. My iphone 15 fits about 3/4 of the way in the back pocket and when I bend forward or sit down, my phone does ride up but it does not fall out of the pocket which is a BIG plus! I washed in cold water and color did not bleed. I also put in the dryer and while I am not sure if it shrunk or not, it definitely fit better, but again, still a bit loose at the waist. It is a "bootcut" and as you can see on the pictures, they have a fabulous flair which I believe works great with my figure. CONS: While I listed it as a "pros" above, I do believe it shrunk a little bit in the dryer which I loved as it fit better, but if it fits tight when you initially try on, make sure you air dry as it will shrink a bit. Overall, these jeans are a complete steal and highly recommend, they are true levi jeans so you get great quality material, great fit and super comfortable jeans!

Great when NEW jeans are shipped! I will say this has been a process. The first pair of jeans came with NO tags and had obviously been worn and washed. They had what appeared to be paint stains and were very worn looking. The material had obviously been washed in either very warm or hot water. They fit very well or I probably would have just returned them for credit and bought Levi’s 315. I was looking for an alternative to spending upwards of $65.00 on a pair of Levi’s. I ask for a replacement and I noted the pair I had received were used. While I support recycling, if you purchase a pair of NEW jeans, they should be that, new with tags. These jeans should be labeled as returned in good condition with stains with a price reflecting that. It took about 2 weeks for the replacement pair to arrive, but they are NEW, unwashed jeans with tags. They fit very well. In comparison to the Original Levi’s 315, the material is thinner. The fit is very similar, but not identical. The 315 size 2 short are snugger through the hips and thighs and the flare on the bootcut is not quite as much. The rise is about a 1/2 inch lower on the original 315. In comparison with price being the main selling point, the Signature series is a good buy. I don’t know if they will hold up to the Original Levi’s quality. I only wash my jeans every 3-5 wears. I turn them inside out and wash with other jeans on cold water, gentle cycle. The front pockets are adequate and the same as Levi’s original. The back pockets have the Signature stitching and are similar to the original in size and shape. These are a lightweight denim and I don’t see them as being warm, probably better for a milder climate, or Fall weather. I have attached pictures for reference. The tags are on the new pair. For reference, I am 5’4” have long legs and a short torso and weigh 112. I wear a size 2 in Original Levi’s so I say these run very true to size. The inseam was right at 29 1/2 inches. Due to these being bootcut jeans, if you shorten them you would loose the “bootcut”. If you need a true short length of 28”, I don’t see these working.

The pockets go more to the side of my thighs than typical jeans. I ordered a size 14 long. I am in between sizes 12 and 13 right now. Most 13s are to big, but I read the reviews from others and went with the 14 long. I am 5'8" and 200-205lbs. Im hoping these jeans will fit me when I get back to 190. I put them on right away to walk around the house in them to see if they loosen up any! The front of the calf area seem a little big/loose for me. Not enough to return them. I dont like the "ruffle" look it's giving off, but after a wash, hopefully itll all pan out. The length seems to be just right with my shoes! If I were a heal person, the length would be just right too. They feel like jeans! Look like jeans!! Just not the pressure from a button or two and zipper digging into my belly anytime I sit down. (I hate jeans)

Dye transfer is a big issue. Fit is great, ordered a 16. I'm 5'5 and the length is perfect. My problem is dye transfer. I washed them 2x before wearing normally and noticed too much die. Did three washes with vinegar and salt. Still dye transfer to the shoes, and wall. . I have never had this happen before. The vinegar salt wash always works. Be aware. Picture of a dry and wet cotton swab gently rubbed on jean and transfer I made rubbing against door frame.

Very Pleasantly Surprised!! I am so glad I took a chance ordering these jeans. I order a lot of clothes from Amazon but have never ordered jeans online before this. I’m 5’2”, 150-155lbs. My measurements are not ideal. My waist is 38.5” and my hips are 41.5” I refer to my shape as Sponge Bob-ish. There is almost no difference between my waist and my hips and I have hardly any butt to speak of. To top it off, I have slender legs! I got a 12 short, (31 waist, the tag says). I unsure about getting short because sometimes they end up too short but these work! I have pants in size 8 to 12 or large but most are medium or 10. I got 12 because HATE tight waisted pants. Also tight waists push the fat up.I’m 48 so trying out alternatives to skinny jeans because they don’t really flatter my box with legs shape. Hope this helps! I got one in dark blue and this light blue. Both fit the same, some say different colors fit differently, I did not find that trueHaving said that all that, these jeans fit and feel great! They aren’t too boot cut but just the right amount. The flat elastic wait band is perfect! I prefer mid waisted pants because I carry my extra weight in my mid section.

Overall they are okay First I do like these jeans and I might order another pair because of the price. HOWEVER, they are very stretchy and don’t seem to be the best quality. They also aren’t as long as I thought they should be. (I ordered longs). I do like them I was just hoping the fabric quality would be less stretchy cheap and longer for my height of 5’9. I ordered 10 longs because I was worried about the length instead of 8 longs, I’m really glad I ordered the 10s because they are long enough to be socially acceptable.

Fits in all the right places! I love love love these jeans. I ordered a size 18, and they fit fantastically! Stretch in all the right places. Make sure you follow the washing instructions because the darker blue does bleed initially - blue hands if you don't wash them before wearing. They are super comfortable. I'm 5'7 and they are the perfect length for me. I'm so excited to wear these over the holiday season.

Stylish, Comfy, Perfect fit! For reference I am 5'6" 243lbs and I ordered the recommended size XL. I am super super happy with the fit and comfort of these baggy jeans. This is my first pair and they give me the exact vibe I was going for while feeling comfortable and stylish. I could live in these they are so comfortable. They fit very well but don't feel tight. The leg/thigh fit is amazing. I have thick thighs and somehow these give fitted but loose looking vibes I can't explain it. Like im Wearing baggy jean but they don't feel like im wearing baggy jeans. I can bend and move flawlessly in these and no backside spillage if ya know what I mean. The length is perfect I paired a simple pair of slip on sneakers and the length was on point. I can literally run Ari and in these all day or night and feel comfortable. They move with my body and do not cut into the waist area. And trust me I have a thicker waistline. The price was well worth it and a great value. There are so many jeans to choose from and I'm so happy I found these and this brand. The pockets are of good size the button and zipper closure work well. I just don't know what else to say about these. I'm super happy with these.

Nice jeans. More of a bell bottom than flare or bootcut More of a bell bottom than a flare or bootcut. However, I love these jeans. They are light weight and not too think which makes for a good summer jean. They fit well and hug the butt nicely. I’m 5’5” and 185ish, I purchased a 12 short.

Good material, flexible fit. Surprisingly Flattering and High-End Quality! I couldn’t believe how great these jeans are! I’ve always been loyal to high-end denim brands, and these totally exceeded my expectations. Right now I’m in between sizes—I’ve gained some weight but am already on my way back down. I ordered both the size 14 and the 12 in short length. I normally wear a 14 (I’m 5’2” and 180 lbs), and the 14s fit comfortably, but I was shocked to find that the 12s fit too! They were snug, but the material is stretchy, soft, and extremely forgiving—surprisingly comfortable even in the smaller size. Both sizes looked amazing in the mirror. The short length hits just right for me when I’m wearing 3–4 inch heels, which is my go-to look. In these images I’m wearing heels, about 3.5 inches. The jeans graze the floor in a flattering way and have that perfect bootcut silhouette that works beautifully over high-heeled boots. If you look at the product photos, you’ll see they fit like a dream and give that sleek, elongated leg effect. One thing to note: the short length runs a little longer than expected, so I wouldn’t wear them with sandals unless I hem them slightly—but with heels? Perfection. I highly recommend these jeans, especially if you’re curvy and love a high-end look at a great price. I’ll probably keep the size 12 as my motivation jeans, but honestly, both sizes are a win. 👖✨

Sudden change in sizing Update March 19 2025: I’m updating my review because I’ve ordered 3 pairs of these jeans now. The first 2 were so great and fit so unbelievably well which is what made me order for the 3rd time. I literally just re-purchased the exact same pair from my previous orders, yet this 3rd pair is nothing like the first 2. They do not fit the same, they are huge and too long and baggy on me. I am so upset 😔 I was telling anyone and everyone to buy these and now they apparently changed the sizing or something and now I have 2 great pairs and 1 pair that is huge on me even though they’re all a size 2 short. What a bummer 😔 I am in complete shock by how well these fit. I never expect jeans to fit me perfectly, even if I buy them in store, because they are always way too long and I either have to go get them altered or roll them up (even if they are “short” sized). But I’m 5’3 and these are actually the perfect length, don’t touch the ground when I walk AND are form fitting and flattering without being uncomfortable and awkward to try and move around in. 10/10 will be buying more immediately
