What's up with the cost difference? I purchased 3 *different* pairs of these jeans. One pair of "Immaculate, One pair of "Janice" and one pair of "Mascara." All in the same size, 16L. All described to be the same cut, "Gold Label" etc. etc.. All you have to do is chose the 'color.' But the price varies wildly. So I assumed something other than the color of the jeans was the basis for this reasoning. Wrong. Apparently the price is soulely based on popularity. Seriously? Please read on, because I do love these jeans & I did get a more than satisfactory response from Amazon. I'm 5'9" and thick in the middle and prefer a little extra length to my jeans. I probably could have gone a size smaller, but I wanted to allow for at least ten percent possible shrinkage due to washing. And I don't want to look ridiculous. The jeans I ordered have slightly different shades of dark blue with gently faded patches. But there really is no other way to tell which pair is which. Remove *all* the tags, and you have 3 pairs of the same jeans on in different shades. When I showed my husband, he didn't notice much of a difference between the pairs either. Just that the 3rd was slightly darker. However all three pairs cost different prices. The most expensive pair being titled "Immaculate." Once the jeans came in the mail and I had tried them all on, surprisingly *they all fit exactly the same.* I have no problem admitting that I'm very happy with all three pairs. But laid out side by side, all 3 pairs of the jeans look *very* similar. Was I sent the same model *twice*, or what? Why is one pair so expensive? No where on the jeans' tags do I see any difference between them other than their scan numbers. They have no 'names' or clear numbers, like my beloved "501's." If I had gone to the mall and picked out the same jeans myself, they would have all been from the same shelf at the same price. They are all the same length, cut, rise, style, and fit exactly the same. The inside and outside labels, text/print and logos are nearly the same as well. And only one pair states "Gold Label" on the outer back leather tag. It's like the one pair of jeans came out a year earlier under a different name - but is otherwise the same in every other way to the other pairs. So I don't get why one pair cost me twenty one dollars, the next pair costing me twenty four dollars, and the last cost a whopping thirty eight dollars? I'm seriously considering returning the most expensive pair/s (which ones though?) and reordering the cheapest pair/s again. Like I said, I *really* like these jeans. They all *fit* the same and fit well and are flattering. But I feel like someone is trying to pull the wool over my eyes here, and I'm really annoyed by the difference in cost because that is the only thing that really differentiates one pair from another, aside from the color. Because if it's color/dye that costs more, that's absurd, and one or more pairs has to go back. In the mean time this whole thing has kept me from actually wearing any of the jeans. So. Which pair or pairs of jeans do I return?!

But laid out side by side, all 3 pairs of the jeans look *very* similar. Was I sent the same model *twice*, or what? Why is one pair so expensive? No where on the jeans' tags do I see any difference between them other than their scan numbers. They have no 'names' or clear numbers, like my beloved "501's." If I had gone to the mall and picked out the same jeans myself, they would have all been from the same shelf at the same price. They are all the same length, cut, rise, style, and fit exactly the same. The inside and outside labels, text/print and logos are nearly the same as well. And only one pair states "Gold Label" on the outer back leather tag. It's like the one pair of jeans came out a year earlier under a different name - but is otherwise the same in every other way to the other pairs. So I don't get why one pair cost me twenty one dollars, the next pair costing me twenty four dollars, and the last cost a whopping thirty eight dollars? I'm seriously considering returning the most expensive pair/s (which ones though?) and reordering the cheapest pair/s again. Like I said, I *really* like these jeans. They all *fit* the same and fit well and are flattering. But I feel like someone is trying to pull the wool over my eyes here, and I'm really annoyed by the difference in cost because that is the only thing that really differentiates one pair from another, aside from the color. Because if it's color/dye that costs more, that's absurd, and one or more pairs has to go back. In the mean time this whole thing has kept me from actually wearing any of the jeans. So. Which pair or pairs of jeans do I return?!

Struggling to find low rise jeans? Struggle no more! I purchased 2 styles of the flamingals jeans. I hate trying on jeans. I’ve yet to find a pair from any of the brands I like. I’m short waisted and any rise over 10.5” basically sit right under my bra. I purchased 2 pairs of this brand to try. The pair w/o a zipper and has the cute buttons/snap design did not fit well. But this pair are the bomb! I’m 5’4” and weigh 145lbs. I’m a medium or size 8 in Talbots, medium in fresh produce, s/m in free people, a size 9 in Maurice’s. Medium in Coldwater Creek. These are advertised as low rise and sit right below my belly button. They fit firmly on my waist & rear. I love the 2 button closure. They have good but firm stretch and none of the seams are twisting around my leg. I’m keeping this pair and will follow up with my review after laundering and wearing a few times. UPDATE: I ended up purchasing size small. Although the medium fit, once they relaxed a little they were a bit looser than I prefer.

Inconsistent sizing, comfortable My experience: I purchased Royal Chakra Size 8 short. They are perfect! Comfortable, just the right length for this 5’3.5” lady. They don’t make me look chubby. This pair is so great I decide to buy another in a second color. I purchase the color Niagara. Niagara is tighter, but still comfortable enough I suppose. I move my legs and then discover this pair is definitely, noticeably shorter than my other pair. No wonder the Amazon reviews have “mixed feedback” on the length and fit. That was very disappointing. I’m keeping the second pair but they are a little too short. I don’t like my socks hanging out every time I sit down. When I’m standing up they both look great… I had wanted one more pair so that I can have three pairs that fit me. Do I go with a different brand? Do I risk getting a black pair and hope they fit? Do I just get a second pair of the Royal Chakra? I wear my jeans for many years-until they’re ready to fall apart-so I hope I end up with a good third pair. Good luck buyer!
Steal $15 jeans have no business looking this good. I'm 5'4", 160, 58 years old, in flabby condition, see photo for why I'm so impressed with these jeans

Size inclusive and comfortable! Great quality jeans! I checked the size guide but according to the reviews many people were saying to size down. I’m 5’4” with most of my height being my legs, 190lbs of mostly leg muscle and I hold most of my weight in my belly. The size chart recommends a size 16plus but I ended up getting a 14plus and they fit absolutely perfect! They feel very stretchy and comfortable and I ride horses so I believe these would be comfortable enough to use at barrel races and I wouldn’t feel like they’re too baggy or too tight. If I had one single thing to say constructively, I’d say maybe they need to be a smidge longer. If I got long sized jeans they’d be too long, and since I’ll wear them with cowgirl boots they may look a little short, but I’m willing to sacrifice that and have them fit everywhere else. Most jeans I purchase are too long so I really like these. Overall 10/10 and worth the money! Quick delivery within 48 hours.

I am 5’3”. Get the short length if you don’t want them too long. I ordered 2 pairs of these about 10 days apart. The first pair in perfect condition (color Magic), fit well, the average length on me is very long ( I am 5’3” ) and I have to pull them up now and then as they get long in the crotch, not a deal breaker. I liked them enough to order a second pair, but this time I ordered the length in short, the length is perfect and they fit well. Disappointing that there is a defect in the stitching on the back pocket and material is a little thin below the pocket which could make them wear out quicker. I tend to wear longer tops so not sure if I should exchange. The colors in the pictures look the same but the Christy Pure is a little lighter. Both pairs are very soft. I do recommend these jeans, they are very comfortable. 4 stars for the stitching defect, 5 stars for the fit in the short length.

For reference I have 42" hips and a 28" waist. Nope! Sigh, the hunt for jeans that actually fit continues. Also, the ad is misleading, these jeans claim to be mid rise, but they are actually low rise, (waist band is 2-3 inches below bellybutton.) If you are looking for low rise jeans that gap at the waist, you would probably like these.

Large waist I am 158lbs @ 5'3", measuring 36DD-32-42, and I normally wear a sz 12 in jeans. I bought a sz 10 in these pants based on other reviews saying to size down, and I loved how comfortable they seemed at first. As someone who has a small belly, I wasn't bothered by the waistband being slightly large, and the waistband didn't roll. They are very soft, not too thick or thin, have good stretch, and fit comfortably everywhere else. I did wish that they had real front pockets. I was ready to keep these, but as I wore them while getting ready to go out I noticed that they just didn't look right with any of my tops, and gave the appearance of being slightly baggy in front. I also had the worry they would loosen more while wearing so I'm returning them. It may be worth it to mention that they didn't come with any brand tag or price tag, so it's possible it was a previously returned item.

Comfortable quality I'm 5'8" and about 180lbs. Most of my weight is in my midsection. I dont have much of a butt or thick thighs. I'm usually a size 12 and that's what I ordered. I did the try before you buy option which is a great benefit for sure. The 12 fit me about right, perhaps a smidge loose in the waist. These jeggings had a nice comfort band and the material was comfortable and a little thicker than I expected. It definitely is not a cheap looking or feeling pant. I sent it back because I didn't like how shirt it was on me. If it had been longer then it would have made a winter staple for sure due to the thickness of the material, which is a little heavier than I'm used to.

Joes jeans look for the fraction of the price! Ok so Amazon knocked it out of the park with these, truly, and I’m SO confused by all the bad reviews! 🤷‍♀️ I know all bodies are different but for me these are PERFECTION. 🙌🏻🙌🏻 They totally look and fit exactly like a pair of Joes jeans boyfriend cut I used to have and LOVED. But I ended up giving them away after I lost weight and they became too big. I’m a Torrid size 2 on top, and apple shaped. I wear a 33/16 in Abercrombie and Levi’s, sometimes a 14 if they have stretch. I ordered a 16 in these and they’re beyond perfect. Slimming through the top half and the legs are perfectly tapered and loose, but not too loose. My legs legit look a mile long in these! 😲😍 I don’t even normally like high waist jeans due to being very short waisted, but the high rise on these just ends up being uber flattering and slimming. I’m 5’6 and they’re about an inch long on me, but nothing a low heel can’t fix. The denim itself is pretty stretchy… stretchier than Abercrombies imo, and MUCH softer. The ‘medium blue vintage’ wash that I got is one that goes with everything in your closet. I LOVE, love these. I own Agoldes, Good American, Buckle Black to name a few and these are better than all of them, hands down. I wish my pics of these did them justice but my full length mirror broke, so all I can do is crappy bathroom pics, sorry! 🤣🤦‍♀️😬 You NEED these jeans!

Good Leg Shape / Heavy & Uncomfortable / Waist Gap Re: Medium Blue Vintage & Black (14 Regular, Low Stretch) Review keeps not posting -- guessing too long? -- trimming and retrying: After reading Jennifer Romolini's glowing reviews, I had high hopes for these jeans. Unfortunately, both pairs fell short of expectations. As a fan of vintage & contemporary rigid denim, and a sometime wearer of semi-stretch denim, I found the fabrication of these jeans perplexing. They're somehow stiff, *heavy*, and uncomfortable, while also being slightly stretchy, and exceedingly quick to bag out -- even during a brief try-on. The straight leg shape is good. However, on both pairs, the waist is unusually high, especially in back. (I'm 5'11" with a long torso, so to have a waistband hit so high is unexpected.) The blue pair was so unflattering overall -- creating a bizarrely matronly figure / butt shape (even worse than the one which exists) -- that I immediately removed them without taking a photo. They were HORRID!! The black jeans looked decent from the front, if bulky; and because they're black, weren't so awful from the rear view (though they felt stiff and immobile, while somehow being quick to sag). Because the legs had the straight, full-length silhouette I'm seeking, I considered keeping the black pair -- but in addition to their stiff uncomfortableness, the waist gapped *too much* in back -- and with the odd fabrication and low price, aren't worth the trouble of having altered. Everyone's taste and body type differ, and perhaps these jeans will work well for others -- but please see attached photo for the waist-gap situation on me. (My specs: 5'11", approx. 186 pounds, long torso / shorter legs, hourglass figure.)

No front pockets 🙁 I was thrilled to see these come in SHORT! Finally, it is a win for us vertically challenged folks. They hit right at my ankles (I'm 5'2 ", 140 lbs, and built like an apple, thanks to grandma's generous DNA donation). Usually, if something fits my waist, it's baggy in the butt and thighs—but these actually fit in both spots. Miracles do happen. I did knock off a star because—brace yourselves—no front pockets. There is a teeny-tiny "pocketwatch" pocket, which is hilarious because A) who carries a pocket watch, and B) it wouldn't fit one anyway. It is the perfect size for casually hooking my thumb in and giving strangers a friendly wave, though. So I suppose there is a bonus. Maybe a new way to make friends. Anyway, I liked them enough to grab the green pair too. Let's see if they're equally fabulous.

Reliable Levi's, though the Amazon size listing is confusing I’m 5’3”, 118 lbs and ordered 28 reg based on another pair of Skinny Levi’s I have. Based on the confusing chart for ordering, I wasn’t sure what I would end up with. I got a 28/32 that also says 6 Medium ( guess that translates to the “reg”). I would say the fit in this Vintage pair is comfortable, if not a bit roomy, but at least not tight or restrictive. Length is perfect. I ordered color StayPut, and the actual version is not as faded looking as the pic online, but I like what I got just as much if not better. I really like the flare (ready for a change from the skinny). I also ended up with the actual “vintage” version, which has really cute 3 row stitching on back and front pocket. I noticed at least one review pic had the traditional 2 row Levi stitching, so odd that you don’t know what you’re getting from this particular listing. Anyway, I am very satisfied with my purchase, with a very fair price for new Levi’s.

Comfy and Stretchy For reference, I am 5'5", 165 lbs. I am more bottom heavy than top. When I ordered, there were 3 choices of inseams, and I chose the middle one. I almost think I could have gone for petite. I also got the XL based on measurements and weight, and they are a bit too loose for the stretchiness to matter much.These are some very comfortable jeans/leggings. Very stretchy. I could buy groceries, do yoga and cook supper and work in the yard, all in 1 day in these. Easy to get on the floor and play with kids/ fur babies. They are extremely easy to move around in. extremely functional. Stays up, so doesn't stretch off of you when you squat or put things in the pockets. I did not exactly like the fit on me tho. They seemed a little frumpy looking to be a stretchy material. (again, while ordering based on what should be my size.) I am not thrilled of how I look in them. I realize that is my figure, but I believe a closer fit, would have been a bit better. The pics are after washing and putting in dryer. I do not think the fit changed any from the fit before washing. So should be dryer safe, but may lessen the lifespan. I do think they are a good value based on the functionality, comfort, and pockets - even the looseness if that is what you are looking for. So worth the price, especially if there is a coupon. I just do not feel they are flattering

I also got the XL based on measurements and weight, and they are a bit too loose for the stretchiness to matter much.These are some very comfortable jeans/leggings. Very stretchy. I could buy groceries, do yoga and cook supper and work in the yard, all in 1 day in these. Easy to get on the floor and play with kids/ fur babies. They are extremely easy to move around in. extremely functional. Stays up, so doesn't stretch off of you when you squat or put things in the pockets. I did not exactly like the fit on me tho. They seemed a little frumpy looking to be a stretchy material. (again, while ordering based on what should be my size.) I am not thrilled of how I look in them. I realize that is my figure, but I believe a closer fit, would have been a bit better. The pics are after washing and putting in dryer. I do not think the fit changed any from the fit before washing. So should be dryer safe, but may lessen the lifespan. I do think they are a good value based on the functionality, comfort, and pockets - even the looseness if that is what you are looking for. So worth the price, especially if there is a coupon. I just do not feel they are flattering

Very nice quality, seem like they're sized big I think these jeans / yoga pants are very nice quality. They are super stretchy, and while they have the feel of yoga pants they do have the look of jeans. I am a 5-ft 4 in female weighing 120 lb and I ordered a size medium. I should have definitely sized down to the small. Medium is normally the size I would choose in something like this, but I do think that these are sized on the large side. I like that there are flared legs, in traditional jeans pockets on the back, they really do seem like something that you could wear anywhere you would wear jeans. Overall I like them I think the value is decent and I would say I consider them on par with a nice pair of yoga pants in terms of value.

Stretchy and comfortable This pair of pants looks like denim jeans but feels like stretch pants. They slide on and off easily because you don’t have to deal with a button or zipper. The pockets are all real and usable. There are actual belt loops. The dark denim looks nice. I’m 5’9” and the medium size was just long enough. If these were any shorter then they’d be too short, but these are just long enough to work. They’re very comfortable to wear. They are comfy enough to lounge in. This style of pant is great for those that have trouble with zippers or buttons, but still want the look of jeans.

Runs a bit small. 5’6” 200# and an apple. I carry most of my weight in my belly. OK, so now that we got that out of the way, onto the pants. I really like these. I bought them in a size 16 medium because I find that Lee pants always run a little small and since they’re for work. I really did not want them to be snug. I got the black and white thin plaid pattern and I really like it. The rise on these is good. It’s a fairly high-rise, but it’s not super high-rise. The waistband is a bit big on me but I’d rather that then have it dig in. The length is perfect for me, but if you’re any shorter than 5’6” you may want to order the shorter size unless you want your pants really long for heels or something. The back pockets are sewn shut, but you can open them up, and the front pockets are pretty deep. Material has a good thickness and has a bit of stretch to it. Everything seems to be pretty good quality and I love the price at $18. These will be great work pants, they might be a little heavy for the summer, but we’ll have to wait and see. Between the fit the fabric and the price. I definitely recommend these.

Long enough! Yay Insanely comfortable and flattering. I'm over 60 and love the trouser look but want the informal look of jeans without going "boyfriend" and sloppy for a woman my age. These are spot on. I'm 5.7 at 115 with 36.5" hips 27" waist and the small is a comfy fit for me but I could've easily gone Xsml if I was looking for snugger fit. These have a nice detail surprise at the waistband. They're not just a wider band. They're sewn well and kind of like a two tiered double waistband which looks very nice and finished well. At my age having a squared up looking rear view is something to keep an eye out for and these don't do that. Very flattering from the rear view and as close to "heart" shaped as I'm going to get at this age and I'm very very pleased. Lots of stretch but they don't look like stretch jeans. Love these so much I got a pair in white. Usually for a trouser look I either had my pricey linen trousers w/ zip, elastic waist or draw string waist. These can be dressed up or down

This one is definitely wide.

Comfortable and flattering! These jeans are just what I was looking for- they’re roomy in the top half of the pants, and bell at the bottom giving a nice shape with comfort. With certain jeans, you get one or the other… a wide leg looking like a boyfriend jean, or a tight leg with a belled bottom. Feels like I hit the jackpot finding a jean with both. With that being said, the color of the jeans themselves are traditional blue jean color with a lighter wash on the legs. The area above the pockets has the already washed and worn look, but it doesn’t look natural. I find this in a lot of jeans though, and some may like it, I’m hoping it fades over time with washings to look more natural. They’re stretchy, not only in the legs, but also the waist, which is nice! I like a little forgiveness in my jeans… for eating out, and having room for the dessert, busting out the millennial dance moves, and simply being able to sit and breathe in them without wanting to unbutton one or all of the buttons depending on the day. No discomfort sitting in these! lol Really hoping these hold up, the material and stitching seems a reasonable thickness for a stretch jean, and the stitching is intact and straight. I am slightly concerned about the buttons, as I’ve had ones like these before (slightly wiggly, if ya know what I mean?) and the top usually ends up popping off. In the event they do, I wouldn’t have a second thought to replacing them with a higher quality button. For the price- this is a great jean. I can already tell they’re going in my go-to, tried and true rotation of pants. (I am 5 foot 1- 145lbs with an athletic, chunky build if that helps. Also wearing flat shoes.)

Pleasantly Surprised I usually only purchase clothing items on Amazon with a 4.5 rating or higher. These pants had a rating of 4.0 but I wasn’t finding any wide leg, high-rise styles that I thought would flatter my short body. These jeans are slightly tighter near the thighs, which makes a short person less boxy. I’m 5’ 1”, 62 years old, and weigh 134 lbs. I’m wearing a 2 1/2” heel in these photos, and the length is great. Most jeans that aren’t specifically made for petites are way too long, even with a 4” heel. I haven't washed these jeans, but they appear sturdy and the stitching in tact. The material is flexible, and not heavy, like some jeans. I gave these jeans a rating of 4, due the the fact that there is a slight oil like order. I’m hoping it will disappear one laundered.

Nice fit but don’t look like advertised The jeans I received look nothing like what I ordered. The waist is completely different and the color is much lighter than the “blue” I ordered. Disappointed because the waist was the selling point for me- I liked the buttons. It was different than regular jeans. All that being said, I’m still giving this four stars because I did really like the fit. I’m 5’1 so to find jeans that actually fit me length wise is really difficult and these are the perfect length for me in boots. And the fabric is very stretchy and very comfortable.

Great fit and quality I am 5'7" and 165. I have pretty long legs and carry most of my weight in my waist so shopping for pants is frustrating. These are pretty stretch but don't feel cheap. Soft material so if you are looking for very structured stiff jeans, I would skip these. The length was great. I ordered 12 longs and I felt they were very true to size.

Rocking those 90's vibes! Where can I start? As a shorter person 5'3" I have major difficulties finding jeans that don't hang many inches below my feet. Not these gems from Aeropostale who offer lengths in short, regular and tall! That's something few jean companies do but all should. The color is that perfect medium/dark wash blue! And the quality is off the charts! You can tell these jeans will be a staple in your closet for years to come. These midrise jeans bring me back to my youth in the 90's when big (within reason) was in. They are super comfortable and at the sale price of $23 they are a steal! So order a pair or 3. They are super versatile and can be worn with a hoodie and sneakers or a blazer and heels. The one complaint I have is the slight gap in the back of the waist. But as someone with a naturally smaller waist and a bigger booty, this is an issue I frequently encounter. It can usually be helped with a good quality belt. All in all a great pair of jeans from a great company!

Pretty nice jeans I felt compelled to write a review because there were none in my size range. I usually wear a 6R at 5'7" 140ish lbs, which is what I ordered. Something I noticed before ordering was that the measurements on the size chart for sizes 6 and 8 were the same. What? So how do I know what size to get? I usually get a 6, but knowing this brand is usually for younger people it felt like a risk. As you can see from the photos, it's a really snug 6. I did try these on at the end of the day when (for me) pants are tightest because of gravity 😂 I think that after some wear and washes, they will get softer. The material is a bit rougher than I usually like, with less give, but you get what you pay for. They will be good for cool weather. I also like the wash, I don't like huge lines on my hips and these aren't too in-your-face. (I would have opted for a darker wash but it wasn't available in my size.) So overall pretty good, I'll throw them in the rotation and pair them with a thrift store flannel to keep the dream of the 90s alive 😎

Might need more colors! Love these! I usually wear an 8L but ordered a 10L after reading reviews that they feel small. I think it’s because it’s not the stretchy denim most jeans are made out of lately. I love the structured waist that doesn’t roll at the button. I have a 34” inseam and these are perfect.

Fantastic! Comfortable real demin looking jeggings! Heathyoga are MY GO TO yoga pants. They are the best fitting most comfortable yoga pants I've ever found and I own an entire collection. They are tight in the legs down to the ankles for the full length ones and offer great tummy control and good compression without feeling restrictive or uncomfortable. They are so soft against my skin and hold up well wash after wash by washing in cold water and hanging to dry. The dark colors don't fade. The pockets are perfectly sized and placed. I have a horrible time finding jeans that fit. I have extremely thin legs and not much of a butt. I like slim cut tight jeans but the legs and calves are always too big and the hip and butt area always sag. I don't have much in the way of curves. My measurements are 41.33. 44. When I came across these jeggings I was super excited! I read the reviews and immediately order a pair in my regular size large for this brand. I'm absolutely thrilled with them! The length is perfect for me at 5'6". They fit to my super skinny legs and are nice and snug on my hips and thighs while still feeling just as comfortable as my yoga pants. They do look like actual denim which is another big plus. My only negative comment is the sizing seems to run a tad bit larger than their tummy control yoga pants so there's not much tummy control in these. I would suggest ordering down one size from your regular heathyoga pants size if you want a snugger fit. I will definitely be ordering these in multiple colors and lengths, the cropped look quite cute too, but I will be ordering a medium for more compression and tummy control. I will still wear the large pair I bought as they do fit great but will probably save wearing them for days I feel bloated and don't want anything super tight on my tummy. I highly recommend giving these a shot. I'm so glad I did.

They are tight in the legs down to the ankles for the full length ones and offer great tummy control and good compression without feeling restrictive or uncomfortable. They are so soft against my skin and hold up well wash after wash by washing in cold water and hanging to dry. The dark colors don't fade. The pockets are perfectly sized and placed. I have a horrible time finding jeans that fit. I have extremely thin legs and not much of a butt. I like slim cut tight jeans but the legs and calves are always too big and the hip and butt area always sag. I don't have much in the way of curves. My measurements are 41.33. 44. When I came across these jeggings I was super excited! I read the reviews and immediately order a pair in my regular size large for this brand. I'm absolutely thrilled with them! The length is perfect for me at 5'6". They fit to my super skinny legs and are nice and snug on my hips and thighs while still feeling just as comfortable as my yoga pants. They do look like actual denim which is another big plus. My only negative comment is the sizing seems to run a tad bit larger than their tummy control yoga pants so there's not much tummy control in these. I would suggest ordering down one size from your regular heathyoga pants size if you want a snugger fit. I will definitely be ordering these in multiple colors and lengths, the cropped look quite cute too, but I will be ordering a medium for more compression and tummy control. I will still wear the large pair I bought as they do fit great but will probably save wearing them for days I feel bloated and don't want anything super tight on my tummy. I highly recommend giving these a shot. I'm so glad I did.

Petite Curvy Girl Approved! These are fantastic!! The material is super stretchy, moderately soft and lightweight, but substantial enough to smooth the lines a bit. My measurements: waist 26", hip 37", 116 lbs, 5'4" tall. Size Small, 31" length fits perfectly. I do still have a tiny amount of gaping at the back waist with these, but it's nothing major. The pockets are generous and the stitching looks pretty good. I love that they don't have those cheap rivets that come apart quickly. Best of all they are suuuuper comfy! Works well for me!

Updated review UPDATE: The company reached out to me after my review and offered to send me a new pair of jeans. They arrived quickly and this pair appears perfect, although I haven't washed them yet. And kudos to IUGA for caring about their customers. Because of this, I've changed my 3 star rating to a 4. I LOVED the feel of these jean jeggings! They were very comfortable and looked very cute. For reference, I'm 5'4", 140 pounds, usually wear a size 10 and the mediums in these fit very well. However, after just one wear and wash they got a pill in them. It will be a hole in no time. Also, one of the hems on the bottom of the legs wouldn't stay flat. That in itself wasn't a deal breaker for me, but the hole definitely is. The fabric just isn't great quality. I might pay $15 or $20 for these, but not $40.

I'm 5'2" 110lbs. These jeans are very soft and the perfect length. I like how they're tight in the waist so I don't have the awkward gap from having a booty. While the pants look smooth in a still photograph, the front pockets bunch and gather when I walk giving me an unflattering front. Perhaps the pockets can be cut out and sewn up, but if I'm paying this much for pants, I don't want to make the effort of altering them.

The 1st pair was great, others are not consistent I ordered 1 pair of these to start and thought they were great! They fit true to size and felt very comfortable and stretchy (almost like a jeggings material/feel)! Although I don't normally wear jeans in the "long" length, I ordered it in these since the regular length was listed as an inch or so shorter than most regular length jeans. I am 5'5". The long length on the first pair seemed great. They were a little long, but I would rather that and make a small rolled cuff at the bottom than too short. Because I liked the first pair so much that I just got, I ordered 2 more. None of them are consistent. They are all made of different blends of material and have a different texture. The sizing and styles are not consistent. Each pair is a different length. The design on the back pockets of each is different. It does show this in the product photos, but I didn't expect the blends/textures and lengths to also be different. While each pair does fit (some longer than others), the fits do vary. The front pockets are also different. Two of the pairs have what I would consider "normal" front pockets for women's jeans. The 3rd pair has pockets less than 2 inches deep that they're basically pointless! I love the 1st pair I got but will likely send the other 2 back. Just don't expect them to be consistent.

Soft and great fit This is my first time ordering from this brand. I’m 5’6” and 127 lbs. I usually like my jeans a little lengthy so that they bunch at the ankles. I normally buy a 30 inch inseam jean to get that look, but since regulars were 29 inches in this brand, I went with regulars, not long, fearing they might be too long. These come just below my ankle, so I will most likely wear them with booties versus tennis shoes. I will have to see if a pair in long is too long or not. The fit is great, the fabric is soft, and I like the rise on the jeans, as I have a very small belly button to crotch length. I don’t like my jeans to bunch up in the crotch when I sit down, and these do not - yay! The lengths were all consistent between the three pairs. I lined them up by the back of the waistband, and the lengths were exactly the same as you can see in my picture. May need to use a belt with them, as I had a little extra room in the waist — again that’s my narrow hip issue. The fabric is very soft and comfortable, maybe just a tad thinner than other stretch jeans I have owned. I really love them, so hopefully they hold up during regular washing and use. I am either a women’s 2 or 4 in other brands, depending on my winter/summer weight. I could see going down to a size 3 in these if I lose my usual 7-10 lbs come summer. I love these and overall I am a happy camper!

Soft & Stretchy Jeans These jeans are super soft, comfortable and flattering! They have a lot of stretch all over, making these jeans ideal for any occasion. They hug my curves, accentuate my butt, prevent love handles, and have a slim fit all throughout. The waist sits right below/at my belly button, which is good because I prefer a mid-rise (super high rise jeans on someone my height/with a short torso isn't great). Because the waist is extra stretchy, I can tuck a shirt in without it feeling tight. I can be bloated as hell and still feel comfortable! The one main negative is the crotch area. When I sit down, the fabric gathers, hardens and points outward in an awkward way (see photo). I absolutely despise the way it looks, but these jeans are so awesome in every other way that I can overlook it. The length is just about perfect on me, which is rare! The shorter inseam is ideal for those 5'-5'3". Because these jeans can expand so much, I can most likely afford to size down. I do have to keep pulling the back up after sitting/lying down. There's plenty of extra room in the waist area so maybe I should've sized down. I usually wear a size 2 Petite in pants and these feel roomier. If you're not sure between two sizes, I would size down, unless you want a roomy waist. Overall, for under $35, these are really nice jeans, and I would get them in other colors/styles. The fabric is lightweight so they're not ideal for wearing in really cold weather. But they work for the hot weather here. They look good, they feel good, and they don't break the bank. What more could you ask for?

Difference between pure and non-pure versions I ordered these jeans in a size 9 in both Amal and Amal Pure colors. I already ordered the jeans in a size 9 in the color Lake and loved how soft the material is. I wanted a lighter color and chose the two Amal colors to compare which one I like better. Both of them have too much fading for my liking so I returned both. Also, the stitching is a bit off and is more noticeable on these jeans than on the Lake pair. The Amal color has the wallflower logo on the back and stitched within while the Amal Pure does not. The Amal Pure has a plain black tag within and less decorative back pockets. Also, the Amal Pure has tiny front pockets while the Amal has more normal sized front pockets that you can actually fit your hands deeper inside. The Amal color jeans are slightly lighter than the Amal Pure. In my pictures with them side by side, Amal is on the left and Amal Pure is on the right. In terms of fit, I am 5'4", 140ish lbs, 38" hips, 29" waist. I usually wear a size 7 in juniors and a 4 or 6 in women's and the size 7 in these jeans was a tad too tight so I kept the 9 in Lake. I love how soft these are but they do slide down when walking around extensively or when getting up from sitting. They are more like leggings than jeans. UPDATE: I wrote my original review in Fall 2018. It is now July 2020 and Wallflower instasoft jeans are the only jeans I wear since they are soft and stretchy and don't bother my sensitive skin. I still have my original Lake and they are a little less dark but still in great shape. I also have and wear regularly Shannon and Hayden all in a size 9. The Lake seems a little tighter than the other two but all are softer than normal jeans. I always wear these with a belt since they are super stretchy. Size up I normally wear a 9 if stretchy. From reviews I ordered a 11 long because wanted them to go lil past my ankles. Size 11 was too tight so ordered 13 L and fit perfectly. If help im 5'6, hips 43, waist 30, weight 162. Only downside is you can see the stitching if you look closely.

My new favorite jeans! I'm obsessed with these jeans! Bought in the 2 lighter colors. Bought a size 3. I normally wear a size 4. 5'2" and 125lbs. I have a very flat butt...I'm talking like a pancake, and these jeans made me feel like I had some lift to it. I tried them on for my husband and kids and they all said these were the best jeans I own. I love the flare and the distressed look. They have some stretch to them, but not to the point where they get loose feeling or baggy. They hold their shape well. I'd highly recommend getting these jeans!

Super Cute Jeans! Retro Bells with Style, Stretch and Comfort Oh my so these jeans are so cute! Soft and comfortable with good stretch, deep pockets front and rear. The bells give a cool throw back vibe but not over the top and the buckle waist is a nice added touch. The waistline is not too low or high very comfortable. The denim blue with fading great color. I am 5'2" 125 lbs the length was a tad long but can still wear them with sneakers a heel pulled them up even better. Never bought jeans with the sizing S, M or L? but guessed and the Med was good for me. If these had some more lift to the rear and a bit more in the tummy control area would be five star all day long. Just feel my behind looked flat with the pocket placement and size. The overall quality is great zipper buttons and stitching all very nice. The price is right for a good pair of jeans that are cute, fashionable and functional.

For my plus size girls 💖 So this is for my ladies who are 220lbs-225lbs with an apron belly! I didn’t find many reviews with my body type on here so I decided to bite the bullet and try them myself! First of all OMG these are super comfy, long enough, and stretchy. They’re not SUPER stretchy but they’re breathable and I can sit in them and not suffocate. THEY HAVE DEEP USABLE POCKETS ON THE FRONT AND BACK. The tummy control? Oh yes girl.. it’s there and I’m here for it. The flare of these beauties are AMAZING. Some of you may remember how flare jeans were in the early 2000s and may even miss them.. well these fit that category, in my opinion! They seem like high quality fabric and I didn’t notice any strong smells or weird colorations when I got them out of the package. In fact, they smell just like how Mudd Jeans smelt at Goodys back in the day. 😂🥲 (ifykyk) These are definitely worth the price in my opinion. DON’T HESITATE, BUY NOW!!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Now she tells me she wants to add “just one more pair” to her collection. Quick Overview: These Avamo high-waisted pull-on bell bottoms are exactly what my wife wanted. They’re soft, stretchy, and, according to her, both work-appropriate and weekend-approved. They look like jeans, feel like lounge pants, and fit like they were designed by someone who actually understands curves. Bonus points: they’ve been in near-constant rotation, which tells me all I need to know. Pros: • Fit true to size and hug in the right places without pinching. • Soft, stretchy fabric that still looks like denim. • High waist is flattering and stays put, no rolling down. • Functional pockets (a rarity in my wife’s pants). • Easy pull-on style means no wrestling with buttons or zippers. • My wife says they make her legs look longer… and when you are 5’2” you need all the height advantages you can get. (and I agree). Cons: • None worth mentioning. My Take: My wife loves these jeans so much that I kept missing my chance to take a review photo. They were always either at work with her or in the laundry. That alone tells you the story. They do exactly what the product claims: comfortable, flattering, and stylish without the denim stiffness. If disco or Navy dungarees ever make a full comeback, she’s already dressed for it. Tips: • Pair with wedges or boots if you want the full leg-lengthening effect. • Wash inside out to preserve the dark color. 📌 Reviewer Note I aim to keep my reviews straightforward and add humor when I can. If this one feels a little “sterile,” that’s just me keeping safe from the robots. Don’t worry, I don’t sugarcoat. (My scale: 1 = No thanks, 5 = Buy it again in a heartbeat.)

Best jeans ever for my body type Legit my favorite jeans all around. I’ve brought just about every color of 726 flares high rise and it fits like a glove. I get compliments all the time on these jeans. I work in a warehouse, so I do a tremendous amount of moving about, lifting, squatting, twisting etc. these jeans are insanely comfortable, they don’t give you that saggy look even after a few days of wear. I like my jeans snug and tight. These jeans can go from warehouse work to an elevated and more elegant look with the right pair of heels. I tend to wear cowgirl boots a lot so these are perfect to cover a boot, even a high one. I personally wait for sales and then buy the jeans and posses them in so many colors. The waist is my favorite part cuz it actually hugs my waist instead of leaving a huge gap. Makes the bum look incredible too. I’m 5’5 and fluctuate in weight about 10 lbs so I really like tight jeans, with a small waist and small hips. These jeans make me look like a woman instead of making me look like I swim in them. I always buy size 24 by 32. I only use a belt for fashion rather than need. They can easily be worn without a belt and hold their integrity.

Cute, but returned I've long been wanting to try a pair of jeans that has the front split hem. I know they come in various styles from skinny to boot cut to flare, and I have no idea which style I will like. I was hoping it would be these, but I decided I need a different style. I feel since these are a flare style split hem, the slit opens up too much for my liking. Or maybe it's just that the slit should be shorter to give me the desired look that I'm going for. I actually fell in love with these upon initially trying them on. They are comfortable and I really like the back pockets. I have a flat butt and it's not often I find a pair of pants that are at least somewhat flattering on me, but I felt like these were fairly flattering and I liked that aspect. Unfortunately, I feel like the overall appearance of these jeans just wasn't the most flattering for my body type. These are high-rise, which I don't mind. They come just to the top of my belly button. But I felt like they were a little too short. I thought that having a 32" inseam, the length would be fine, but I would have actually preferred them to be at least an inch or two longer. These are nice for wearing with flat sneakers, I guess, but not with boots. Between the high-rise waist and the slightly too short inseam, they just look kind of funny to me. Also gives me the appearance of having short legs or just overall looking kind of short and squat. I didn't really like that. I'm 5'7", so I'm not really short. I thought I might be able to adjust the appearance by using safety pins to hold the slit together. I thought the safety pins actually looked cute and would hopefully allow me to keep the jeans. But the more I tried them on, the less I liked them. I'm also not a big fan of medium blue jeans. I feel like they look good on a lot of other people, but they just don't go with my wardrobe. I got my normal size 27 and they fit great. They are a true 32" inseam. The waist is 13" and the rise is 10.5". The ankle is about 10" wide and the slit is 9" long. PLEASE NOTE: the pictures I took are with the slit held together with a safety pin unless otherwise noted.

SIZE CHART IS NOT ACCURATE FOR LENGTH I’d give these 5 stars if I was given the right size in length for what I ordered. However, they fit like a glove and really flatter my body type. For reference I’m about 5’1, 136lb, with 29-30 inch waist. Im also a mom and these hide my small pooch beautifully. I am curvy so they are snug but not tight, which is what I wanted. I usually aim for jeans with 28” inseam if applicable, and with these I was given the option to get short and the size chart also mentions that 28 is the short, but upon doing my research, this is not true. On the Levi’s website itself for the 726 Flare, the short sits at 30 inches. I was going to return them for the right length but I would have been sent the same 30” inseam length. I will keep them and another pair that I got in black but will have to get them tailored. Size chart is not accurate in length for these specific 726. If you were to order something that was advertised as “ankle length” then it would be accurate, but for these 726 the short is 30”. Hopefully people see my review to be able to make the right decision about these.

On the Levi’s website itself for the 726 Flare, the short sits at 30 inches. I was going to return them for the right length but I would have been sent the same 30” inseam length. I will keep them and another pair that I got in black but will have to get them tailored. Size chart is not accurate in length for these specific 726. If you were to order something that was advertised as “ankle length” then it would be accurate, but for these 726 the short is 30”. Hopefully people see my review to be able to make the right decision about these.

Pleasantly surprised! I have always wanted to try on a pair of Levi’s and I’m so glad that I did! They are true to size for me. I ordered a 29 short, but I knew they would be too long because I’m only 5ft and 30 inches is not short enough for me, so as usual I will be taking 2 1/2 inches off the bottom. I like the flare because they are not too much of a flare, they are like a boot cut flare. They feel stretchy and comfortable which is always a plus! If I can find the same exact pair in a different color, I will purchase them for sure. The price is right! I took one star off only because I wish they would offer x-short like American Eagle does. Petite used to be 5’2 and under and now it’s 5’4. I guess short women are a dying breed lol Anyway for reference I am 5ft 128 lbs., I usually order a size 8 and the 29 fits great! I will add pictures. In the photos I folded the bottom under 21/2 inches.

THE PERFECT Corduroys! These wine color corduroys are exactly what I’ve been looking for! I own multiple pairs of Levi’s skinny style jeans, so I ordered the same size as the rest of my Levi’s, size 31. For the Regular length the size chart lists 30-32 length. I was super excited when I pulled these out of the package and saw that they are 32s. I’m 5’7, 175 lb and size 31W/32L fits perfectly and the length allows me to wear heels with these pants. The fabric is soft and somewhat stretchy, it is medium weight - not thin that feels cheap, but also not overly heavy. I love the style and the look of these pants, great addition to my wardrobe as I will be able to dress them up for the office and dress them down for casual wear. I feel like finding good quality corduroys that fit well is extremely hard (or at least I have went through several purchases and returns) that’s why I feel like the price is justified.

A little on the long side… These are crazy comfortable!! Like wearing cozy joggers but look wonderful on! What I loved: *The tiny pocket fit my entire earbud case, or my entire house key ring! *The side strip makes my legs look more streamlined ( lol). *Seriously comfortable!!! *The color was exact to the photos provided. *The sizing is accurate. Cons: *If you have a bigger bottom the small back pockets placement enhances that (you probably already knew this but just in case you didn’t. *If you’re under 5’3 these will be way too long for you. They are probably too long for me but I’ll wear them with higher shoes and hope for the best.😒 *The tag on the back is an ugly pleather so I will be seam ripping it off. Wish I’d noticed that but I would still have got these because they’re wonderful looking on. *The top front part does not hide, tuck or flatter a round tummy so keep that in mind-I’m just going to be wearing longer shirts to cover that.🫤😊 *I also plan on wearing a slightly longer shirt to cover those pockets. I hope my review helps you make the best choice for your needs! (:

Beautiful Wide Leg Pants for Curvy Petite The wide leg jeans in brown are very pretty. The fabric is soft and has a little bit of stretch. The pants had a little bit of smell originally, and it went away with the first wash. I am 5'2" tall, 130lbs, 29" waist, 41" hips and size 12 fits well from the waist, hips and they are a little long on me but wearing them with boots they elongate my legs. If I wear the pants as high rise the pants are a little big from the waist, but if I wear them as a mid-rise then the waist fits great. The pants form some lines across the front, but they don't bother me, and I like the way they look. The pants are comfortable, and they move with my body. I like the thickness of the fabric and how the fabric falls on my body with the wide leg. I wash them using a cold, normal cycle and I dry them using low heat to avoid shrinking. I steam them to remove wrinkles. I enjoy wearing them with black tops and black boots for a nice contrast.

Nice. Update: they relax over time Size 8. 5'6" 155lb, (being modest) I have a tiny waist, broad hips, moderate butt. I carry my weight in muscular upper body, and dense lower body these days. I usually wear pretty curvy jeans, because the waste usually gaps in EVERYTHING I own. I wear a belt with ALL my jeans, because of the gap. These jeans fit well, enough that I might consider a size 6 next, with the expectation of a tighter fit. Not perfect fit in the waist, but they probably are a decent "curvy" fit. I liked Levi 529, this is a close next best. I wear a belt so I dont have to keep hiking jeans up, but find I did pull these up several times a day. The legs are mostly straight through the thigh, a tiny bit narrower at the knee, but not much taper after that. I have solid thighs, and large calves (boot buying is never fun) and own slim ankles... these jeans do not cling, but wearing a neoprene knee brace underneath leaves no spare room. I might have considered a 6, but was hoping for space for the brace. The fabric feels like traditional modern denim, not 1920s canvas-denim, and not leggings style stretch-denim...., just modern denim with a tiny bit of stretch... which I hoped meant 2-3 days wear, without looking /feeling worn out. But they just seem to relax over the first day, and bummer, feel like "5th day wear" on the second day... so to me, that's 1 day wear jeans. Color was good. Update: ive had them a few months, they RELAX and dont gently hug curves like they used to. The knees are stretched out. The fabric fibers are starting to give in the knees and thighs giving them a bit of a fuzzy "needs to shave" look. Still confortable as my "slightly looser" pair of jeans, but I have to belt them. Photos attached. I wash/dry them in the machine like everything else, using cold/warm water and timed dry, w name brand soap and dryer sheets... but they dont hold up like my other brand jeans of similar value.

I have solid thighs, and large calves (boot buying is never fun) and own slim ankles... these jeans do not cling, but wearing a neoprene knee brace underneath leaves no spare room. I might have considered a 6, but was hoping for space for the brace. The fabric feels like traditional modern denim, not 1920s canvas-denim, and not leggings style stretch-denim...., just modern denim with a tiny bit of stretch... which I hoped meant 2-3 days wear, without looking /feeling worn out. But they just seem to relax over the first day, and bummer, feel like "5th day wear" on the second day... so to me, that's 1 day wear jeans. Color was good. Update: ive had them a few months, they RELAX and dont gently hug curves like they used to. The knees are stretched out. The fabric fibers are starting to give in the knees and thighs giving them a bit of a fuzzy "needs to shave" look. Still confortable as my "slightly looser" pair of jeans, but I have to belt them. Photos attached. I wash/dry them in the machine like everything else, using cold/warm water and timed dry, w name brand soap and dryer sheets... but they dont hold up like my other brand jeans of similar value.

I Like These Pants I've been looking for pants like these for quite awhile. I wanted lightweight pull-on pants with pockets big enough for a cell phone that I can wear during the summer months. For me, the hardest part was determining the right size. The online size chart indicated that I should wear a size 18P, which is larger than anything I own. Nevertheless, I bought a pair and they turned out to be too big. I knew as soon as I tried them on, though, that I wanted one in every color. So, I ordered two more pairs in both a 16P and a 14P. The 16P turned out to be the best fit, even though it is still a little loose; but I definitely didn't want pants that were too tight. These are good every-day pants for a retired woman in her 70's. I am 5'2" and weigh about 160. I had no problems with making multiple returns.

Super comfortable but beware of snags These are as comfy as wearing sweatpants! The wide waistband is comfortable and doesn’t dig in. Love that it has usable pockets too. The length is fine for me (5’4”) if I wear low profile sneakers. I deducted a star because the material snags SO easily that I don’t see them lasting that long. I’ve included a pic of some of the snags that have happened after wearing the pants less than 5 times. They occurred when… 1) my wedding ring, which has a smooth standard shared prong setting for the stones, brushed against the material when I rubbed my leg with my hand. 2) my dog put her paw on my leg to get my attention (her nails are trimmed) Those things don’t cause snags in any of my other pants. Maybe the darker color options don’t show snags as much.

Be Prepared...You are going to be asked, "Where did you get your pants?" Let's just start out by acknowledging the amazing colors of these jeans! Buy the jeans you won't regret it!!!

Adorable. Comfortable. Affordable. It's a gamble purchasing clothes online. But with the easy returns at Amazon, I no longer fear being stuck with yet another piece of clothing I don't wear. While these took a long time to get here, when they eventually arrived, they were packaged well for travel from China. These jeans are just as pictured and true to color. I could have gotten away with a small, but I was scared and got the medium, figuring I could use a belt to keep my britches up if these were too big. They look identical to FP, at a fraction of the cost. Soft, comfortable and have a broken in feeling right out of the air tight plastic bag. I've washed them, but just casually tossed them in with my regular stuff and washed on cold. Hung to dry. Came out great. I'm probably too old to be wearing trendy fashions, but I don't care, I loved the bandana pattern. Not so sure about the whole weird barrel leg deal...but these are comfy, and there are a lot of possibilities for styling. Happy with purchase. I usually wear a 4, I got a Medium. I probably could have got the Small and been ok, there is a good deal of stretch to these. I have pretty long legs for my 5'5" frame, and these hit just above my ankle... making them look intentionally cropped, and not accidental floods.

It probably would o that if you have a very flat tummy.All in all, I love these pants and I’ll be ordering more.

If you're tall, don't bother. Now I remember why they've just about gone out of business. If you're going by the product specs, don't bother. Listed as five pocket, but the three front pockets are fake/decoys. I'm 5'9" so I ordered a 29 Long. Specifications for regular are a 29 inch inseam, and "super skinny" leg. I like my jeans to fit tight around the ankle, like leggings, and sit below the ankle at the top of my foot. These arrived, the fit is forgivable dispite no front pockets and the rear pockets very small, but the inseam is only 28". On top of that, the ankles are more like a boot cut than skinny, much less super skinny, flapping above the ankles like high waters. I guess, you get what you pay for!

Yes, Buy Them. For someone who has struggled with jeans for literal years, these are almost perfect. I don’t like high waisted jeans and if I ever find jeans that aren’t super high waisted, they’ll be too long even for petite sizes. These fit PERFECTLY, down to the ankle. I’m 5ft (maybe 4’11 idk) but if you’re short, get these.

Too short, good fit though I got these in 27 petite. For reference, I’m 5’0” and 107 lb. These pants were supposed to have a 29” inseam, according to the size chart, and they are much shorter than that, as you can see in this photo. The fit is otherwise comfy and fine though, so I’m going to try to exchange for regular inseam.

Sizing is WAY off! I have a 33" waist and need a 31" inseam so I ordered a 32 Regular based on the size chart provided. What I received was marked 14/32 but it had a 37" waist and 26" inseam. I was sent a replacement incase it had just been mislabeled. The replacement was also 37"x26" so I returned them both. I believe that the inseam listed in the size chart is actually the total length from waist to hem not the actual inseam. (The review I originally posted was rejected so this is my rewrite.)

I believe that the inseam listed in the size chart is actually the total length from waist to hem not the actual inseam. (The review I originally posted was rejected so this is my rewrite.)

Good but not for me These were true to size and the fabric was really nice, I got the 27 length and it hit me two fingers above my ankle (I’m 5’0) so that may be short for some people but it didn’t bother me I took off a star because these jeans particular mid rise fit visually cuts me in half in an unflattering way so I had to return them but still wanted people to know that if none of these are dealbreakers for you you should get them.

Love them Nice quality medium weight denim with a stretch. I’m 5’3” 130lbs , shaped like a rectangle according to my scale app (flattering, I know 🫤)l got a sz 27 & fits me perfect. Not too tight, a little loose in the leg like a boyfriend cut should be.

Decent fit, a bit big I ordered a 29 (size 8) as I am 5’5 and 152 lbs. they fit well in hips but the waist is a bit loose, I will be wearing a belt so they don’t go down when I squat or bend. I bet I could have sized down but shipping will take 1-2 months (?) so I’ll just keep these. Nice stretch, which would probably also allow for sizing down.

Best Jeans Ever These are the perfect casual pair of jeans! I’m only 5’2 so finding the right ankle length is difficult, but they hit just right. Soft with just the right amount of stretch. The first pair I ordered were too big, definitely run big so size down. The picture doesn’t do them justice.

Love at first try on The jeans are not as light-colored as I had expected, but I put them on and fell in love! They fit just the way I'd hoped - fitted to the knee then flared out. Fits like a damn glove! I usually wear 10, and the 9 fits wonderfully. I'm 5'6" and 150. Order them!

If you only buy one pair of jeans in your life... THESE are them!!!! Most jeans give me muffin top, sag at the butt, slouch down with my active lifestyle. Eventually I have to wear a belt and my jeans look sad and worn after a few washes. Instantly after putting these on, I just knew. It was heaven! They somehow made my lower stomach/hip area feel snug and controlled, yet No muffin top. I practiced squatting around in them, did a few jumping jacks, etc. There was no shift or need for adjustment. I was amazed. Truly, I did not want to take them off. Felt like second skin but durable. I don't know what kind of magic is in these, but keep it coming! If I wash these a few times and they fall apart, I'll revise my review. But after raving to family about these and those who know about Democracy already, I have a hunch they'll stand the test of time. Okay, they're a little pricy, so start off with a pair that are on a good discount. So far, I can say I'd spend the retail price for the luxury of a good, flattering pant. Photo: I'm 5'5" 140 lbs - size 6 regular

Comfortable waistband but too large for me everywhere else. I was so excited to find Democracy Jeans because I typically have trouble finding jeans that fit my waist comfortably. (I’m 5’7 and 140 pounds. I’d describe my body type as slim but with excess weight in the belly area.) I usually wear a 6, but these jeans were so stretchy that the 6 was too large everywhere so I exchanged for a 4. The 4 fits so nicely at the waist and really hides my stomach! Sitting down is comfortable and I don’t have a bright red waistband line across my stomach when I take these off! But the fit in the legs and butt is too large (definitely doesn’t give me the promised lift) and there are little folds of excess material everywhere. Also, the fly won’t stay up to save my life! Every time I stand up, the barn door is WIDE open. Overall, these jeans deliver comfort… and I love that they exist to accommodate more body types! But I don’t think mine is one of them :(

So, so comfortable!! These are probably my favorite pair of jeans I have ever bought. So, so comfortable and flattering. The prices vary wildly depending on the wash. The dark indigo I purchased were $35 and 100% worth it. Other washes go up to $75. I also got them in a grey color. They fit like a glove, and the waist sits perfectly on me, not too high, not too low. I am 5'3" and 135 lbs and I bought a size 4. I can move and bend over and they stretch and move with me....but not so much they get saggy. They continue to hug me throughout the day without bagging or bunching or riding up or down. Pretty sure I will be purchasing these in other washes soon!

BEST JEANS I'VE EVER HAD These are AMAZING! I'm 5'6" 192 lbs, I have a big belly & love handles. I chose 12 tall and it's the perfect length because I like my jeans long. Your business doesn't pop out when you bend over. I have big love handles so I have a little bulge on the sides but I have this in all mid rise jeans. The best part is that they're COMFORTABLE! Like actually comfortable!! I'm gonna live in these. I feel like I could do a cartwheel in them... Ok I just did and nothing came out! lol Buy them!

Nice fit These were recommended to my by a friend who is short and curvy (10S). I got the 6S and am very pleased. I dont have the biggest butt, but being 5'2" and 135, it is difficult to get butt pockets on my actual butt! These are on there. The pic isnt very good, but I really like the curved cut and smaller pockets. The give on the jean is stronger than most jeggings Ive tried, which makes them feel more formal than stretch pants. has a true button/zipper and all 4 pockets are real. This dark wash is definitely fancier looking and will work very nicely for going out. I could have squeezed into one size smaller and had lobbage/been uncomfortable, so I am glad I got this size (fingers crossed they dont stretch out). I was reticent regarding the price, so hopefully these hold up for a long time.

Luv this brand Democracy is a great fitting jean have worn for a few years. I usually find them at Marshalls/TJ Maxx for around $30-35. and buy when I can. At $37. these were a great find too. Major storea sell for $60 +. I purchased the Indigo and they are not as seen in the first few photos - more like the last 2 pictures (threading is much lighter not bright gold). I'm 5'6 and 130 and size 4 fits perfectly. I think there is a bit of stretch that will happen while wearing too. If you're looking for a dark denim with comfort give them a try. The elastic in the waist is a bonus. Very similar to the brand JAG - JAG sizes same and material is a bit thicker but has same elastic in waist.

Comfy, cute, and mom of 2 under 2 approved :) UPDATE! As of 10/21. GO WITH YOUR ORIGINAL SIZE I returned for a size 28. They fit MUCH better. Length is the same. But taking an inch off for better and more comfortable on the hips and waist. Not as big of a gap on the back. I kinda felt like I was forcing myself to be happy with the “baggy look” in the 29. I only got the 29 because that’s what Amazon suggested. However, the 28 and a size 8 is my true size in jeans. So I would say go with your original size! They are still that baggy relaxed fit, but more complimenting in your true size. Squat and sit down tested. Still comfy! (FYI, pics below are from the size 29, not the updated 28). The length was perfect for me. I’m 5’4 and about 127 to 129lbs. I usually wear a size 8 or 28 in jeans but I went with a size 29. I contemplated if I should have sized down to my normal size or not, but based on their chart saying size 28 is a 6/7 in jeans and how they don’t have much stretch, I’m afraid it would feel too tight near the crotch when I sit or squat down to pick up my kids, or that the length would be too short if I sized down. I only marked it one star down because I haven’t tried washing it yet, and maybe the fit will be more perfect fit after I dry it, and it shrinks a little, because I personally would like it to fit slightly more. I think it’s a good value for the quality of the jeans. I don’t really spend more than $50 on jeans anyway, so this was one of my more expensive ones I bought that will hopefully last many years to come. Coming from a new Levi’s jeans buyer this year, I’ve found that these jeans are a bit tricky with sizing because their jeans don’t have much stretch. So depending on your body, where you fill out more (hips, butt, thighs, height) the fit drastically varies. Material feels thick and durable, not much stretch. With that being said, I find the relaxed fit very comfortable as a mom of 2 under 2, and now days I don’t want anything constricting me. I usually opt for a high waist after my 2 c-sections I had for emotional support lol, but these mid rise jeans hung on just right, while covering that little mommy pooch hehe. My booty has shrunk after I gave birth, but I still find the jeans to be flattering in that baggy, “I don’t care” but looks cute look, without it sagging on the crotch. I think this could be dressed up with a cute top, heels, and accessories as well.

New Favorite Jeans! I absolutely love these jeans! The fit is that perfect in-between relaxed without looking baggy, and super flattering through the waist and hips. The denim feels like real quality: sturdy, not too stretchy, and they hold their shape all day. The knee distressing is cute and trendy without being over the top. I’m 5’7” and the length hits just right with sandals or sneakers. They’re comfy enough for running around with the kids but still stylish for going out. I’ve already gotten so many compliments! If you’re looking for that effortless, lived-in denim that still makes you feel put together… these are the ones.

good quality! These are perfect. I’m 5'2" and 117 lbs. I got the black ones and I love them. I prefer a lower rise, so keep in mind that this jean is mid-waist. They’re not stretchy. They look great, but they’re not the most comfortable because the denim is thick and high quality.

Happy with these jeans! I am a regular Levi’s wearer. I am 5ft 8, weigh 145 pounds. I prefer a higher rise, and in a rib cage I wear a 27, in a wedgie or anything lower I wear 28. I bought these in a 27 and I think that was the right choice. The rise is a little lower than I would prefer, but I still really like how these jeans fit. I have a small amount of gapping at the waistband, but I wouldn’t size down because then they would become too tight on my butt and upper thighs. I also think the length is pretty perfect, which feels lucky. I often look like I’m wearing high waters when I don’t get to select a length. The material feels like what I would expect from this price range of Levi’s, meaning medium weight, with what feels like pretty minimal stretch. They certainly aren’t stiff and thick like the real deal Levi’s.

Jeans that fit! Finally a pair of jeans that don't create a muffin top or cut me in half at the waist! I've always loved Lee jeans and these fit my 72 year old body great. Thus grandma isn't ready for pull on jeans yet, lol. There is just enough stretch in the fabric to be comfortable but not saggy after wearing for a few hours. They sit right at my navel, which I like in my jeans. Plenty long enough too. And I really like the lighter color denim.

Good Deep Color These jeans are made well from thick & quality feeling fabric and have a little bit of give to them. Since there is only one length to them though, they are several inches too long for my 5'2" self so I've got to get them hemmed. I do think the stitching around the button hole is not very secure and seems like it could come undone after several washes. The color is a deep dark blue and they look nice overall for less than $30 if the length is good for you.

These are just a bit long for my short stature but with shoes or boots on they will be just fine. Overall these are nice for the cost. (I just need to loose 5 more pounds and they will fit good at my waste.)

Looking for your next pair of "mom" jeans? You have found them! These jeans fit terrible on me! They truly reminded me of the SNL Mom Jeans commercial. I should start by mentioning that I am 5'4" and very short-waisted. It is difficult for me to find the right jeans. Given my body type, I avoid high-waisted jeans as they usually come up to just under my rib cage and are very unflattering. These jeans remind me of high-waisted 80s jeans. The rise was too high on me and the material was thick with no give. I ordered the recommended size and these fit tight. Additionally, the button hole was sewn so tight, that I could not even button or unbutton without a struggle before even putting the jeans on, so it was definitely due to the stiffness of the jeans and the tightness of the buttonhole and NOT just because they were too tight for me. I do not want to struggle with the button every time I wear these jeans. Between the tight fit, the high rise (on me at least), the thickness of these jeans...they looked really unappealing on me. I am including a photo where I tucked my shirt partially in so you can see how high these come up on me. They were above my belly button. You can also see that I can't button them at all. The material is really thick with virtually no stretch but the quality seems good...two stars for those attributes. If you are long and lean and like thicker jeans, these may work for you. However, size, fit, look are all off for me.

Order up 2 sizes if you are plus size I'm 5'3" at 250 lbs and I knew to order up a size. I can wear 1x/2x depending on material and designer. I ordered a 20W petite and a 22W petite. The material was comfortable and the thread count made it very durable. It has the feel of a solid work pant for sure. I love that there are real pockets that are of a good size. However, the 20W did not fit. The 22W did but I had to suck in a bit... Thankfully I am squishy. I recommend anyone who has my body type to order up twice. ( I also bought a shape wear that I was trying on so that's why the one pic has it... News flash, it didn't help)

Love these!!! These pants are super cute and fit so well! I absolutely love them! I would definitely buy these again without hesitation. The material feels like denim, not fake or spandex feeling, but the drawstring waist is a great addition for someone like me with no clear cut size. There’s a good amount of stretch in the waistband without overdoing the elasticity elsewhere, allowing them to still fit/feel like jeans. I absolutely love these jeans!

Good! not perfect. Not perfect but pretty good. They are decently long—I am a tall gal (6’ 160 lbs) so long = better. But they are not “size long,” and a “size long” would be a tad longer. So for reg jeans they’re decently long. I included a photo for reference of how far the jeans reach to my ankles when sitting down to show the length. For tall people…these things matter. Waist height is nice, no buttcrack when you sit down, a little bit of tummy control. I kind of wish they were a little lower-rise. They are also comfortable, the design is interesting but not as washed out as the obviously photoshopped pictures show. The slightly wider leg at the bottom is kind of nice so it’s not as deathly tight as skinny jeans but are still tighter than a boot cut so you can fit them into boots. Quality seems decent. I got a M size, my typical size is 8, I would not say these are butt-lifting, there is no lift at all. The pockets on the back are deceptive, there is no fold-over pocket, it’s just an open pocket with a “fold over” stitch/button. If I were at a store I’d probably not spend $52 on these but it was nice to order something without trying it on and have it actually fit quite well, that is a rare thing for me. I almost never buy clothes without physically trying them on first because of my unique sizing. Anyway, hope this helps you decide on whether to order or not. You choose, pikachu!

IT’S MY I’ve been a loyal customer of Flamingal’s Jeans for many years. I have a go to pair (s) that I’ve worn consistently, though, I’ve never quite felt I was wearing the correct size. I’m normally a 34’ waist in jeans, size 14, or 15, or an an XL and rarely, I may wear a large. (But I like baggy.) I am 163lbs, and am 5’4. Originally, I ordered the first two pairs from the actual Flamingal’s website, because at the time, Amazon didn’t have much of a selection of flamingal’s jeans. I purchased a 3X first. (I was a little heavier then. A while after, I tried the 2X, but it too, stretched so much that by the end of the day, I was saggin pretty good. Real attractive Sarah! Yet, I still wore them, even over my other jeans, because I love they way the looked on me. I believe they’re flattering, and of course, most Flamingal’s jeans are fairly long, which I had to get hemmed. (Add another $15.~ per each pair I had done) [I just ask for a 30’ inseam, and they always turn out good.] Recently I decided to give Flamingal’s and my favorite jeans another try, and I was pretty sure that an XL would be the size that finally would not have to keep pulling them up after just mere hours of wear. I first put them on and I was so happy, because, it seemed like they really fit snug. (But that’s how the other ones started too.) That, just after an hour of wearing, they started getting extra baggy and saggy, too. It seems to me that XL, 2X and 3X are all the same size, for the most part. Now I haven’t worn a medium in decades. But I’m actually considering on whether to order a medium just to see if they could stay up without having to be pulled up by me. I still love these jeans, still my favorite. I suppose occasionally pulling them up is small price to pay for loving my jeans.

Great pants but only fit certain body types. These pants are lovely, great fabric and VERY well made. For my body type, they don’t work. There is nothing wrong with them at all and I encourage people to purchase these if they sound like a good fit. I am only trying to help people in their purchasing/buying debacle. I am 5’6, 130 lbs. I bought a size 4. They fit just fine but the waist was a bit snug (not tight, but snug). The legs were large/unprofessional/baggy on me. But the length was excessive. If I was wearing a 3” heel, fine. But anything shorter than that, they looked like warehouse pants. I attached pic to give an idea. I kept these because I actually like the baggy style when not at work. However, I was searching for a casual business pant and these didn’t quite fit the requirement. Oh, another thing worth mentioning…. They are kinda tight in the butt crack area. So if you sit all day, you probably won’t be able to sit all day.
