There is nothing wrong with them at all and I encourage people to purchase these if they sound like a good fit. I am only trying to help people in their purchasing/buying debacle. I am 5’6, 130 lbs. I bought a size 4. They fit just fine but the waist was a bit snug (not tight, but snug). The legs were large/unprofessional/baggy on me. But the length was excessive. If I was wearing a 3” heel, fine. But anything shorter than that, they looked like warehouse pants. I attached pic to give an idea. I kept these because I actually like the baggy style when not at work. However, I was searching for a casual business pant and these didn’t quite fit the requirement. Oh, another thing worth mentioning…. They are kinda tight in the butt crack area. So if you sit all day, you probably won’t be able to sit all day.

Comfy, dressy pants. New fav. Ok, I still don’t know what cut these are but I would say somewhere between bootcut and like a straight wide leg? They are fitted through the top, straight and then straight to the bottom with the a wide leg opening from the knee down. But ad pictured in the product photos, these are not like the flowy wide leg pants a palazzo style. I got the Heather gray color and they look very nice and classy. Definitely going to be my dressier work days go-to. I wore these and dressed them up with the heels, and the black top/blazer you can kind of see pictured to work one day and my husband was like oh you look extra professional today!The main highlight: They are comfy as heck! The stretch is just right, and pretty much true to size, with some caveats. I’m 5’4” and about 170ish, thick thighs, some butt, not a lot of hips, then some pudgy stomach stuff takes over the waistline. I normally wear a 10 regular and purchased that size. At first, I was a little worried they would be tight and that it would put strain on the fly/button but once they were past my thighs and all the way up I could tell they would be fine by the stretch in them. I was very pleased with how they hugged my butt and actually made it look more like the cute booty I used to have in my 20s! 😂 I tried to take pics of back but, girl, they couldn’t do it justice. lol Anyway, the only con would be if you are short, these absolutely run long. I don’t mind that, I will be wearing these with heels and I like my pants on the longer side, so not a problem for me. I did add a pic of the length in a pair of heeled boots, probably about 3” heels so you can see how they hit.The material weight is a mid weight. Might be ok in summer with AC in office setting, maybe a little warm. Not heavy though I’ll be wearing these through Michigan winters just running from my car to the office. It’s not like I’m going doing anything active in them. Perfect for fall/spring.I have only worn them with belts. I just feel like it pulls the outfit together better. So not sure if there would be the back gap issue or slipping but they feel like they’re secure all around without a belt.All pockets are real, just bit smaller like you’d expect on dressy pants. They have been very wrinkle resistant so far, even a bit pet hair resistant. I’m hoping the black material is the same because I just ordered these again in black. (Fingers crossed they’re just as good)I do still prefer everyday casual wide, flowy work pants, but these are absolutely up there with my new top fav pants.

Lighter than what I expected The color of these is lighter than I was expecting them to be. They aren’t baggy, fit a bit loosely but comfortably. Great fit, comfortable, and decently flattering. I'm 5'7", about 160-165lbs, and bought the brown ones in a size 10 based on my measurements and the sizing chart. They fit me just about perfectly. The fabric is medium-thick, and has a surprising amount of stretch to it. I LOVE that there are nearly full sized functional pockets in both front and back, though since the fabric isn't as thick as old school non-stretchy jeans, the outline of the front pockets is visible. The top third or so is pretty fitted around the waist and hips, and the legs are roomy. Personally, I really dislike super skinny jeans, but though I grew up wearing some pretty wide-legged pants, I wish there was the slightest bit more of a taper to the bottoms of these. They're also a little more distressed looking than i find necessary, but its relatively well done. Overall, I'm surprisingly pleased with these jeans, and would recommend them to a friend.

Very comfortable and fit perfectly! These jeans have an incredible stretch. They fit the body without constricting, are super comfortable to wear all day, and feel like a second skin. Nice material I'm 5ft so these were a little longer for me. The material is great and soft. Although the fade of the pants in the front are a little different from the picture.

Great jeans, nice price, good quality. For reference, my weight is 170 & height is roughly 5'5", I wouldn't say I fall into apple, pear, triangle or whatever shape, I'm shaped more like a darn eggroll quite frankly! There is literally about 1-2" between my rib cage and my hip. Anyway, I went by the size chart and ordered a 16. Returned them and purchased a 14. The length is perfect for my height, suitable with flats or heels. The material quality is nice! I have them in the wash now, hoping they will shrink just a bit, because honestly, I think I could have gone down 1 more size, but I'm not willing to chance it. I enjoy food entirely too much for my own good. I'll definitely be purchasing more!!! YES, I did let it all hang out, ask me if I care 🤪

Comfy and flattering I love these! They are comfy and stretchy. The high waist with criss-cross is genius. (Usually I hate high-waist jeans, but most lower-waist cause really unfortunate rolls visible under a shirt). I'm 5'5", almost 200 lbs, XL regular was a great fit, including for length, and really flattering. I do have to hike the legs up regularly, but that doesn't really bother me. The waist/butt stays up pretty well. I got them in the dark denim and black. I wish I could find them in a wine/burgundy color!

pleasantly surprised I was skeptical these would be long enough since most pants in long/tall sizes don't end up fitting. But these are a perfect length. They do stretch out in the waist as you wear The jeans are very stretchy and have a little bit of a rough feel. They fit very well when I first put them on, but then kept stretching out in the waist. If I had realized they were going to continue to stretch, then I would have gotten a size 6. I also was in the midst of losing weight when I first purchased them, so I am 5’6 and around 140 when I ordered. After I lost 10 more lbs, then the jeans were way too big. Overall, the jeans were cute, and the rips made them look very casual and in style.

Very strong perfume scent on my new jeans I have a 30 waist but ordered the size 6 (for 27 inch waists) because I saw the reviews saying these run large. They are still too big, but they’re cute, the right length (I’m short, 5’2) and close enough to my waist size that it can be covered up, so I figured I’d keep them and make it work. Here’s the thing - they had an artificial, perfume scent on them when they arrived. I washed and dried them, the scent is still there and as strong as ever. I’m letting them hang to hopefully air out the smell, but if that doesn’t work, will need to return (I have artificial fragrance sensitivities, perfume makes me sick). I’m also nervous that these may not be new jeans. They had no tag and they arrived in generic, unbranded plastic.

Great jeans for my curvy bottom Love these jeans! I am slightly curvy in all the right places, but jeans can be tough to find. These have a little bit of stretch and I’m 5’4” and they go to the perfect place above my ankles to show off my jewelry or shoes. I wore them quite a bit this spring and I still love them, my only reason for 4 stars is that the pockets are distressed, as someone who has her phone in the back pocket more than not, this made my pocket not very functional

One of my favorite pairs of jeans! I love these jeans. I am 5’6, about 123 lb, with shorter waist and longer legs. I got a 4 since that’s the smallest size but I normally would get a 2 or 4 in pants. The 4s fit great- not too big, jut perfectly. They are very comfortable in the waist and in the legs. They don’t itch and the material is not too thick at all. They are so nice and stretchy. I love the jeans length and they go with so many types of shoes. I love the rips in them.

Fit nice but… ehh Ordered these white pants for the summer. Pro: The cut/style of the pants fit very nicely. They don’t feel too thick to wear in hot weather. They’re comfortable Cons: The material is cheap looking. They’re not completely white in color, has a blue-ish tint. The closure button came broken. Ordered a small but received a medium. Besides these solid pants, I also ordered the ripped style pants (from DLAYBGFA) which also aren’t completely white, but did arrive in a size small and I like the way they fit. I like my pants fitted. I’m 5‘2“ 140 lbs. I’m returning these because they’re not the size I ordered and the button came broken. Won’t be reordering because they’re cheap and blue-ish, but I’ll keep the ripped pair that did come in the size I ordered, they fit very nice and I’ll just wear them out at night. Attaching a pic of the blue-ish pants on top of an old pair of white pants that I was trying to replace.

Fits great Im about 130 lbs 5'4. Bought a small. Fits perfectly. Wore them to Nurse John Comedy Show! I like them i I think I prefer low rise or mid rise waist. These jeans are high waisted and tends to create muffin top when sitting but other than that they're great! Im in the middle with white top and black stressed jeans btw!

Super cute, too tight on calves Really cute, great color, plenty of room for curves up high, but I have decent calf muscles and they are way too tight on my calves so I’m sending them back. Material feels kind of thin, which is typical of other Levi Strauss pants I’ve had, but compared with those it’s not nearly as stretchy. For reference I’m 5’11, 190 lbs, usually wear a 14 and that’s what I ordered. It fit pretty well up top but it’s definitely made for extra curves…I had a little extra room in the waist and butt.

Definitely “SUPER” skinny When they say “super” skinny they are definitely “SUPER SKINNY” Material is thick like a regular jean w a small amount of stretch to them, and color is exactly as pictured. I’m a size 0-1 & in order to fit my waist properly & snug I ordered 25” regular. Cobalt Overboard goes all the way to ankle unlike description photo Im 5’9 169 postpartum and these are my first pair of postpartum pants/jeans and after just trying them on Im SO HAPPY (size 31 regular). Even though I ordered Cobalt Overboard the packaging says Marine Overboard but it’s fine because they fit beautifully and go all the way to my ankle which was a pleasant surprise because the photo makes it look like they’re shorter and have more of a capri look. I prefer the longer look actually and can just roll them if I need/want to.

I finally found some black levis that fit. These are the blackest black levis I have ever seen. They are soft and stretchy and they fit great (My butt is lifted up). As I mentioned in another review (Levis 311 skinny plus, kabekona camo), I currently wear an 18 in Womens Levis 505 which run larger than most levis so I sized up to a size 22 to be safe. I probably could have gotten away with a size 20 with these but they are so comfortable (I don't want to take them off). However, I did not have to hold my breath to button them or wiggle into them, they just slid on, so I'm happy. I will also have to wear a belt with these which is always the case for me. For reference: I am 5"5 with thick hips, butt, and thighs and currently weigh around 260 and my stomach is not flat but it is smaller in proportion to the rest of my body.

Very cute but might not work if in-between sizes I cannot decide if I am going to keep the medium or not. Way too small on the waist. No stretch. 198lbs, 5’7. I purchased a XL even though Amazon recommended that I get a Large lol. Cute bootcut I’m 65, 5’6 and 158lbs. I purchased a size M. When I first put these on, they felt a little snug in my waist but perfect everywhere else. With a little stretch to them, it wasn’t an uncomfortable snug feeling which did loosen up slightly after wearing them for a few hours. The rise is a little longer than I’m used to but again, not uncomfortable. The length is perfect for a low healed boot or shoe. May be too short if taller than 5’6. I love the light blue color, the button on the bottom. Overall a cute pair of jeans. I did purchase a 2nd pair in dark blue. These may be my new go to bootcut jeans

Good fitting jeggings. Look like real jeans. My jeans are all a bit tight after I gained 10 pounds so I have been on the look out for jean-like leggings that didn't look like too much like fake jeans. Not an easy thing to find. These No Nonsense Women's Denim Legging actually look like jeans. They are not thick and stiff like jeans but they have good texture and coloring. I like that, if I roll them up, they have jean-like coloring on the inside so they look very much like a real pair of jeans. I am 5"5", 139 pounds and got size medium. I also tried the size small and they were just too tight around my gut to be comfortable. The mediums fit really well around my stomach, hips, butt, and legs. I do have thin legs so these don't cling super tightly like a pair of high Spandex leggings but that's fine. That makes them look even more like real skinny jeans. The length is good and they seem to be made well. I will probably buy a pair or two more. They're not warm but definitely not see through or flimsy. If they were just a bit thicker for cooler weather, they would be just about perfect. They are the most realistic looking jean leggings I have found so far.

Perfect affordable pregnancy pants! These are seriously the best "pants" ever. I usually only wear dresses and leggings (blue jeans are SO uncomfortable), but I wanted something to wear tshirts and sweaters with. I have these in a medium prepregnancy and they fit perfectly (for reference I was between 135-140 pounds, 5'5", and a size 6). I wanted comfortable pants to wear during my pregnancy and even with maternity pants the crotch area would be too tight and cut into me, and with how much my hips spread my thighs felt too restricted (not to mention that maternity clothes cost a small fortune). So I took a gamble and ordered these 2 sizes larger and the XL are PERFECT! (Hoping I can get a little more wear out of them postpartum until I can get my body back) For reference, I am 36 weeks pregnant, 5'5", size Medium in Motherhood Maternity/Old Navy Maternity, and 190 pounds. Most of my pregnancy weight is in my belly and hips. P.S. The length is good too, fits about the same as the mediums. There is a little bunching around my ankles but nothing bothersome, I've worn both tennis shoes and boots comfortably with these.

NOW THIS... Is a home run buy!!! LOVE LOVE LOVE!!!! THESE ARE FANTASTIC!!!! Totally different from the"classic" pair I bought and gave away ,, in the light denim. There is a HUGE difference in the CLASSIC and the this one that just says " no nonsense woman's legging" . Beautiful stretch denim material. Light and nice texture... They sit a tad below my belly button ( I am short waisted). FIT IS PERFECT...Hug you in a all the right places. No uncomfortable wad of fabric In the crotch. Ladies you know what I mean! No rubbing. I am 5' 4 106 lbs. , I got the smallest. I was SO HAPPY right out of the box. :):) The length is perfect, and if you want to roll the bottom up a bit with a casual shoe it looks like real jeans, except you won't want to take them off the first chance you get. LOVE THEM> The waist band is the perfect size too... doesn't dig in . Very smooth silhouette. These can be casual or dressed up . They have a deep and generous back pocket. My IPhone 7s fits nicely without it popping out. Compared to the 'classic" ones...these have no brass button ( which i prefer) and I still can't believe the difference in the fit. I LOVE THEM>>>>going to get more as they have a great selection of colors and patterns! I also bought a pair of the capri's in pink and they too are awesome. Same great fit and no button.Nobody would know these are 'jeggings"... they look like jeans , and I am sick of spending big bucks on uncomfortable jeans!SO CUTE!!! *addendum I wore this today in the car , 90 degree heat , runner no around doing errands etc. I am happy to NO SAGGING OR BAGGING ! awesome!☺️

Not bad I’m 11 weeks post partum and struggling to find clothes I feel comfortable in. Pre-pregnancy I was 115 lbs and a pretty standard size 4 in bottoms and small in tops. I’m right at 135 now and unable to fit in most of my clothes, but have been hesitant to spend too much knowing ultimately I’d like to lose some weight. I’m probably between sizes 6 and 8 now depending on the brand and material. I am 5’4” for reference. Best guess on my current bra size is a D. Sharing all this just to make it clear, I don’t really have a “normal” to use as a size comparison right now. Also offering up having a newborn as an excuse as to why my mirror and room are such a mess lol. After reading reviews I ordered a medium, and I’m glad I did. I think I would have struggled to squeeze into a small, and a large would have been too big. I know not to expect top of the line quality from most Amazon clothes, especially in this price range. The quality was about what I expected, not terrible but obviously not top of the line.

Tears easily I was actually surprised this was my fav pair to try of the 3 different brands I ordered. They’re super comfy. I’m muscular in the legs and am 5’0 tall. I do NOT put my hands in my back pockets much with an occasional phone, which is in my right back pocket. The left has started to tear at the corner already, and I’ve worn these a handful of times, mainly at work for casual Fridays. I can sew them back with reinforcement, though it’s only been 6 weeks since I bought them. I’ll be ordering another pair. The hole is the only reason it’s a 4 star.

The small is too loose for xsmall wearers I'm 5'2" & at the time of the photos, I was 26 waist 36 hips & around 107 lb. I bought this pair in 2019 & wore it for about 3 years before it became very faded. It is very stretchy, it's flattering except the pockets are kind of narrow & longish? makes your butt look like it is slimmer but longer lool. It's not durable, don't expect to last more than a couple of years, depending on how much you wear & wash it. Back then when I wore this pair the most, I was around 115 - 125 lb & it became my go to pair for everything. I would say, I only recommend this pair for people over 115 pounds, otherwise it would be too lose so if you wear xs, the small will probably be too loose. I hope it helps.

Length is too long I have lost weight and was excited to order a size down. The waist was great. Pictured is my old size 14 on top of the new size 12. The length was way to long and for straight leg cut they were very very wide. I am 5’6.

Nice Jeans Length is for Taller Women I am 5.5, 150 lbs. The size 10 was a great fit except for the length. A 30 inch inseam is too long for my height. I will have to have the pants hemmed about 2 inches. I give a 5 star for comfort. I haven't washed the jeans yet, but I did wear them all day and found them to be very comfortable.

Great jeans!! These jeans are great! They have a bit of stretch which is so comfortable. They do run a bit long (I'm 5'7" 135 lbs) and bought the size 6 regular. With boots/ heels they'll be perfect, but with flats the hem is a bit too long. Will see how much they shrink in the dryer. But overall I am very happy!!

My new favorite TRUE plus size jeans Where's my late 90s, early 2000s teens at? We are late thirties, early forties now. We grew to LOVE our high rise jeans. We accepted skinny jeans as any good young adults would. But don't y'all miss the nostalgia of our wide leg, drag the floor jeans!? I did. I was tired of the uncovered ankles. (Where did our 2 inches' worth of denim fabric go?) These jeans are IT for casual. I get the wide leg. I get the slight stretch. I get THE HIGH RISE, GLORY DAY!! Are they going to get soaking wet if I wear them in the rain? Yep. Are the back leg hems going to fray from walking on them? You betcha. I can't wait. Height 5'9, weight 305ish, size 22 with a little room to spare.

Size runs a little on the large side - women's stretchy jeggings These are very nice and comfortable women's jeggings. My wife prefers jeggings way over jeans. These are very comfortable and stretchy. The material is high quality cotton, polyester and spandex. They are black, have an elastic waistband, easy to pull on and off, have two front pockets and two back pockets, and no zippers or buttons. The only reason I am taking one star away is that the sizes run a bit big because the way they are classified is a big jump in sizes. For example, the small says they'll fit sizes 4-10. My wife is 115 lbs and 5'4 so she got a small. They are a tad bit looser than shed like, but hopefully they'll shrink in the wash. Other than that, they are very nice!

user_comment: Surprisingly Comfortable and Winter-Ready Jeggings I wasn’t sure what to expect when I first felt the material—it’s thicker than typical jeggings, and the waistband is a wide elastic band with a stretchy fit. But once I tried them on, I was pleasantly surprised by how comfortable they are. The stitching looks durable, and I love that the pockets in both the front and back are actually functional. At 5'9", I often struggle with length, but these fit perfectly. I’m currently between a medium and large, but since I’m losing weight, I opted for the small-medium size and they fit comfortably without being too tight. They’re a bit too warm for Phoenix weather right now, but I can already tell they’ll be ideal once winter rolls around.

Wide legs must mean baggier fit. Good craftsmanship. Denim fabric for longer life and use Its baggier than I thought, but perhaps that's the nature of "wide leg" pants. Guess I have to get used to it. It's also longer than I expected, but that's an easy fix. Better that it's longer than shorter (I'm 5'4" --- typically described as "regular"). This is probably longer than 30" in seam. The craftsmanship is pretty good, so I don't expect the seams to unravel at any time, judging by the reinforced seams. The details are there in terms of the pockets on the legs, which identifies this pair as cargo pants. When it's new, it should be acceptable for business casual dressing, but once the color fades, then it is simply a pair of black jeans more suited for sport.

Baggy and loose cargo pants I ordered the IWD Womens Cargo Pants High Waisted Straight Leg Trousers in size small and they run LARGE! I washed them three times in hot water and dried them to shrink them up so they fit better. There's still quite a bit of room in the waist and hips, even with the elastic waist in the back. They are mainly cotton (97% Cotton and 3% Spandex) and machine washable. The fabric is thick and stretchy. The side pockets on the legs have buttons which keep the flaps down. After washing they now brush the floor. For reference, I'm 5'3" and 125 lbs.

Happy with these! These are comfortable and have some stretch! I was in between sizes based on the size chart and opted to go up, but I should have gone down. They are a little baggier than I would like. They are also a little long on me at 5’ 4” but I like this length actually. Roomy pockets!

Skinny Jean Wearer Converts to Wide Leg Jeans! I've been wearing skinny jeans for years. They hug in all the right places and I can wear boots that I love. Now we have this wide leg jean fashion trend happening. So, I thought I'd give it a try. I'm so glad these jeans were my first pick because not only do they look decent on, they feel so incredibly comfortable. Since buying these, I purchased three of a different brand of wide leg jeans, and NONE of them fit the way these do. I think the seam down the front gives it a slenderizing look. For reference, I purchased a size 16. I typically wear a size 14. I'm 5'6" 195 lbs. The size 16 that I purchased are not loose or tight, they are a perfect fit for my body shape. These jeans have some stretch! The double button is cute. The length is on the long side.. I'd say 31 or 32". I wear them mostly with boots or shoes with a 2" heel. When I wear sneakers they are a little bunchy at the bottom but still fine. Give them a try. You can always return them if they don't fit but I hope you love them as much as I do!

No pude elegir correctamente la talla y aunque son buenos pantalones me quedaron grandes. Lamentablemente, estos pantalones me quedaron algo grandes ya que yo elegí la talla incorrecta. Siempre que compro algunos jeans, elijo la talla M (Mediana) y listo!, Sé que me quedarán bien, pero cuando las tallas son con números, ya se me hace más difícil elegir la talla y tengo que guiarme con la gráfica que proporcionan en la descripción. Siguiendo ésa gráfica parecía que me quedaría bien la talla 14, cuando me probé éste pantalón, me quedó algo flojo o "aguado" de la cintura y las piernas, quizás una talla menos me hubiese quedado bien ya que la cintura es elástica. Como referencia yo mido 5' 7" y 147lb. La tela de mezclilla es buena, es gruesa, es me gusta cuando elijo unos buenos jeans. Sólo recomiendo que chequen 2 veces su talla para no cometer mi error.

Nice jeans, the fit just doesn’t flatter my body type So these are obviously shown to be a pair of truly wide-legged jeans, the type that are very trendy for this fall. They’re really great jeans but just know that they may not be flattering on those of us who are at the higher end of the size range here. For instance, I’m 5’7”, 165 lbs, average build with a mom pooch that makes it so I need a larger size in bottoms to accommodate my waist than I need for my hips, thighs etc. I would otherwise fit about a size 10 in jeans except I usually have to get a 12 or 14 for my waist. After reading the size measurements I decided to go with the 14 which I think was the correct pick, these fit around my waist perfectly. They have a moderate amount of stretch and I may have been able to fit the size 12’s waist but they likely wouldn’t have been comfortable, whereas these are insanely comfortable! They’re also just… much bigger and baggier around my butt and thighs than I expected, and while I like the trendy style, I just don’t think these are flattering on my body type. But I love the fabric, how soft it feels, the jeans are just the right amount of stretchy, and if they flatter you’re body type I’m sure you will love them. I’m going between 4 and 5 stars because it really might only be a “me” thing and perhaps most people are buying these expecting them to be quite baggy in the backside, but I just didn’t think it would be this much, but again it could be due to the fact that I do have to size up for my waist. I’d give them 4.5 stars if I could! Overall really lovely, just not sure if they’re for me. Only other note would be that I ordered the color simply titled “Blue” and they are darker in person than how they looked online but only a little, it could definitely just be different monitor settings and all that. I hope this is !

I’m going between 4 and 5 stars because it really might only be a “me” thing and perhaps most people are buying these expecting them to be quite baggy in the backside, but I just didn’t think it would be this much, but again it could be due to the fact that I do have to size up for my waist. I’d give them 4.5 stars if I could! Overall really lovely, just not sure if they’re for me. Only other note would be that I ordered the color simply titled “Blue” and they are darker in person than how they looked online but only a little, it could definitely just be different monitor settings and all that. I hope this is !

Nice jeans, wrong style for me The idea of jeans with wide legs as opposed to the skinny legs we have worn for a while now appealed to me. It's going to take some time for me to adjust to the style again. When these Sidefeel jeans arrived, I was impressed with the softness of the denim with just the right amount of stretch. They are not stiff. The fabric has a worn look with the whisker lines below the waistband. The seams down the front of the legs give an interesting detail. They were packed in a roll tightly sealed in a plastic bag and I expected wrinkles. There were no wrinkles so that is a plus. I didn't detect any odd smell like some new fabrics have. I like the 2 metal buttons and the sturdy zipper. The front pockets don't lay completely flat so it adds some bulk to the belly area. I laundered them as directed on the label and they came out perfectly.Now for my personal opinion of fit. I carry extra weight in my thighs and thought the front seaming would be slimming. It isn't enough to make the cut of the wide legs work for me. There is excess fabric in the back of the leg that is not attractive on me. I am 5'4" and the length is perfect. The size chart is accurate. I like the jeans enough that I plan to redesign the legs to remove some of the width so I will like them better. Also, I was impressed enough that I searched the Sidefeel brand and ordered another pair of jeans with a narrower leg.If you are comfortable with the wide leg look, I would say you would like these.

Done with Gloria Vanderbilt I am 5'10" tall, 150 lbs. My waist is 31, my hips are 39, my upper thigh is 23.5, my knee is 16.5, my calf 14.5, and my inseam is 34.5. I ordered an 8 long. (My legs are large, so these are the closest I can realistically get to a somewhat skinnier, non-baggy look.) This is online shopping, ladies: let's help each other out with stats! My local stores don't carry tall jeans, so I can only order online, and I need all the help I can get choosing! Buyers of this Levi style say the denim fabric is thin - it certainly is - and it is probably the reason it does not cut into my waist like the Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda classic straight jeans, which I wore in my 50's, do now. I am switching to this Levi style now I've entered my 60's; my waist is bigger and more sensitive to pressure from stiff and heavy fabrics than it was in earlier stages of my life. For someone my height, the pair I ordered in 8 long covers my ankle bone, so my socks won't show when I wear shoes. This is all I was looking for in that regard. Bottom line: Yes, the waist runs big - hallelujah! - and there is some stretching out, but, I personally have never ever owned a pair of jeans that hasn't stretched out over a day of wearing, so this is not a deal breaker for me. Yes, the denim is thin, so if I want to wear these and be warm here in Michigan in winter, I will have to wear tights under them if I spend any significant time outside, which, I rarely do. I also wear a very long coat, so I expect to be fine regardless.

Jeans material has issues! I’ve lost weight and needed to get a whole size down on jeans. 5’4”, 115 pounds and small bone frame. Bought many different colors in these jeans. They fit very comfortably on me and have room left in them for flexibility. I wore these one pair of jeans once and washed them. Put them on and noticed a fabric snag in the inseam area of these jeans. It wasn’t there prior to washing—front loader on delicate cycle. Now I am concerned, especially since other buyers noted they had fabric issues too. Will these pants keep having more issues each time I wash them?? Not what I am paying for on a name brand style of jean. Will try to contact seller to see what is going on with the material they are using. And to see about a return. So sad as I do enjoy the color and fit. Don’t enjoy them starting to snag tho!

Fit Like a Glove So I purchased a 2L because I have long legs. They are long enough that they hit the top of my feet and half of my heel. I have a thick heel though. lol. I weigh 163 right now. Even if a gain or lose a pound or 2…they will still fit fine I’m sure. My waist is usually 28inch but I sometimes go for smaller waist sizes for the nice cinch it can create. Due to what I read in the reviews before I purchased I opted to try a size 2 long. These jeans feel like a super stretch light material. Like a begging but heavier. Not too light that it doesn’t hold but feels good and comfortable. It almost covers most of my belly button and covers my little booty. I love them. I was a little worried when I pulled them out the bag but I’m pleasantly surprised and pleased when I tried them on. They need to make more colors!

Comfy, comes in long I'm excited for these. I've only tried them on but they have a nice high waist and long inseam. I'm 5'9," and 32" inseams don't quite cut it. There was the option to buy 12 Long or 12 Tall, but under product description, it didn't have a definition for what tall meant, which is frustrating. But I bought the Tall size and when I got the pants, the sticker tag said 34" L which is what I was hoping for. I'm 180 and I got a size 12. They fit good now but I think I will probably want a belt because they are quite stretchy.

Great fitting jeans! I wanted to get some straight leg jeans and these are exactly what I was looking for! For reference, I am 5'3", 133 lbs. The length is perfect to wear with boots, flats or sneakers. I read someone else's review and it was most ! I ordered the 4S, W27/L28. Perfect fit, great quality and great price!

Surprisingly impressed! Love the fit. Signature jeans are a little thinner and more generous than normal Levi's. These fit really well. Nice length. I'm usually an 8 in other Levi's but in Signature I easily get away with a 6L. I'm 5ft 9" and generally need a 34" inseam on straight leg jeans. My waist is currently 32-33" and my hips are 43-44". Hopefully that helps someone more than the usual height and weight which tells us nothing about weight distribution! 🩷

Cute and Comfortable Stretchy Denim Button Fly Jeans The Grapent wide leg, high-waist denim pants are cute and comfortable. They are stylish and can be worn year round. The Medium Blue is a light to medium color blue denim. The material is a middle-weight denim with stretch. The jeans fit as described in the product description. I ordered based on the size description. I am 5’3” and weigh 140 pounds and the size Medium fits me well. The length is perfect for me, it is 30 inches. These jeans have a straight leg, which provides a relaxed fit with a button fly and high waist. These jeans are flattering. This is a good price for a pair of comfortable and stylish jeans.

Incredibly comfortable and true to size I am 5’9” and 140 pounds and got the medium - they fit true to size. The denim is soft and stretchy, but still feels sturdy with great stitching and overall quality. The fabric has a good amount of stretch so if you’re in between sizes, I’d recommend sizing down. They do loosen up a bit as you wear them. These sit at a natural waste, which I really like - and the button-up front is super cute and surprisingly easy to use. Some button fly jeans are stiff or tricky, but not these. They are labeled as wide leg, but I’d call them more of a straight leg, which makes them really versatile. The brown color is a nice switch from blue denim and pairs well with a variety of tops. Overall stylish easy to wear and a great value.

Wish they came in mid rise. Good quality and color but im 46 years old and grew up on low waist and I just CANNOT do the high rise. I just want a normal mid rise pant. When I pull them down to where I feel comfortable they just look worse because then there is too much fabric in the crouch. I just cannot wear pants that go to my belly button. So this is all for those ladies......

Cute Camo, But Fit Runs Large For reference, I’m 5’4” and 120 lbs. I ordered a Medium based on reviews, but I should’ve gotten a Small. On me, these are not high-waisted and were very baggy — you can see in the photo where I can stick my fingers into the waistband, showing how loose they were. After the first wash, they shrunk length-wise, which I didn’t love because they’re not long enough for me now. The waist and legs are still relaxed. Cute camo print and comfortable fabric, but they do run big and shrink in length. I paired them with the OQQ Women’s Ribbed Seamless Tank Tops (size Small – Black, Candy Blue, Light) and I really love these tops. They’re durable, flattering, and sexy without being uncomfortable. Tip: Size down and be aware they may shrink in length after washing.

On me, these are not high-waisted and were very baggy — you can see in the photo where I can stick my fingers into the waistband, showing how loose they were. After the first wash, they shrunk length-wise, which I didn’t love because they’re not long enough for me now. The waist and legs are still relaxed. Cute camo print and comfortable fabric, but they do run big and shrink in length. I paired them with the OQQ Women’s Ribbed Seamless Tank Tops (size Small – Black, Candy Blue, Light) and I really love these tops. They’re durable, flattering, and sexy without being uncomfortable. Tip: Size down and be aware they may shrink in length after washing.

True to size and great for short folks Apparently these are men's jeans. I didn't even notice that at all when I bought them. I got them in the size 18 short. I'm 5'1", and these fit me about 2 inches longer than I like but absolutely not a deal breaker. I just don't like it because they are slim leg so because they're a bit long they just bunch at my ankle and I don't like that. A quick little fold and you're golden. They are great quality, the material feels a bit stiff and it is definitely a much thicker Jean. Like a lot of jeans, these do get more loose throughout the day. For me, it's not that noticeable and since they're made of more of a stiffer material they tend to hold their shape better. I think I could have sized down to a size 16 because I do prefer a much tighter Jean in the legs however I will say that the size 18 feels good around the waist. Overall I would say they are true to size but if you prefer a much tighter hold around your tummy and or legs you can def size down.

Sizing & Material differs per color I'm 5'6 about 130lbs. I ordered the black and rose pink. I ordered the rose in a 2 and the black in a 4; both regular. As posted with the picture of the tags, they are made of different materials - the pink was noticeable softer and made in an entirely different factory. So you're not really buying a different color of the same pants, you're buying a different material. It's pretty thick material, stayed up after wearing 2 different days before washing. I'd say the length is weird. I wish it hugged at the ankle. It is a PAIN to pull up and down though. It's not the most stretchy, BUT it didn't stretch out. They do have functional pockets. The PRICE will differ on when you buy them. When I bought them, a little over a week ago, the prices were $5 cheaper each for some reason. I washed the black and dried it recently. Haven't tried it back on. But if I can't wash and rewear, I'm returning. Too much work. As note: the 2 In rose fit like a 4 in black because the black was less stretchy.

I got mine just delivered and tried it on and so far, I like it. I was looking for some really comfortable pull up jeans that I could slap on the go. My regular jeans were suffocating me so I was really going for comfort. I was scared to get it because there were some splitting reviews. I got the size 4 and inseam 30. I'm 5'4" (163cm) and 130lbs (~60kgs). They are about the perfect fit and size for me, a tad bit loose on the calves so giving a loose skinny look. (I prefer the slightl loose compared to the skin tight jeggings look) They do give me a bit of a muffin top but I don't really mind as long as they don't either slip down as I walk around or squish me breathless. No slipping noted as I walked around. I had on regular skinny jeans right before (wax jeans size 3 - 26" 27" waist) and they are really similar at the waist. Band feels nice and secure and it didn't seem to give me the weird crotch. I will see how it wears after a wash but so far so good.

Best jeans I have EVER owned! The fit: there is a meshy band (like heavy duty pantyhose) across the belly that completely eradicates my "Mommy" tummy. I have a little pooch, loose jiggle, and sometimes it buldges if the waist line pinches. The waist band on these secures and FIRMS my entire waist - no jiggles or buldges. Then there's my rear, which isn't so perky anymore. These grab my cheeks and pull them up - my butt isnt flat in these. My inner thighs are smoothed out, and cellulite is hidden everywhere.The fabric: they feel high quality, well made. Soft, but durable like a denim/canvas - and they stretch and give when I bend/move without loosing shape or stretching out of shape when I srand back up. I wore these to 3 childrens birthdays in one day and still felt so confident in them to rock to a friends party that night.Size: I am 5"11, 160, bought the black long in a size 10 and they fit great. ps - the long still barely hits my ankle bone, but at least thats better than capri length.These pants are magical and I am SSOO bummed the other colors are out of stock. I did order their recommended similar jeans (skinny, signature, totally shaping) but they zip - dont have waist band and dont lift my rear the same.

Unsure I’m still trying to decide how I feel about these pants. The 12s were too big, so I purchased the 10s this time. For the most part, they fit well, though a bit snug in the waist (I do carry most weight in my stomach). They have enough stretch to be comfortable though. The back pockets are big, which would be fine (since I keep my phone in my butt pocket), except they’re big enough that it goes below the bottom of my butt and creates an odd look. I’m certainly not bootylicious, but these pants definitely make my booty look flatter than it is. If you have any chub in your stomach, these pants don’t look great with a tight/fitted shirt - you’ll want to pair with something looser. I have examples of two different shirts of reference. I love doing a French tuck with my tops (tucking the front into my pants) - it looks fine from the front, but from the side, the pants definitely accentuate my stomach pooch. Since measurements are when deciding to buy clothes online, mine are 40-33.5-39, 168 lbs. As you can see from the pictures, they’re definitely a longer pant - I’m just shy of 5’6” and they go just below my ankle bone. Super comfy, but I’m not sure yet if I’ll keep them, since I want to feel confident in whatever I’m wearing for work and these definitely accentuate some flaws and are a bit hard to match tops with.

If you have any chub in your stomach, these pants don’t look great with a tight/fitted shirt - you’ll want to pair with something looser. I have examples of two different shirts of reference. I love doing a French tuck with my tops (tucking the front into my pants) - it looks fine from the front, but from the side, the pants definitely accentuate my stomach pooch. Since measurements are when deciding to buy clothes online, mine are 40-33.5-39, 168 lbs. As you can see from the pictures, they’re definitely a longer pant - I’m just shy of 5’6” and they go just below my ankle bone. Super comfy, but I’m not sure yet if I’ll keep them, since I want to feel confident in whatever I’m wearing for work and these definitely accentuate some flaws and are a bit hard to match tops with.

Great for thick-waisted, perimenopausal/menopause body changes 49yo, 5'3", 185lbs, Style: Sun Worshiper, size 14. I'm presently in the throws of perimenopause and at my heaviest. These are the only jeans that flatten my tummy area, hiding a small apron depending on underwear with minimal muffin top. I'm self conscious about the weight gain and feel most comfortable in these jeans. The inseam sizing is off as I initially tried on 14S but they had an inseam is 27 so I went with 14. I took photos without a shirt for transparency of how they fit on someone thick waisted like myself. With a shirt you wouldn't be able to detect the waistline. The slimming tummy panel hides any fat rippling. These are jean material and have the same weight and durability as a regular pair of jeans versus jeggings. Once I lose weight I will size down as the tighter my midsection gets then the slimming tummy is more effective.

The material isn’t thick which could be considered good and bad. I did not see my undergarments through the material but if I had ordered a smaller size that might have been an issue. They are lighter weight so I feel like these could be worn as a nice transition piece. Overall they’re comfortable and could be used as a nice pair of boot cut jeans for someone shorter.

Great if you can get a size that fits! Based on reviews and the size chart, I ordered a 2XL. It was too big for me, and definitely would’ve been falling off once they stretched out from wear. My waist is currently 37”, my hips are 45”, I am 5’ 7” tall, and weigh 170.4 pounds. I can wear a 19 or a 15-17 in juniors jeans (No Boundaries brand specifically). Women's jeans between size 10 to 14 fit me, depending on brand. I am planning to exchange for a size down and will update when I get those. The 2XL pair also had a defective pocket, as they had caught the hanging part of the pocket inside the jegging in the pocket rivet, so the pocket was effectively riveted shut. They were nicely made other than the sizing issue and the pocket defect, and I am excited to get a pair of these that fits. They are heavy for a jegging and feel more like an actual jean with a high spandex content. UPDATE: The 1X fits perfectly, and the pockets on these were just fine. Love them!

Comfy but Meh Slimming Effect These jeans fit ok. I got the large (5ft 1” 175-180lbs) & I fluctuate between medium & large usually. The legs are too long which isn’t surprising as most jeans are unless they come in petite/short. The waist goes up over my belly button, they’re easy to get off & on. Def more comfy than normal jeans & honestly prolly a bit more slimming than normal jeans but the “slimming waist band” is like an inch wide & for me that’s not wide enough to slim my belly. But it might suit other body types well. They have no fly but do have pockets (front & back) for some reason one pocket gets having part of the inside fabric peeking out which looks dumb. I tuck it in but it happens again soon. The legs are rather baggy for leggings on me.

Chubby and now comfortable 😊 So I gave this 4 out of 5 stars because the stitching is a little irritating near my thighs and the tears look silly when I sit, however I have big ol thighs lol. I am 5ft and 182lbs with big thighs and a big gut as you can see in the pictures and I read they run small and I’m normally a L or XL so I got a size 1XL for normal and a 2XL for when I’m more bloated and honestly both of them fit great! The ones I have in the pictures are the 1XL (light wash) and I got the 2XL in the dark wash because I know dark wash tends to not stretch the same in denim (fun fact) I have IBS so I bloat twice my size sometimes and these are amazing! The material seems sturdy and the stretch in the tummy is so good! The thing I hate most about wearing jeans is that whatever size fits me when I’m standing does not fit me when I’m sitting. This solves that problem completely, they fit nice and tight when I’m standing and they loosen up and give plenty of room for my stomach when I sit down. And in my opinion they’re indistinguishable from normal jeans in both fabric and look. I would totally recommend these for my lady’s with some tummy and also for a cheaper option to maternity pants, I plan on being able to wear my 2XL for the first few months after I get pregnant. Hope this helped you ladies out and feel more comfortable investing in these. Side note I’ve already put them through the washer and dryer and they didn’t fall apart! 😊

Chubby and now comfortable 😊 So I gave this 4 out of 5 stars because the stitching is a little irritating near my thighs and the tears look silly when I sit, however I have big ol thighs lol. I am 5ft and 182lbs with big thighs and a big gut as you can see in the pictures and I read they run small and I’m normally a L or XL so I got a size 1XL for normal and a 2XL for when I’m more bloated and honestly both of them fit great! The ones I have in the pictures are the 1XL (light wash) and I got the 2XL in the dark wash because I know dark wash tends to not stretch the same in denim (fun fact) I have IBS so I bloat twice my size sometimes and these are amazing! The material seems sturdy and the stretch in the tummy is so good! The thing I hate most about wearing jeans is that whatever size fits me when I’m standing does not fit me when I’m sitting. This solves that problem completely, they fit nice and tight when I’m standing and they loosen up and give plenty of room for my stomach when I sit down. And in my opinion they’re indistinguishable from normal jeans in both fabric and look. I would totally recommend these for my lady’s with some tummy and also for a cheaper option to maternity pants, I plan on being able to wear my 2XL for the first few months after I get pregnant. Hope this helped you ladies out and feel more comfortable investing in these. Side note I’ve already put them through the washer and dryer and they didn’t fall apart! 😊

Stretchy but with support I’ve had a pair of these jeans for several years, that went through many weight changes with me. I even had to get the crotch resewn because I’d ripped them out LOL. I, FINALLY, needed to retire them because they got too big and decided to try and find them. I was so happy when I found them on Amazon and at such an incredible price! The old pair I had was a large so I figured I’d need a medium but, after reading reviews, opted to get both a M & L to try. First of all, the color was totally wrong (not sure if I ordered it accidentally or they sent the wrong one), secondly, the medium was like a torture device lol and the large is perfect, even a tad on the tight side. I’m guessing that my old pair must’ve just been super worn in and maybe stretched out. The color sent was navy blue, which was more of a light/medium wash and I returned both and got dark denim distressed and black distressed in large. The black fit slightly tighter than the dark denim but not enough not to keep them. I love the stretch in them, they smooth out and hold in all of my lumps and bumps, making them very flattering, and they’re stylish. The length is pretty long but I just cuff them which I think looks cute anyway, and they stay without a problem. For reference, I’m 5’4 and 170, definitely curvy through hips, thighs, and butt, and typically wear a size 10 in pants. Also, worth noting, I like my jeans to fit more snug than loose.

Awesome Jegging! Unknowingly I purchased another jegging by the same brand. That item is the "Women's High Waisted Stretchy Pull-On Skinny Denim Jegging & Bermuda Shorts" also by Jvini. I bought a size large in both. These are definitely my favorite, though! I'm crossposting the review from the other ones here since my views are the same on both. Quality is the same and the fit is the same on both: I was super wary of these leggings after reading so many mixed reviews. I am SO glad I decided to purchase though! The only place I've been able to find jeggings I like is at Wal-Mart and they stopped selling good quality ones in my area. I normally wear a size medium 6-8 jegging, but went with the size large in these after seeing so many people say to size up. For reference I am 5'5" and 160 LBS, a little overweight for my frame but not plus size or petite by any means. That was a good choice! While the size large is still a little tight in the upper thigh area when first putting them on, they definitely stretch out as you go through your day. By the end of the day they are still form fitting and loose enough to not have to pull up every five seconds and I love that! Some jeggings have a weak elastic at the top that doesn't hold form all day. These don't! It's a thick elastic waist that comes right up over my belly button and aren't too tight around that area at all. The quality of these are super thick and not thin, which is what I like because my thighs rub together and end up making holes in the crotch area over time. Don't forsee that happening to these any time soon.

Sure get em can’t go wrong🤩 Okay so I’m 200lbs 5’6” and I first ordered the XL light washed, when I first took them out of the bag I thought “oh my this isn’t going to fit” but it did stretch enough to pull em up, I had mixed feelings about it the material is actual jean yes it does stretch but not too much it’s thick, I have jeggings I bought from Walmart but I have to buy a pair every 3-4 months because I wear them a lot and they rip and wear fast... these look like they’ll hold up for sure! They do go up past my belly so that great because it hides my tummy rolls... they definitely are true to size so if I was 5-7lbs any heavier they wouldn’t fit lol anyways I’m starting to like them so I bought the dark wash too 😊 16-20$ is definitely worth the Jean def can’t go wrong w the price. Hopefully my review and pictures help💕

GET THESE JEANS!! I ordered these jeans because they were recommended by a youtube influencer and I have 0 regrets! They are a fairly heavy denim with just the right amount of stretch to them. I'd consider them to be mid to high waisted since the closure is right below my belly button. However, if I hike them up a bit they sit right above the belly button. I washed and dried them with little to no shrinkage. Length-wise they're slightly cropped on me, and fall right above the ankle. These jeans are honestly SO versatile because they look great with ballet flats, heels, tennis shoes, AND suede or leather ankle boots! Since I couldn't decide, I ordered them in two colors (iced blue light wash & medium blue vintage), and I love them both!! For reference, I'm 5'6 and 170 lbs. I ordered size 12 and they fit great. Highly recommend. You CANNOT beat the price for this level of quality in my opinion!!

BUY IT but size down I read review after review before purchasing this shirt. Almost everybody said this shirt was HUGE, whether it was the sleeves or the length of the shirt everybody commented on on the “oversized” being way oversized. Taking that into account, I did want a huge baggy shirt that was down to my thighs. I am 5’1, 160lbs with a short torso and a little on the thicker side. I usually wear a size medium, situationally a size large. I decided to order a XS. I pulled it out of the bag and it looked small, and I was kind of annoyed because everyone said this shirt was massive and it didn’t look massive..it looked like an XS. It didn’t immediately feel as soft as everyone was saying either. I tried it on anyway and fell in love. The size was perfect, it is soft and super stretchy. I isn’t super long, if I pull it down, it fall to just below my buttcheeks. The neck line isn’t low but isn’t too high. It’s not thick but it’s not see through. I think you could dress it up or dress it down. I am pleasantly surprised and am very happy with this shirt, especially for the price. I haven’t washed it yet, so we shall see.

Decently cute and comfortable I am 5 foot three, 113 pounds for reference and I ordered these jeans in the dark blue denim cat scratch color in a size small with a 28 inch inseam. Initially, I thought they would be way too long for me, but once I tried them on, I decided I ordered just about the right fit. I like them, but I can’t say that I love them. They are comfortable and cute enough, but I am not sure that I’ll be reaching for these over some of my more beloved pairs of jeans.

Super Cute and Comfortable! I've kind of been wearing these nonstop since they've come in. I'm 5'2 and these hit me a little lower than the image but not enough where it bothered me. I usually wear shoes with a bit of a platform so truly this isn't an issue at all. This doesn't drag (even without shoes) and is a great length. The material is super super stretchy, especially around the waist, but still feels soft and breathable. It's fitted around your waist and behind (not in a bad way, very nice and flattering), then loosens a bit for the straight leg look. It's a good in-between for those who aren't all-in on the baggy look but also don't want skinny jeans/jeggings. These fit super nicely and comfortably, are breathable, and look great. I do wish the front had some kind of fake button situation so it isn't obvious you're wearing stretchy pull on pants, but that's my only qualm.

It's fitted around your waist and behind (not in a bad way, very nice and flattering), then loosens a bit for the straight leg look. It's a good in-between for those who aren't all-in on the baggy look but also don't want skinny jeans/jeggings. These fit super nicely and comfortably, are breathable, and look great. I do wish the front had some kind of fake button situation so it isn't obvious you're wearing stretchy pull on pants, but that's my only qualm.

Millennial Approved Jeans!! Millennial approved! These jeans fit perfectly - just like in high school. I’m 5’4 and 150lbs; I bought a medium (definitely more curvy fitted) and I like how they look and fit. The length is a little too long, but that’s something I always struggle with unless I find a petite fitted size somewhere. 1000% love these and recommend!!

Ok pants but not for the price I love the brand which was why I purchased these. However the jeans came with defects. Zipper did not work properly I had to soap them up to get it to work. At the bottom of the jeans one flare clearly had a piece of fabric missing/cut from it. Size was as far as chart went it was fine. They do have some stretch and are comfy. Length is long so I had to cuff them but I am also short so expected that. I purchased a large and an XL to be sure I would have a pair gang fit. The larges for great for reference I am 5’ 1” weigh: 173 and I have a bit of a belly. For the price they defiantly could have been better. They will stay in my closet for throw on and go pants but not for dressing up like my others by the same brand.

My New Fave Jeans! Great for petites! All my short girlies need to buy these jeans! I’ve already bought five pairs in two sizes. For reference, I’m 5 feet tall and 157lbs. I normally wear a size ten. I sized up per recommendation and the 12’s fit great. Since I’ve been losing weight I also bought some size 10’s. The 10’s are more snug, but still look good, and the amount of stretch in them, makes them very comfortable. Photo is of me in size 12’s. The models show the cuffs as floods on taller people. Unless you’re super thin, I think floods look awful, so I love that these fit me right where they should. And you cannot beat this price. I’ve received many compliments on these jeans so I’m also happy that I’ve bought them in five colors! Buy these jeans, my petite ladies! You won’t regret it.

Great pants in remarkable color Really like the fabric and fantastic color. Fabric has some stretch to it and is medium weight which will be perfect for spring. I am 5’4 and 125 lbs and size 2 fit perfectly. It is high waisted which I like. Hem hits right at the ankles and perfect with boots or heels. Great value for the price. Pairs well with a business jacket which I wanted for a mid business look. Haven’t washed yet so will see how that goes but biked to work with it and wore all day and still held the stretch.

Unattractive Placement of Fading Great fit on my 5’2” 170 lb. The waist is high and snug leading to an attractive look for front tucking. Fabric is very thick but stretchy. Maybe because it’s still warm here, but I’m not a fan of the thick fabric, even for winter. It feels bulky. Also, fading on the front thighs is not at all flattering as I feel it widens the widest part of my athletic legs. I’m thicker in the waist but not large in the rear and thighs. While I wear denim to my job and style jeans with blazers, cardigans ,and crisp white shirts, fading in those areas is too casual for my tastes. Pockets and hem are lightly sanded but not frayed, which makes for a jean that’s versatile for work or out and about.

Nice and stylish for a good price! I really like these! I normally wear between an 8 and a 10. I am bigger in the hips, so I ordered the 10. They have a nice amount of stretch to them, but the waist is a little tight. The length fits me well- I’m about 5’3 and they hit right at the top of my shoe. The material is soft and not too heavy. The style is trendy without being over the top. Nice jeans for what I paid- a lightening deal!

High waisted and wide legged love! I’m really impressed with these jeans. I just tried them on so I haven’t washed them or experienced how they hold up, but the fit is amazing and I like the amount of stretch. I’m 5’3 so the length is perfect for me, even a tiny bit high-water, which I prefer. I’m about 150 lbs and went up a size from my usual to a 10 which is perfect. These even arrived earlier than expected!

Short girls get them! These are the perfect length for a 5 foot short girl. I measured myself, got the size 4 and it’s perfection! I actually got 2 pairs. I was between the lightest blue (glacier I think), and this one (Sky blue), they were almost this same. I like the this shade a better being a hair darker. So love these. Stretchy and hit a chunky loafer or low heel perfect.

Super flattering & comfortable I get compliments on these jeans all the time. Super flattering and even more comfortable with just the right amount of stretch. I’m wearing a size 12 and I’m 5’8 and 180 lbs.

I ordered the light blue jeans in a size L, (I normally wear a size 29 but since these are supposed to be baggy I figured the L would work best). I tried them on and they actually fit great. If they stay the same or even shrink a tiny bit they’ll be excellent. If they stretch out over time like some jeans do, then they’ll probably be a little too big I think and not in a baggy good type of way. I’m 5’5” and they’re a good length; a little bit shorter than most of my jeans but perfect for sneakers without dragging. You could even wear these with high sandals or heels if you wanted to show-off some fabulous shoes. These jeans would really showcase them. I really liked the fabric and the slight stretch. They are SO comfortable and soft. I also like the two buttons above the zipper instead of the typical one button. The color was great too. Now, it says they are mid-rise, and perhaps it’s a size thing or just a preference of mine, but when I wore these mid-rise, there was too much fabric down there. If I wore them high-waisted the rise fit my body well and comfortably. I just don’t like the look of jeans that have that excess fabric in between the legs because I think it just looks strange (same with if the fabric is too tight- not a good look). So, I’ll probably wear these high-rise (or semi-high-rise). Maybe I should have ordered the Medium I really prefer jeans that are loose around the waist so maybe the true test is to see if they shrink or not after washing them. After looking at the photos that I’m adding to the review, I think it would look better with the back-pockets a bit closer together (more like the model’s picture). They’re still keepers due to the comfort, the unique style and unique length. I like jeans that are a bit unique or different than all the other pairs and these fit that bill too. Style Tips from Y2K: I was a teen in the 90’s and sometimes I would wear sweatpants or boxer shorts under my baggy jeans (lots of girls did this). We made sure the waistband underneath stuck out a bit so you could see it. We would pair that with a tight crop top or tube top. I would also be using a shoelace as a belt or some type of chain belt. Finally my pager would be clipped onto my front pocket and an attached chain would then be clipped onto my front belt hook. I’d have sneakers on. I’d also be wearing a dark burgundy lipstick with the outer lip liner visible since it was slightly darker. How fun it is to reminisce and also see a style go round the second time!

Good quality, very comfortable and I like that these are 70% Cotton! These are the "009-mid Blue" style. They seem to be true-to-size. I am 5'5" and roughly 117lbs, and these fit well in the waist. Note: they are more high-waisted than mid- waisted. The length with the unfinished bottoms and for my height, look better with heels (for me, in my opinion and personal style). They are slightly too short for me to wear with flats, but they do look cool with casual flip flops. I like that these are 70% cotton, and they have a nice medium weight to them. The drawback for me is that they just aren't flattering on my body type - most especially from the back. I love the front pockets and color of these jeans, and the quality seems to be very good.

High fashion, a cut at the ankle if you are tall These jeans have some really nice detailing. The pockets are at a slant and it gives them a cool style. The jeans are wide leg. I’m 5’6” but have a high waist and if I pull these up to be high waisted, they are pretty short. I know lately women have been wearing pants shorter to the ankle, so I’m okay with it. It just takes some adjusting because I’m 42, and when we wore baggy jeans as young kids, they needed to be long. I love the black wash.

Cute design and good quality, but runs small These jeans have a nice material quality and I really like the overall design. However, the sizing runs small. I ordered a large, my usual size, but they are very tight at the waist and fit more like high-rise jeans than mid-rise. Because of that, they create a bit of a bulge that isn’t very flattering. The length was good for me at 5'3", and would likely work well for someone taller too. I’d recommend sizing up to get a more comfortable fit.

Good fit and style, but those bows though At 5'9" and about 150 lbs, these size 8 jeans fit well and the wide-leg style looks good. The length works for my height, which is always a win since a lot of jeans end up being too short. The fit through the waist and hips is flattering without being too tight or too baggy.The only thing I'm not a fan of is the bows down the side. They're a bit much and feel like an unnecessary design choice that limits when and where you'd actually wear these. If they were plain without the bows, these would be perfect everyday jeans. As it is, they're still wearable but the bows make them feel more trendy than timeless.The denim quality seems decent for Forever 21 and the price point. If you like the bow detail or don't mind it, these are solid wide-leg jeans. If you're like me and could do without the extra decoration, they're still good enough that the fit makes up for it.

Adorable jeans that my teenage daughter already stole from me. I thought I'd ordered these light blue with pink bows wide legged jeans for myself, but silly me, I have teenaged daughters in the house, who are obsessed with oversized jeans. They are well sewn, really sturdy, the bow pattern is consistent. There were no stray strings I had to cut off. The buttonhole was large enough and the zipper glided easily. The pockets are great sized (tiny or fake pockets in women's wear is a pet peeve of mine), but these ones will easily fit cellphone, keys, etc. I ordered a size 14 because I am in between sizes and I wanted to order up for comfort. These were plenty big on me. I'm also 5'7", and they were plenty long as well (I could easily wear platform boots with these jeans). They say low rise on the tag but the crotch would be too low if I wore them that low and they fit fine at the mid waistline. They feel really nice on. The design is comfortable, and the jean material is fairly soft (not stiff or rigid), but there isn't any stretch to them. Due to the oversized design, it's not the most booty flattering, but it does have a nice shape at the top back. I'm wearing them in the first pics (size 12-14, 5'7" 153lbs), and my daughter is wearing them in the last pics ( size 6, 5'9", 135lbs). She said the only thing she doesn't love is how when she wears them low-rise, the front looks very long (they're also ginormous on her though). The price is pretty typical for this type of quality and cuteness.

The design is comfortable, and the jean material is fairly soft (not stiff or rigid), but there isn't any stretch to them. Due to the oversized design, it's not the most booty flattering, but it does have a nice shape at the top back. I'm wearing them in the first pics (size 12-14, 5'7" 153lbs), and my daughter is wearing them in the last pics ( size 6, 5'9", 135lbs). She said the only thing she doesn't love is how when she wears them low-rise, the front looks very long (they're also ginormous on her though). The price is pretty typical for this type of quality and cuteness.

Well stitched bows I got these pants for my 15 year old daughter and she loves them. She likes the color, the wide legs, and the bows. The only thing that isn't great is we got the size 4 (she typically wears either 2 of 4) and they are too big in the waisted. So if you are between sizes, definitely go down. One great thing about these is the stitching of the bows is great and looks like it should hold up well. Overall, these jeans are worth the cost and very in style for teens.

Good quality jeans, ribbons sewn in. Great jeans! They are a good denim without being too heavy or too thin. The ribbons are actually sewn in and are a pretty, satiny pink. They're a little small for me right now since they didn't offer them in bigger sizes, which would have been nice, but when I can fit my big self into them, they will be one of my new favorites. I am 5'8 and the length is perfect with a size 14 as it's just above the ground. I have a feeling they run a little small. Made in China on the tag.

Love everything about them, but the rhinestone pockets are too young for me These are actually really nice quality. At under $27, it is an incredible value for the money. I love the color of the denim, and the quality of the denim is nice also. It is not too thick and not too thin, and it is very soft. I am 5'4", and I think the length is perfect, so it's possible they might be too short for anyone taller. I am around 175 (I gained some weight recently), and I have an hourglass shape, with a bit of a tummy and also a bit of a booty. I am usually in-between a 12 and 14, and I'm very glad I ordered the 14. They are very stretchy and comfortable. And the flare leg is so cute and flattering, in my opinion. I've never been a fan of skinny leg jeans for my body type, but I also like it or be form fitting on my booty. The problem is this: I didn't notice the rhinestone design on the back pockets when I ordered this. I love most everything about the jeans, but this feature just isn't right for my 40-year-old self. I might end up returning them, unfortunately. They are very Y2K, and I can pull off some of those styles. But the rhinestones are just too juvenile for myself. I didn't take a star away for this, because it was totally my fault for not noticing this obviously displayed feature when I ordered them. And to be honest, the rhinestone design is actually quite beautiful. And it looks like they might be durable and stay on over time. I just feel bad I missed this when ordering.

Comfy jeans, true to size, might be a little long These Flair denim high-rise jeans remind me of the jeans I used to wear in the '90s. I wasn't sure what size I needed, because the size charts weren't very . For reference, I stand about 5'3" and I weigh between 135 and 145 at any given time. I wear a size 6 in most women's clothes. My reference point of Forever 21 was from my time working in the mall and I thought I remembered the clothes running a little bit smaller, so I ordered the size 8. When I got the jeans I was a little bit confused, because the tag says that it's a low-rise boot cut but that didn't look like the pants that I was holding in front of me so I tried them on. Anyway. They are definitely high-rise and they're definitely flares. I think they just got mismarked. There's some spandex in these pants and so the size 8 fits fine but I should have gone with a size 6 because after you wear it for a little while it loosens up like denim does. The high-rise for me is definitely a high-rise; It goes over my belly button. I should probably mention that I am very short-waisted so this part didn't surprise me. They were a little long, however. I'll probably need to get them hemmed. One thing I did notice is that the description states that the pockets have stars on them. You know for a little bit of whimsy but the pockets on my jeans had no decoration of any sort. They were buttoned down flaps for the back pocket which takes some getting used to since. I typically will put my cell phone in my back pocket and I found myself trying to do that multiple times without success. Apart from these few small points, these jeans were very comfortable so my only suggestion is probably stick to the size you normally wear cuz they seem to be pretty true to size.

Decent quality jean for the price point, stretch and fit decent, size up. I am 5'8" and 170lbs with a 31" waist. Normally I would order jeans in a size 10 or 12. F21 always runs small so I ordered these in a size 14 and they fit pretty well. The inseam was long enough for me, which is always nice since I have a 31" inseam. Shorter girls would struggle with these. These have a nice high rise in the front but do gap a little in the back for me. As they day does on they do stretch out and I needed to put a belt on midway through the day because I found they were slipping.The denim is a cheaper, stretchy, thin feel. Pretty typical for what I expect from F21 and at this price point. I do like the stretch which makes them more comfortable. The pockets were a good size in the front and back, which was a bonus.After washing I didn't notice any shrinkage, so that was nice. I am not a huge fan of the flap over pockets because it's hard to get my phone in and our of my back pocket, but that is a style choice I guess. The flare leg is well done and flattering.I thought these were decent jeans for the price point and would recommend if you are looking for this style at a lower price point.

Three times is a charm they say. I bought two pairs no issues. Third one, boom! lol I decided to downsize by one and these fit much better. The light ones seemed a little baggier and didn't look appealing at all. I wore them one day and when I got home from work, in the washer and dryer they went and then into my "give a way" bag. One of the customers did state that the darker color fit a bit smaller. And they do. Bring on the shorter side of the scale. These hit just at the top of my shoe maybe a tad longer. Which was fine. I often wear anything like that a bit longer so it covers my whole ankle and even slightly touches the ground. I'm 5'2" 115 lbs. medium build and the size 3 fit well. I didn't see where the jeans wrinkled by the end of the day. If so, it must have been pretty minimal. They do have stretch to them as well. Not a lot, just enough. UPDATE: A month later I bought another pair of these jeans but the darker shade with no holes in them. Well, I was kind of wondering why these particular ones were marked down which got me to buy them nearly asap. Come to find out there is a hole near the belt loop. Almost looks like a mouse chew hole. Yes, I've seen enough to know lol. Normally that would be a game changer for me but seeing though I wear longer shirts and hoodies, it will not be visible. My only concern will be if it tends to get larger the more washes and wear. We'll have to wait and see. Other than that, I still love this style for us shorter women and the shade of denim is nice and not cheap looking.

Great value but ran short on me These light blue wash bootcut jeans are stretchy and the fabric is soft. The color is great for spring and summer. The distressing is light and does not go completely through the fabric so there are no holes in the jeans. Much preferred by me and the current trend.They are pretty much true to size. I am 5'-7" and 130lbs and the size 5 that I got fit well. I thought it was strange that they were sized in "junior" sizes (3, 5, 7, 9, etc.). I haven't seen those sizes in many years. Junior sizes USED to be slimmer through the waist and hips to fit teenage girls better. These fit me well in the waist and hips and I certainly do not have a "girlish" figure.The shape of the bootcut flare is very good. The thighs are slim and the flare begins at the knee. The flare is not very wide. I'm not even sure they'd work with full height boots underneath them.Unfortunately, the length was short on me. You can see from the photos that without shoes, they did not even touch the floor. Nor are they short enough to be considered cropped. For this reason, I took off one star. If you are on the petite or short size, these will work for you perfectly.For $30, I think they're a great value.

Sizing and Washing I ordered the Mid Blue Wash Midrise in Size 9. I’m not exactly sure why I ordered that size because my waist is 33” and my hips are 40” and the Size Chart for size 9 says 29” for the waist and 37” for the hips but I’m glad I did, as they are definitely not too small. In fact, when I first wore them (without first washing them) I found they were sliding down, as they are very stretchy. Afterwards, I machine washed them in cold water and hung them to dry and they pretty much kept their same sizing. As they were still riding a bit low on me, I machine washed them again in cold water, but this time I put them in the dryer, and I found that the fit was better. If your jeans already fit good, I would say you are definitely safe to machine wash and probably also machine dry as the shrinkage was very minimal, I saw it mostly in the length (I am 5’5” tall). They are also very comfortable. My only minor complaint, and the reason for the 1 star deduction, would be that the back pockets look a little strange as the top of the back pockets didn’t lighten as well as the rest of the jeans, my guess is that the top of the pockets were folded over and therefore did not get the bleach that the rest of the jeans got (see photo). Regarding the Ripped & Distressed details, I’m in my sixties and don’t normally wear jeans with holes in them, but these holes are nicely done because you don’t really see your skin through the holes as the Manufacturer left the white strings in place which really helps their appearance. I hope some of this insight helps!
