As they were still riding a bit low on me, I machine washed them again in cold water, but this time I put them in the dryer, and I found that the fit was better. If your jeans already fit good, I would say you are definitely safe to machine wash and probably also machine dry as the shrinkage was very minimal, I saw it mostly in the length (I am 5’5” tall). They are also very comfortable. My only minor complaint, and the reason for the 1 star deduction, would be that the back pockets look a little strange as the top of the back pockets didn’t lighten as well as the rest of the jeans, my guess is that the top of the pockets were folded over and therefore did not get the bleach that the rest of the jeans got (see photo). Regarding the Ripped & Distressed details, I’m in my sixties and don’t normally wear jeans with holes in them, but these holes are nicely done because you don’t really see your skin through the holes as the Manufacturer left the white strings in place which really helps their appearance. I hope some of this insight helps!

Stylish jeans that actually exceeded my expectations I rarely wear jeans because it's hard for me to find a pair that are flattering on my body type while also being comfortable. I have the unfortunate problem of having no hips and a pair of pancakes for a butt...literally, it's completely flat back there. And then I tend to carry my weight around my middle. So finding something that's comfortable for my waist usually looks awful on my butt. So I didn't have super high hopes for these but they actually fit better than I expected them to. I am 5'3", 145lbs and I'm normally a size M/8-10. I opted for the 8 and while there is a small amount of stretch the waist is still a little snug. But I do feel like the fit is just right everywhere else. I would say these are a true high rise, at least on someone my height. They have a good flare to the leg and I think the length is almost perfect for me. I love the front seam down the leg and the seams on the back, at the waist and above the pockets, are a nice touch. I wish the pocket placement was a little lower just because I think it's a little more flattering... particularly when you are lacking in the rear end dept. But overall I would say these are well made and stylish jeans.

Vintage Vibes with a Modern Twist These flare jeans hit all the Y2K notes—from the distressed detailing to the bootcut silhouette—while still feeling current and wearable. I ordered the Dark Blue Mid Rise in size 7 based on the size chart (I’m 5'9", 127 lbs, 27" waist, 37" hips), and they did fit true to size in terms of waist and hips. However, the length falls a little short—just something to note if you're planning to wear them with heels or boots. What Works: The fabric has a great stretch without feeling thin or overly clingy Strategic distressing makes them feel vintage, not overly ripped Mid-rise waist holds you in with a flattering cut Pairs effortlessly with fall sweaters, or blousy dress shirts, boots and belt. Fit & Finish: These jeans hug curves in all the right places and flare out perfectly for that retro bell-bottom vibe. They’re stylish enough for streetwear, but also comfy for casual weekend outings. The denim feels substantial, not flimsy—more structured than jeggings but flexible enough to move with you. Comfortable jeans! Jeans are comfortable made with cotton and 2% spandex. I personally prefer full cotton jeans, but I’m getting older and sometimes a bit of stretch helps w getting clothes to get on lol . The description says they are high waist jeans, and maybe it’s just my body but they sit right at my belly button and kind of reveals my muffin top (as you can see in the pic). I prefer high waist to cover my belly button. The inseam is perfect for me because I like to wear Vans and most jeans come with inseams that are usually way too long in length requiring me to get them tailored. These jeans are definitely not as expensive as designer jeans but they fit decently and give me the casual look i’m going for. For reference I am a 5’5” and 145lbs. I am usually a size M or between 5/7 (please excuse my dog in the 2nd pic and my dirty mirrors).

Overall, I like these jeans! My biggest complaint is the sizing. Typically I’m a size 6 regular. I’m 5’3, 125 lbs. I washed these jeans (inside out to preserve color) and put them in the dryer on medium heat. That’s how I always wash my jeans. I ordered the size 7 in these, thinking it would allow some shrinkage. These are snug, wearable, but snug. Definitely consider ordering a size up! Otherwise, they’re decently thick material, the rips add cuteness, they came as described. The pockets are a normal length (no short pockets!) and the bootcut is a traditional bootcut-not too much flare! Overall, they’re cute and I like them but I wish I had ordered another size up.

Nice jeans, a bit thin, soft, true to color, a bit long in the zipper These jeans run true to size and they're not overly long - I am 5'6" and generally wear a 29" inseam and the length on these is fine without needing to wear a heel. The bootcut flows nicely, and is flattering on my athletic build. The jeans are in a darker wash, so could be dressed up or down. The stretch is very nice on them, but the fabric is quite thin and I don't think will hold up very well through multiple washes. Besides the thin material, one design aspect makes these less attractive for me, and it is that the zipper pull is longer than on similar jeans, so you would want to cover it up with a longer shirt. For almost $36, I feel like they're a bit pricey for the quality.

Super comfy, good quality, soft fabric Love these jeans! Perfect color, not too dark, not too light. They are not pull-on but they do have stretch, I am so glad I ordered a size down because this size gives me room but aren’t baggy anywhere, they fit nice, the next size up would have been big, everywhere. I am 5’7” but have always worn long or tall inseams and these fit perfect in length but they bunch a little at the bottom. As a Levi’s wearer since I was a young teen, these are the same quality but are softer and not stiff like the ones I wore as a teen. Will be buying more! The material isn’t thin, it’s very decent. The legs are straight. I have muscular calves and these were not tight on my calves. The pockets are small so you can’t get a cell phone in them, a few keys or maybe a credit card would fit. I didn’t really notice the shaping much but they felt comfy when I put them on.

Nice jeans! I’m 5’8 and the bottoms go over my heels but the materiel isn’t too thick so usually it bunches nicely around my ankle with shoes. These are great if you wear boots or heels. The size and fit is awesome and stretchy enough not to feel rigid while also not looking baggy or too loose anywhere. I appreciate that it’s comfortable while also maintaining that high waisted look I love. Very soft and durable, I haven’t had any issue with wear or pelting where my thighs rub and no snags or hanging strings. The only issue I’ve had with wear is the little fabric logo tag in the back has worn almost completely off but I don’t mind that. I need to go ahead and remove it if I can without damaging but the color is very consistent and hasn’t faded and the tag blends in so it’s hardly noticeable.

Great price for quality Very comfortable and the length is perfect for me. I'm 5'10 with longer legs. I probably could have ordered a size smaller, but have recently lost 30lbs and wasn't quite sure of the size so I can't tell if they run big.

Very Confortable! True to size ! NOT long! 👖 In the picture : color "Blue Ice" size 12 Long ( 5'7-168cm / 200lbs-90kg) Looks better in person. Pro: - Very Stretchy - SUPER confortable - color is nice, does not fade (1.5 year of use at the time of review - washed with the outside in to prevent fading) - True to size - medium waist height (not low rise - not high rise) Cons: - Long version of this pair of jeans is NOT LONG. They fit! I’ve gain weight over the winter from yet another physical disability and even my “fat jeans” are not as comfortable. Didn’t notice that these were a form of stretch jeans - the flattering fit type . Normally, I shy away from anything ’stretch’ because they simply do not look well on me. As soon as I received them I tried them on to discover that as have to admit that not only do they fit well but the stretchability as not the cheap spandex fabric. It’s tummy smoothing but not to the point of suffocation or back pain or pinching. The fabric is a light to medium firmness around the tummy and derrière and actually comfortable (my problem areas). They’re mid-rise I think and the waist doesn’t dig into my stomach as there’s good stretch there too. Also…no “muffin top”. The legs are long and smoothing also. I would suggest to get your measurements correct to get a good fit. I really like the straight leg silhouette. My legs are more firm and muscular and these jeans don’t squeeze; no struggle getting them on. I have to cold wash them and wear them to see how well they hold up after time. So far I like them. If anything changes I’ll update my review. I bought size 18M, 34 waist, 32 length and I’m about 5’4” and 175 (+/-) lbs.

They deserve the high rating! These jeans deserve the high rating. I was skeptical, but I love them. ❤️these! I love these! I also have them in black as lighter blue. I’m 54, and totally guilty of wearing the slimmer jeans with larger/longer tunics as a staple for years now and decided to update a bit. I’m normally between and 10 and 8, so I ordered the both and the 8 fit the best. I like that it has a slight bit of tummy control across the front panel, but it’s not the kind that holds you in so tight that it causes discomfort. I’m 5’ 6” and the length is perfect for flats or some cute fall boots /shoetees, loafers, espadrilles etc. cute functional pockets. Good quality…no up uneven stitching or loose seems. I wash mine in cold and tumble dry on lowest setting and haven’t had any issues with shrinkage. The material is SLIGHTLY stretchy, but not like the kind that stretch out and look two sizes to big by the end of the day. Zipper function is good. I would consider them a medium weight…light enough to be comfortable and breathable, but just heavy enough to be perfect for fall and winter AND to not show panty lines (see through). ❤️

Wish I didn't have to return them! I'm giving 5 stars even though I'm returning them because these are great jeans! I really want them to work for me, so this review makes me sad. I'm a bodybuilder with large, athletic quads. Legs are especially big in comparison to my waist. I am 5'6, but my legs are also long for my frame. I have hardest time finding jeans, if they fit my quads they usually gap open in the waist. If they don't have any stretch and I happen to be able to get my legs in them, they look like they are cutting off my circulation. Being too short is almost always an issue too. I have made it my mission to find at least 1 really good fitting pair of jeans. I have ordered jeans from all over the internet and have a pile to be returned. Unfortunately, these have to go into that pile! I'm especially disappointed about these because, for a split second, I thought I had found the perfect jeans! They fit my waist, hips, booty, and legs like a glove! I mean, they hug everything perfectly and feel so good! The material isn't too thick or heavy and doesn't feel stiff like some jeans can. They have the right amount of stretch to add to the "hug" feel they give, but not so much that it feels like they will have that 3pm sag to them. So perfect everywhere, EXCEPT the length. They are too short and look like I'm ready for the next flood. If they didn't have little flare to them, I'd keep them and tuck them into some mid-calf boots I have. They look and feel so perfect except for where they land at my ankles. I wish they sold these in different inseam lengths and colors, I would buy every color. Just so disappointing! For reference, I am 5'6, 145 lbs. Typically a 30-31" inseam works best for me, these measure out to a 27" inseam (I didn't see that info in the listing or I wouldn't have tried them). If you're shorter, but thick legs, trust me....these are the jeans for you! You'll love them! If you have a build like mine, I need to know which jeans you're buying!

FINALLY!!! I have been searching high and low for something that reminds me of the old cargo pants I used to wear back in the 90s, and these are it. For fit... I am 5' 2", 140lbs, average size 6, with a body somewhere around an athletic build. After some trial and error, I ordered these in an 8, as the 6 was tight on my rear. Once I found my fit in these, the went from meh to great. Pros: The elastic waistband is forgiving for anyone that struggles with bloating/gut issues. The rest of the fabric has some stretch, and I'm able to pull these up and down without unbuttoning. I'd say they're high rise and they don't irritate my sensitive stomach. They can be dressed up or down and work with a variety of tops. They're even good for rigorous hiking. Also. SIX FUNCTIONAL POCKETS! Not those itty-bitty teases they call women's pockets nowadays. We are talking almost on par with men's pockets (but not quite, guess they gotta keep us humble or something). The length for me is good as well. Although I'm vertically challenged, most of my height is in my legs, so I tend to wear a regular inseam. These wash just fine, no fading or bleeding. I can't speak to drying, as I hang dry. Cons: If you are super leggy, these may be too short. If you carry weight in your midsection or rear, size up. They may (?) shrink in the dryer. Overall, I highly recommend these pants. I'm wearing the darker grey in the photos, and I've come back and ordered them in multiple other colors. I basically live in these now. And I like them as much as I do my Le.vi 724s, and that is saying something.

It’s more like a barrel cut with way too much fabric at the hips and generally around the crotch. The first photo is with the extra fabric pulled out. I am not shaped like that. I don’t think anyone is. The fit is weirdly baggy at the upper thighs and more straight cut at the calves. The back side is horrible with extra fabric in all the wrong places. They stretched out to a super baggy fit after working in them for 1 day. Not attractive or flattering.

Dokotoo casual jeans for women. Stretch denim loose and baggy wide leg jeans; raw edge hem. Color black, size 18. Light to medium weight very stretchy denim is very forgiving and super comfortable. Definitely a casual style and not one that I would personally wear “out”. I realize that this is the style and how they’re intended to be - but I still wish they had just a little more structure in the front panel area so they would give some “structure” and an appearance of neatness to the pants and not just sag out with my belly - and thus looking a tad sloppy (in my personal opinion and to my personal taste). Sizing seems to be on par with other brands. These are a size 18 and I usually buy a size 18 in most other brands as well (Carhart, Frye, and others for instance) and these fit as expected. For reference I am 5’8” tall (used to be 5’ 9.5 when younger) and weigh 200 lbs. I have an extremely long leg inseam (tall or 34”+ usually when available) and these pants are still an appropriate fit in length for me. I’m not sure if they’re meant to be worn as a shorter ankle pant (thus meant to be cuffed up perhaps?) or just meant to be whatever length they end up being lol… I’m not a huge fan of the raw hemline - but then again that’s a fashion choice and to each their own. Also, I happen to be 65 years old so I’m probably not the ideal target market for this brand, but I’m trying to branch out and try new things :) The pants achieve what they advertise themselves to be. I deducted one star not for differences in style preference but because of the unflattering nature of the fit on the front belly area. Because of the excessive amount of stretch in this area I feel it can make the jeans look a little sloppy and unattractive (so definitely harder to style around) and inevitably they may stretch out too much in this area requiring that they then need washing/drying after each wearing in order to return them to proper fitting form. So all in all good jeans that are what they claim to be (casual, baggy jeans). Accurate sizing. Nice color choices. Comfortable. Solid 4 Stars. Thanks for reading and I hope this review has been to you. Have a Blessed Day :)

I realize that this is the style and how they’re intended to be - but I still wish they had just a little more structure in the front panel area so they would give some “structure” and an appearance of neatness to the pants and not just sag out with my belly - and thus looking a tad sloppy (in my personal opinion and to my personal taste). Sizing seems to be on par with other brands. These are a size 18 and I usually buy a size 18 in most other brands as well (Carhart, Frye, and others for instance) and these fit as expected. For reference I am 5’8” tall (used to be 5’ 9.5 when younger) and weigh 200 lbs. I have an extremely long leg inseam (tall or 34”+ usually when available) and these pants are still an appropriate fit in length for me. I’m not sure if they’re meant to be worn as a shorter ankle pant (thus meant to be cuffed up perhaps?) or just meant to be whatever length they end up being lol… I’m not a huge fan of the raw hemline - but then again that’s a fashion choice and to each their own. Also, I happen to be 65 years old so I’m probably not the ideal target market for this brand, but I’m trying to branch out and try new things :) The pants achieve what they advertise themselves to be. I deducted one star not for differences in style preference but because of the unflattering nature of the fit on the front belly area. Because of the excessive amount of stretch in this area I feel it can make the jeans look a little sloppy and unattractive (so definitely harder to style around) and inevitably they may stretch out too much in this area requiring that they then need washing/drying after each wearing in order to return them to proper fitting form. So all in all good jeans that are what they claim to be (casual, baggy jeans). Accurate sizing. Nice color choices. Comfortable. Solid 4 Stars. Thanks for reading and I hope this review has been to you. Have a Blessed Day :)

Cute Jeans, But the Length Fits a Little Odd on Petites These wide-leg jeans are really cute and very comfortable, with a nice amount of stretch that makes them easy to move in. I love the high-waisted fit and the relaxed, baggy leg—it gives that effortless, laid-back vibe without feeling sloppy. My only issue is the length. I’m 5'2", and they hit at kind of an awkward spot on my leg—not quite full length, not quite cropped. It’s not a deal-breaker, but it doesn’t look as intentional as it probably would on someone closer to average height. On taller women (or even just a few inches taller than me), I think the silhouette would fall much more naturally and be more flattering. The denim itself feels nice quality, the color is pretty, and the pockets are roomy. If you're petite, just be prepared that the length may not be perfect—but for everyone else, these are a fun, comfy pair of wide-leg jeans.

Great petite jeans!!!! 5"2, 28in waist, 35in hips, 125 lbs I've never found jeans that fit me without cuffing at my height but these hit right at the ankles. I initially thought the stitches of the pant looked weird, but upon wearing, somehow really gives me some shape. I also appreciate that these make my butt pop a tad and actually hug my waist (many jeans do not do this). They do feel in between leggings and real jean so it has both stretch and form. I got these in size 4 and I would say very true to size as I'm usually a small/medium in bottoms. Ps. I am the kind to never wear jeans and these are one of the two pairs I actually have!!!

I ordered EMMIOL Women Bootcut Denim Jeans. I ordered them in the Black color, in a Size 6. I am about 5’4” tall, weigh around 125lbs, they fit very very nicely! The Fabric is 70% cotton/28% polyester/2% spandex, and MACHINE-WASHABLE! The stretching ability of these Jeans is awesome! They are considered Mid-High Waisted Jeans, (they are more High-Waisted fitting on me, which I really like). They have awesome flared legs, definitely a Boot-Cut, so they fit over my boots perfectly. They have 2 front pockets and 2 back pockets that are usable, which I truly am glad. So they are not only comfortable but practical also. They have a faded look in the front on both legs and on the back pockets. Kind of gives them a ‘Old-Worn-Look! OVERALL….. AWESOME! And AWESOME AGAIN! Soooo comfy, breathable, stretchable! Quality is GOOD! Price is FAIR!!

Tight in the right places with a touch of class These are super cute! I actually had to return the 36L because they were too long! Which I’ve never had happen. For reference I’m 5’9 170lbs and the 28w34l fit perfect. The wrangler logo on the back is the perfect classic touch also. Flare jeans aren’t really my thing since they’re usually too short but as you can see these touch the floor! The only reason I’m leaving 4 stars instead of 5 is because I just got them and haven’t seen yet if they hold their shape. That’s my biggest issue with 100% cotton jeans. I’ll try to back to see how they fit after a full days wear.

Best jeans ever!!!! Amazing quality and amazing price. I am 5’4” 145 lbs, 9 months pp after my second C-section and was just praying these fit. My prayers were answered because these fit like a glove. Perfect length with low heeled boots. I am obsessed with these jeans and so happy with my purchase. 10/10 recommend!!!
Fits perfectly These are perfect! I’m 5’2 115 pounds and got a size 4. A little bit long but with heels they fit perfectly.

Nope. These did not fit anything close to anticipated. Quite hilarious to try on.. I’m 5’4 125lbs with athletic build (more muscle than skinny). Ordered a size 0 which seems insane bc 0 should be the smallest of the lower bodies. Which I am not. They are huge. I’m small and fit, but I don’t have the tiniest waist, any hips, and working on backside curve (build of a lower case L). The waist is huge, the back and hips fit decently (ish), and the fit of the thighs (which is where I have my thunder) is extremely loose. Worst of all, they are about 8” too long (hyperbole but pic is shown). I’m not sure what human would fit these. But recommend for the 3 humans out there that are 5’10 with 23” thighs, 34” hips and 33” waist. Who identify as a size 0. I’d recommend ordering if you’d like a laugh.

Size runs roomy. Comfy 240 lbs 5’2” ordered Petite 20 womens; these jeans run roomy so if you are between sizes, size to the smaller end. They are very comfortable. Very comfortable sitting in them and they do stretch out and partly why sizing down would work for most. I have heavy thighs and these are good. Color is standard jean color. Recommend.

The black pair deserves all 5 stars and even more! I am highly recommend it!

Every pair are completely different in material makeup Be careful ordering these!!!! I LOVE THEM, BUT... not only is the material breakdown wrong for each style, but they are all different. Some are 26% rayon, while others are only 11% which then were small on me. I ordered 9s and 11s and had to buy around 13 pairs to find TWO that fit great. So that was extremely frustrating. I wish they had the material breakdown correct. Also, I ordered a size 9 and size 11 in the SAME PANT (Kelly) and they are completely different colors AND completely different material makeups!!!! How the eff does that make sense. Pictured with me wearing a pair are the Riverton jeans which were absolutely perfect. I am 5'7" and 165 pounds for reference. I rely on reviews when making purchases so I hope this helps someone.

Actually fit My stats: almost 70y/o, 5’4”, 112lbs, waist 27”, hips 38”, inseam 29”. I bought size 6 with a 30” inseam. These fit my body type well. The fabric is not very thick like jeans with a higher % of cotton. I’m assuming like all jeans with a synthetic/cotton fabric that they will stretch out as the day progresses. I will wear an elastic belt to keep them fitting as this occurs. They are standard height waist, not high waisted as described. The waistband comes even with my hips. I haven’t washed them yet but I think they are very nice. If they shrink, it’s nothing that a few squats won’t fix by stretching them out. I am happy with my purchase.

My Go To Jeans, Very Pleased! Read All - I am very happy with how these jean fit. I was not sure what size to order, as my weight has been fluctuating. I usually were a size 12 in most pants, I weight 164 and I am 5'5". When I looked at the size chart and checked with another pair of wranglers I already own and still fit at size10W, I went with the 10W 30L. Length is perfect for my built however the fit is pretty snug. What I mean is it feels like I'm between sizes. The jeans are made with a stretchy material and give enough (At least for me) to get into. Maybe I should have ordered a 12W but these look flattering and if I lose 5-10lbs they would be the perfect fit. Side note-I hold my weigth mostly in my hips and some in my gut. I'm not sure how they wash up or if they shrink in wash. Although they are on the snug side, they are comfortable. I have attached some photo and hope this helps with your decision.

Not for the long legged girlies I really wanted to love these jeans because they hit all the marks for a cute, semi-cropped flared look, but the proportions were a struggle for my height. At 5'10" with long legs, the "cropped" cut felt a bit too short; they ride up significantly on my calves whenever I sit down. Additionally, as someone with a long waist, the high-rise fit only reached my belly button rather than sitting higher up. While the fabric has a really nice stretch and is very comfortable to move in, I did encounter a very strong dye smell right out of the package. Even after a wash, it took a few weeks of "airing out" before the scent fully dissipated. The color held well after the initial wash being a black jean but time will tell if they fade more. Ultimately, these are stylish and well-made, but I wouldn't recommend them to my fellow "tall girlies." If you are a few inches shorter, these would likely be an A+ fit, but for me, they just needed a little extra length to be perfect.

Super Cute Boutique-Quality Styling and Fit - without the High-End Price Tag! These wide-leg black jeans are absolutely on point for current denim trends. The high-waisted fit, front and back pockets, subtle distressing, and relaxed wide-leg cut give them that effortless, cool-girl vibe that works year-round. Trendy Queen continues to be one of those brands I can rely on for consistent sizing and modern styling. The fabric blend is excellent - soft, breathable, and just stretchy enough. The cotton base keeps them comfortable, while the spandex gives enough flexibility to move easily and still hold their shape after wearing them all day (no sagging or bagging, which is huge). The denim is a typical mid-weight - not too heavy, but also not thin like a chambray. They are a solid but SOFT denim! I’m typically a size 2–4 in jeans and ordered a 4, and the fit is spot-on in the waist and hips. The only thing I’m slightly undecided about is the length. On my 5'6" frame, it lands in that in-between zone - not quite cropped, not quite full length. That said, it’s an easy fix and honestly adds styling flexibility: I may hem them to an ankle length or cut a raw edge for a more casual, street-style look. These jeans are incredibly versatile and easy to dress up or down - pair them with sneakers, boots, crop tops, or cozy knits. Comfortable, flattering, and very wearable, they’re a great addition to any denim wardrobe. Another solid win from Trendy Queen!

High‑Waisted Wide‑Leg Jeans — A Hidden Gem for Short Ladies As a short lady who constantly battles the “too long, too baggy” jean struggle, I have to say these jeans were an unexpected win. At 5'2", finding a pair I can wear with flats or tennis shoes without hemming feels almost impossible yet these hit the perfect length right out of the package. I have an average‑length torso but shorter legs, so online shopping is usually a gamble, but these surprised me. They even work with a small heel, which feels like a bonus. The high‑waisted cut has become one of my favorite styles because it’s genuinely forgiving, especially if you’re carrying a little extra around the midsection. These trouser‑style jeans create such a clean, flattering silhouette. The fabric has that ideal balance: structured enough to smooth and slim, but still very much real denim and not flimsy, nor stiff, just right. It hides imperfections without feeling heavy. They come in several colors, but I went with black for the winter season, and it’s been the perfect versatile staple. I’d say order your true size since there’s a bit of stretch; if you prefer a more relaxed, drapey look, sizing up could work too. Honestly, I haven’t stopped wearing them since they arrived. They’ve quickly become one of those rare pieces that make you feel comfortable, confident, and stylish all at once — and that’s worth sharing. I hope the pictures help you decide.

Good quality material, runs a bit small and short For reference, I am 5’5, 119 pounds. In pants/jeans I wear a small or US size 2. I ordered these in a size 4, the smallest size offered. I do buy some of my jeans in size 4 when I want them to be baggy or a looser fit. My first impression when I took these out of the package was there was no way these would fit me. The waist looks so tiny. They do fit me, but I personally think they fit more like a size 2 than a size 4. I wish they were longer too. At 5’5 they sit at an odd length on me. They are not as long on me as they are on the model. I will not be drying these jeans in the dryer because I don’t want them to shrink at all in length. I did measure the waist of the pair of jeans I received and it isn’t quite the 26.6 inches as stated. The jeans are a soft stretchy material made up of cotton, polyester, rayon, and spandex. The material is good quality! They do feel like real stretch jeans which I appreciate, they are not simply pants that look like jeans. The material is nice and thick, not flimsy or thin. The jeans are soft and comfortable to wear. With as tiny as the waist looked to me on first impression, the jeans are stretchy and I can comfortably wear them. As the day goes on the waist stretches out more. The style of the jeans is cute. The front pockets do not offer much in actual usability, they are more decorative than practical. The cuffs at the bottom flare out slightly and have that wide trendy cuff. The back pockets are a decent usable size. The jeans have a button fly and zipper, all which work as they should, no flaws. While baggy is in the title of the jeans, these jeans are not baggy at all. They are straight leg with a bit of flare at the bottom. These sit right at the belly button on me. There are a few seams that are uneven and have thread gathered in places, but that shouldn’t be noticeable from a distance. The price is reasonable as far as jeans go. The quality is good, especially compared to other jeans I’ve ordered from Amazon. Pay close attention to the size guide. I think they run small compared to other jeans I own from name brand clothing stores.

Thank you Wrangler! Wrangler replaced my jeans, they have decided to stand behind their product!!!! Out of the seven pairs of Wrangler jeans I’ve bought, only TWO haven’t split, ripped, or betrayed me like a backstabbing ex. One pair actually blew out on the first wear—ripped clean down the leg like I was attacked by a bobcat (spoiler: I wasn’t). The Sadie jeans? Cute name, but unless you keep your shirt tucked like you’re reporting for duty, those grippy waistband things will pinch the living daylights out of your skin every time you move. Surprise! Instant exfoliation. On the plus side, these jeans are actually long enough for us 35” inseam gals to wear heels. That’s a small miracle. But here in small-town farm country, where I’m actually using my jeans to work—not just pose in a pumpkin patch—I don’t have time to wash ‘em, drive to FedEx, and send them back like I’m returning fine china. Wrangler’s solution? “Just send them in.” My solution? Calculate the fuel cost and just wait for a sale. I must be a sucker, because I keep buying them. Why? Because they fit like a dream. They hug the right spots, make my legs look like a 90s denim ad, and don’t stop mid-ankle like some toddler-length trend. If only the low rise was actually low rise (and not mid-rise in disguise), I’d be throwing stars like it’s a rodeo. So here’s the summary: If you’re into jeans that feel amazing, look fantastic, and then explode at the seams without warning, these are for you. If you enjoy that rush of will my butt be covered today or nah? and like your denim with a side of personal injury from waistband claws, then saddle up, baby. But if you want durability, actual workwear grit, and not just fashion farm cosplay, Wrangler’s got some serious patching up to do. Wrangler, fix your quality. Your fits are gold. Your fabric is chaos. Update 05/22/2025- looks like the right leg stitching is about to let go like the left leg. Just added that last photo

I read a lot of reviews and am usually pretty solidly a 10 with about a 32” inseam, but I ordered the 8/32 They fit amazingly- no snug spots, not even in the waist, which can be troublesome for me. The legs are lean, but loosely so- not too short, not dragging on the floor. The waist doesn’t gap, even when I’m squatting and messing around with my chicken coops. I’m pretty flat in the butt, so use my photos for reference if that’s you too. But these fit like jeans used to fit me. Size down for them to fit like they do in the pictures. Great jeans. Love that they’re Levi’s, love that they feel like sweats without even washing them. I’ll update is anything changes after they’re laundered!

I got these Levi's straight leg relaxed jeans to wear on errands and casual appointments. They're comfortable, not too loose and not too tight. I'm pear-shaped so i have to buy jeans 1 size larger to accommodate my hips. This does create a gap at the small of my back (this us always a problem for me). The waist is a bit lower than i would like. However, I plan to take them in myself because I got them for a ridiculous low price and tailoring will make them look high-waisted as is the trend. These are straight leg and long enough for my 5'7" frame. I weigh 140lbs and ordered a 30 (size 8). They're comfy like mom jeans and they're neither too loose or too tight. Just right! Overall, this is a decent pair of jeans.

Great fit for curvy, pear-shapes I’m 5’4” and my weight fluctuates between 170–180 lbs. My pant size is usually 12–14 regular, and I’m a true pear shape (average leg length). I’m also in menopause, so my body has changed - a little softer, less firm, and my butt isn’t as full as it used to be. That said, these curvy fit stretch skinny jeans in the 31L (Long) fit perfectly! The material is solid and structured - not flimsy or “jegging-like.” They look like real denim with no sagging in the crotch, and the longer length is just right. What I really love is that they don’t leave a gap in the back at the waist-they hug just right without digging into your stomach. They have enough stretch to allow room for your belly but still fit smoothly and comfortably around the waist and hips. For those of us who are pear-shaped, that’s a big deal! They even give my backside a little shape again. I was hesitant to order after reading mixed reviews, but I’m so glad I did. I even tried them on over another pair of pants and they still fit the same, so I’m not worried about shrinkage. The zipper is solid and the front pockets are deep enough (5 inches). I love them. I got soft black on sale for $20. It was worth it!

Not my favorite, but they're cute. I purchased these in a size 28 Regular and 29 Regular, both in the color (New) We Have Arrived, because I wasn’t sure what size I’d need. I’m in the size 28 in the pictures with the black top, and I’m in the size 29 in the pictures with the pink top. For sizing reference, I'm 5'3", currently around 143 lbs with measurements about 39"-30"-39" (bust-waist-hip), and I'm wearing size 4-6 in jeans or a medium in bottoms in most brands right now. Another random note but I have a shorter torso with longer legs (proportionally), aka I don't wear short length in pants and even mid-rise bottoms hit pretty high on me. I was on the fence about which size I wanted to keep because I could really wear either one. The 28 was a snugger fit without any bagginess and looked good, but it dipped down a little lower in the front, while the 29 still fit well but had some extra room in the waistband, though it had a little extra material in some spots. I ended up holding on to the 29 because it was more comfortable. I feel like Levi’s are always inconsistent in their sizing, but these fit pretty true to size, so I’d say go with whatever size you usually wear. I would recommend getting the short length if you’re my height! I don’t normally get short length, but these are long on me – I have to roll them twice to get them to sit at my ankle. Material wise, these are thin with good stretch, so don’t purchase if you’re looking for a thick, stiff denim. I don’t see these lasting for years with regular wear and washing, so not great durability. They have a zip fly, thank goodness – thanks to Levi’s, I know I hate a button fly, haha. This color is the perfect standard denim shade, too. Overall, I got these at a great price and I’m happy with them because of that. I’m probably going to grab them in another color.

I would recommend getting the short length if you’re my height! I don’t normally get short length, but these are long on me – I have to roll them twice to get them to sit at my ankle. Material wise, these are thin with good stretch, so don’t purchase if you’re looking for a thick, stiff denim. I don’t see these lasting for years with regular wear and washing, so not great durability. They have a zip fly, thank goodness – thanks to Levi’s, I know I hate a button fly, haha. This color is the perfect standard denim shade, too. Overall, I got these at a great price and I’m happy with them because of that. I’m probably going to grab them in another color.

Comfortable, large pockets, decent pair to add to your rotation! After losing a good bit of weight I am having to replace most of my wardrobe. What enticed me about these jeans is the large front pockets and the washed color / design. For reference, I am 5' tall and about 150 lbs and ordered size large. As you can see from the pictures, they were a little long on me but I expected that as most jeans are. The waist fit almost perfectly. It was slightly tight when sitting down but not horribly uncomfortable. They do have a little stretch... not so much they feel like jeggings / not so tough they feel like starched or stiff but just enough to be comfortable. Just use my references for your guide and make a size choice that's best for you. I think this is a decent value, I would buy again but in a different color for variety.

Great, slimming boot cut jeans I love how these jeans fit! I’m the one on the left in the picture. I have thin legs a flat bottom so I usually wear a slim fit jeans but these are a nice option to be more stylish. They are slightly fitted in the thigh and narrow toward the knee then start to flare out to the bottom. It’s a spinning design without being too fitted or too flared at the bottom. The jean material is stretchy with flat pockets on the front and back. I am 5’5” 145 lbs. I can wear a size 10 stretchy jeans but it’s not flattering on me (because my derrière is flat) so I usually wear a 12. I ordered these in size large which I think is pretty accurate. They are between a 10 and a 12 fit.

Comfort Meets Retro Style – Great Versatile Bootcut Jeans 5 Stars – Comfortable, Flattering, and True to Size These Luvamia women’s high-waisted boot cut jeans are a great find. I ordered a medium, and at 5’5” and 165 lbs. they fit perfectly — snug enough to be flattering but still very comfortable. The denim is soft with just the right amount of stretch, so you can move freely without losing shape. The high waist and slim-fit cut accentuate the waistline and visually elongate the legs, and the 30-inch inseam gives a nice bootcut silhouette. On me, the length runs a bit long, but nothing a pair of boots or chunky-soled shoes can’t handle. I don’t mind a slight bunch at the ankle, but if you prefer a completely smooth line, you may want to hem them. I also like the four patch pockets and the classic zip and button closure — they blend everyday practicality with a subtle retro style. These jeans are easy to dress up or down and would work well for casual outings, work settings, or even a country concert. Overall, these jeans are a versatile wardrobe staple that run true to standard US sizes. Comfortable, stylish, and flattering — I’m very happy with them.

Comfy fit, nice looking jeans. These bootcut jeans from luvamia are well made of a medium weight fabric that consists of 75% cotton, 23%polyester, and 2% elastane. The material is quite soft and has a good amount of stretch in it for ease of movement. They have 4 patch pockets. The front ones are very roomy and the back ones are average sized. The button and zipper closure both work smoothly. I ordered this pair of jeans in my regular size of XL and they fit me very comfortably. They are high waisted and thanks to the elastane there is no pinching or binding when I bend over or sit down. The are a tiny bit long for my 5' 4" height, but not enough that I will get them shortened. The classic blue color looks really nice and is easy to pair up with all types of shirts. I like them and they are a good quality pair of jeans, so I will definitely recommend them. The fit is true to size. The price (9-26-2025) of $35.99 is a fair value for them. However, there currently is a coupon available to reduce the price to a better value. I would buy these again. The quality and comfortable fit are worth it, in my opinion.

They are high waisted and thanks to the elastane there is no pinching or binding when I bend over or sit down. The are a tiny bit long for my 5' 4" height, but not enough that I will get them shortened. The classic blue color looks really nice and is easy to pair up with all types of shirts. I like them and they are a good quality pair of jeans, so I will definitely recommend them. The fit is true to size. The price (9-26-2025) of $35.99 is a fair value for them. However, there currently is a coupon available to reduce the price to a better value. I would buy these again. The quality and comfortable fit are worth it, in my opinion.

Lots of stretch. Thick jeans. Short length, belt loops with button nice addition. I ordered 10S. I really wish I would’ve gone 8S/ 152 5’2” . I’d even suggest going down 2 sizes if you’re wanting fitted pant. ( the crotch hangs low, my reason for suggesting size down). The inseam most definitely is not 30/ it is 27-28

Jeggings-the best of both worlds!! I am in love with these jeggings!! They fit me like a glove-they are so comfortable, no pinching at the waist and plenty of room in the thigh/seat. I’m happy with the mid rise waist-I have an ‘apron’ stomach/belly from my c-section deliveries and the mid rise allows for a more comfortable fit than the restrictive high rise. These jeggings have a good amount of stretch and feel great on and I don’t look like I’m stuffed in them-which is what has kept me away from jeggings for years. I really love the look the color adds-they have that perfect broken in look without looking worn out. I’m 5’3” and ordered short length for the first time-and I couldn’t be happier!! No more wet pant bottoms!! I will definitely be ordering a few more pairs.

I feel like they fit me well and I appreciate that they have short versions, especially in the plus sizes, because I'm both bigger (220 lbs) and shorter (5'2"). I like that they're skinny jeans. The only downside I've noticed is that they show lint and cat fur fairly easily, but that's also to be expected with all black pants, so I'm still giving these the full 5 stars.

Soft, Stretchy, and the Right Length, but the Waist Fit Misses the Mark These jeans have a lot going for them. The fabric feels soft and has a nice amount of stretch that makes them easy to move around in. The stitching looks clean and solid, and the overall quality feels dependable. At 5 feet 2 inches tall, it can be tough to find jeans that hit the right length, but these sit exactly where they should without bunching at the ankle. The downside is the waist. It is noticeably too loose and slips down throughout the day. I constantly find myself pulling them up. A belt does help, but it causes the waistband to fold and pinch, which ends up being uncomfortable. If the waist were more fitted, these would be an easy favorite because everything else about them works well. With the right body proportions, these could be great. If your waist and hips are close in measurement, you might not run into the same issue. If your shape is curvier through the hips with a smaller waist, the fit may be tricky. Potential shoppers should take a careful look at the sizing chart before deciding.

Great jeans but a little on the snug side I agree with other’s reviews in that these jeans are a little on the snug side on the waist. If you’re a postpartum mom like me with that extra pooch our babies have so graciously blessed us with, you may want to think about sizing up. Otherwise, you may need to pull out the faja for this one! Overall they look great and are comfortable besides the waist being snug so definitely don’t wear these to Thanksgiving or anything! The quality is great for the price. Not much stretch to them though. I would get these again but probably mainly as a motivator to lose my baby fat lol! Also I will say I appreciate that the “short” length is actually short and perfect for me as 5’3” woman. No more scrunched up pant legs or rolling the cuffs, woo! PS- if you’re a double coat dog owner like me keep in mind these are not as forgiving when it comes to lint and hair attraction as most denim tends to be so fair warning. See my photo for proof 😂

These skinny jeans are IMPRESSIVE! I have lost a lot of weight and with the cold seasons coming, I am in need of new, smaller pants. I have had good experiences with other Amazon Essentials clothing so felt confident in buying Amazon Essentials Women's High Stretch High-Rise Skinny Jeans. The first thing I noticed upon getting ready to place my order is that the size chart is incorrect - waist measurements seem significantly incorrect - but hip measurements seem correct. I took a chance based on hip measurements that I would be a size 14 short. These jeans are made of 65% Cotton, 20% Cotton Recycled, 13% Polyester Recycled, 2% Elastane, and I love that they are size inclusive, ranging from size 0 to 20 and short to long lengths. These arrived tonight and they’re super cute! I thought they looked a bit small, but they fit amazingly. I am 170 pounds with an apron belly remaining after significant weight loss and I’ll likely wear them with tunic style tops. They fit my body well. The short inseam is perfect for my height at 5’ 4”. They have some stretch, but it’s not super stretchy. They maintaining a denim jeans material vs jeggings and I wore them for several hours without them losing their shape. They aren’t super skinny - they're a bit looser than I like my skinny jeans and look much tighter in the model pics but I have a bit stomach and small legs so that might be why. Even still, I love these jeans. The seams are stitched well, and they feel high quality with a zipper and top button fly. The pockets are nicely sized. The high rise fits me perfectly above the belly button. The medium blue vintage color is perfect for me! I think they will look adorable with the cuff rolled once and ankle booties! In conclusion, these are another Amazon Essentials home run! Great quality, great fit, stylish and super reasonably priced at $28.50. The only downside is they’re slightly looser than I’d hoped and the size chart for waist measurements seems off. I noticed they just released several styles of women’s jeans and jeggings and I absolutely intend to buy more pairs!!

These jeans fit pretty well true to size but don’t really smooth you out or synch you in. They do have decent stretch and feel comfortable overall. The color isn’t as bright as I expected but I prefer a darker wash anyways so I’m ok with it. The price point was decent. Seem like they’ll hold up well for washes overall. There was no smell or issues on arrival. Allow for decent mobility even with the skinny legs design. The length hits perfect on me at 5’5 size 10. I just wish they had more compression for the lower belly as that’s my area of concern but it protrudes in these so I would only wear them with a longer shirt.

Great Jean but super long if you’re shorter but need a large or X-Large. These are a nice thick pair of Levi’s that are well-made. The fabric is nice and thick. Their zipper and seams are sewn well, they actually remind me of my Lucky jeans. They are fairly comfortable and soft but tighter on my tummy. I got them in a large so they would fit my waist and they are several inches too long. I actually think they’d be too small in my waist because I don’t really have a waist but they have a ton of stretch in the material. They fit my hips and butt, and then the legs are very roomy. So they are a true baggy jean with a whittled waste. They are just extremely long on me. If I stand on my tippy toes, they fit right to the floor, which would be perfect if I wasn’t on my tippy toes. For reference, I am 5’4 and weigh about 175 pounds and am wearing a Large.

Mars power Women's Wide Leg Jeans High These jeans are super comfortable! More like pants than any jeans I’ve ever owned. The fabric is stretchy but looks like standard denim and the cut and fit is flattering, for baggy jeans. They fit me well around the waist - I usually wear a 4/6 in jeans and went with a medium. They’re a little loose in the waist but not enough to fall down or anything. The pockets are medium size, not micro like a lot of women’s jeans but they could definitely be bigger. Length is a tad on the short side but I do have longer legs (I’m 5’4” and these don’t quite touch the ground but don’t show a ton of shoe either). They were $50 when I got them which I feel is too much but are currently priced at $35 which is very fair! They’re a very high rise, at or past belly button. I tend to be a bit sensitive to cheap metals and the button on these makes my belly break out unless I tuck in undershirt, hence the 4 stars. Not a huge deal but also not something I’ve dealt with in jeans since my teen years!

Decent jeans but needs a longer shirt These are really heavy jeans, but they are actually quite soft and comfortable. They fit pretty well but are just not super flattering. The length is just barely long on me without shoes, but that's pretty normal for me at 5'3" 160lbs. I'll probably just cut the bottoms for a more casual raw edge. The waistband is tighter, but stretchy enough to still be comfortable. I'm not a big fan of the fit of the butt, but the biggest problem is in the front packets. The pockets don't have enough jeans material, so the lining pops out the front even though I don't think they are too tight. These won't work with a shorter shirt, but they look fine with a shirt long enough to cover the front and back pockets. These are casual jeans, I don't think these would dress up very well, but they'd be good with an oversized top for a comfy fit.

On trend, quality jeans! I love these so much! I was nervous when ordering because I couldn’t find a size chart on this listing anywhere. I am 5’7” - 168 lbs and have somewhat slimmer hips and thighs for my weight but I have a mom pooch at the waist, so depending on whether the jeans are high waisted or not (and how stretchy they are), I tend to wear either a juniors’ 11 or 13, misses 12 or 14, and then either L or XL in letter sizes. Since I wasn’t sure what to expect here I went with the XL as I prefer my jeans to be a little looser than having them be a little too tight. The XL ended up being a fantastic fit! The jeans are maybe medium stretch, I wouldn’t call them “stretch denim” but they have more stretch than some jeans if that makes sense. What really impressed me is the quality of the denim. The asking price of almost $60 is a little higher than I usually pay - I try to stick under $50 for my jeans - so I was skeptical that these would be worth the price but I get it now. This is just really nice denim that looks great and is very comfortable too. I love these jeans and I think the color and style is just like it’s represented in the listing photos. As for sizing, like I said I’m usually a 12/14 or juniors 11/13 and the XL fit great on me, and XL is the most common letter size I order for denim shorts or jeans. So I think they essentially run true to size but maybe a tiny bit smaller as the XL is a comfortably snug fit, so it does fit just a tad smaller than I’d expect an XL pair of jeans to fit me. Basically I’d recommend going with your regular letter size for denim. If you’re between two sizes, pick the larger if you want to make sure they are roomy where you want them to be, pick the smaller if you don’t want that baggy look. I hope this helps!

On-Trend Barrel Jeans with Comfortable Elastic Waist These black color barrel jeans are very on trend right now and such a fun update from traditional straight or skinny styles. The knees and bottom area are faded and give that worn look. They do run slightly large, so if you’re between sizes, I would recommend sizing down. I’m 5’6” and they hit just slightly above the ankles on me, which makes them perfect for showing off loafers, mules, or sandals. The denim is thick and feels durable: definitely not thin or flimsy. Because of that, I would avoid fully machine drying them, as they may shrink. I let mine lay flat to dry to maintain the fit. They are not jeans I would wear if it were too hot outside. The stretchy elastic waistband makes them incredibly comfortable and easy to wear all day, and the pockets are surprisingly deep (a big plus). Overall, these are stylish but still practical and comfortable; a great option if you want to try the barrel trend without sacrificing comfort.

Excellent pants that fit across the sizes! We absolutely loved these pants! The fit was impressive, the size 16 fit not only a size 16-18 6'0 body very well, but also comfortably on a size 10-12 5'8 body! You loose a bit of the bagginess in the leg depending on your specific leg shape, we found that the illusion of the barrel shape was easy to achieve simply by wearing these pants, though the ankles are unable to be tightened further. The pockets are DEEP! The fabric is very stretchy and feels almost like jegging material, but it's thick, absolutely not a knock on this product, just an example. There are no zippers, or ways tighten these pants or add a belt, but the elastic does stretch very well.

Love these (and the brand) - def have some stretch I’m 60, 5’5” and 160lbs. Waist is approx 35,” hips 42” and I’m also very short waisted. Per Sidefeel’s size guide, I ordered sz 14. These fit me loosely, but I actually prefer a slightly oversized fit. However these also have some stretch to them so I feel strongly I could easily wear sz 12. I received these through Amazon Vine at a discounted price in exchange for my review, but I’ve paid full price for several jeans from Sidefeel in the recent past. It’s one of my favorite Amazon brands for denim. These are super comfortable. Not rigid - very soft and a bit on the thicker side. I’d consider these more of a Jogger style than true Barrel, but still, they make for a slimming silhouette. At the time of this review, these are slightly marked down. Due to the stretchiness, I’m considering trying sz 12 in one of the blue shades. Will update my review if I do. Definitely recommend these, but keep in mind the stretch factor.

Very comfortable! I cautiously trusted the manufacturer's size chart and decided to order one size larger than my usual size. This decision was based solely on listed waist measurements. I am glad I did. I do not like my clothing to fit snugly or tightly. These fit comfortably and I even have room if they should shrink in waist or length. The jeans are much heavier weight than I prefer. I like thinner, lightweight denim. However, they fit well and are very comfortable, so I will wear them. The front pockets are enormous and extend nearly to the knee and are nearly the width of the top of the thigh! I am 5' 4" and usually wear a size 12 due to my thick waist. I ordered these in a size 14. If you have a slender waist, you could certainly order your regular size. These rest below my natural waist, even in a larger size. I imagine that these would take much longer to fully dry because of all the gathers and heavy weight denim. I usually only dry my clothes for a few minutes, then hang to fully dry to avoid wrinkles. If you think you would like this style jeans, I highly recommend thes Sidefeel Barrel jeans. They are well made and very reasonably priced!

Runs a little small, but very comfortable. I ordered a size 4, my usual size. They do fit me, but are snug from the waist to the hip. So, one star off for sizing, these being more of a Junior fit. They are so stretchy, though, that they are actually comfortable and will look nice with a waist length/crop top. No room to tuck anything into the waist, however. The length is perfect for me at 5’2” and 118 lbs. They do stretch out a bit while wearing, but if you want a real baggy look just size up one size. The fabric is lighter weight for a denim, with a lot of stretch. The seams down the center of the legs gives the legs a flattering, longer look.

Casual and comfortable These jeans are flattering and fit my daughter perfectly right out of the package. She is only 5’2” so it might be a little short on taller gals. She said it was very comfortable and loves the deep front pockets. The back pockets are very functional as well. On this light colored pair, the variations in color were a little strange in that it almost looked color blocked with a dark section in the waist/hips and ankles and a lighter section in the thighs/knees. I would have preferred a bit more subtlety. The stitching shows in some places on the side seams so I don’t know how well these will hold up in the long run. But overall, a good casual pair of jeans.

I make it a rule never to order jeans online since I've never had any luck. I took a chance as I liked the style of these and hoped they were comfortable. I am so happy I did take a gamble as these are much, much nicer than I even anticipated. The fit is spot on all around. The waist is not too tight or loose, the crotch area doesn't unflatteringly bunch up and the length is perfect. For reference I am 5'7", 130 lbs (I ordered a size 8). I do have longer legs and usually have a problem with the length being too short but these are not too short or too long that I step on them. These are listed as high waisted but honestly they are not. For someone trying to wear these as high waisted pants, I could see how the comfort level would drop, as well as the length being too short. These sit nicely on my hips and as a 90's teen, it's what I personally prefer But I thought I'd mention that for those who do prefer higher waisted pants since the description is misleading. The material is soft and has a nice stretch to it. The zipper works well without getting caught and is smooth to open or close. I love the color (dark blue) and it matches the color in the listing photos. Overall I think these are well made with straight, even stitching and no loose threads. I personally find these comfy enough to lounge around the house in which is surprising as sweatpants are my go to. They are the 1st pair of jeans I can say that about. The material is 70% cotton, 26% polyester, 2% viscose and 2% elastane. Since I personally love these so much, I think they are priced well and would not hesitate to order another pair.

Great Fit! I am so happy with these pants! They fit well, are stretchy, and flattering. I bought 8S and they just touch the floor with no shoes on so they are a tad on longer side than most short length pants but not by too much For reference, I’m 5’2” and 161 lbs (thanks to Xmas treats 🥴), hourglass shape, pretty evenly proportioned w/ weight evenly distributed. W/ a mom tummy. They say to measure at my natural waist which is 33” an hips 41”. The size chart is way off or I do not know how to read a size chart?!. I am normally a size 8 or 10 so I bought both sizes and the 8 fit (a bit snug in waist but I hope to get rid of Xmas goodies waist) According to their size chart I’m a 12! I measured the size 8 pants on my body. from the crotch seam to the top of the pants is the rise =10.5” Waistband =35.5” Hips = 41” Inseam 30” The waist sits right in the center of my navel. If you have a shorter longer torso they may sit higher or lower - which is why garment measurements help

Don’t remove tags without double checking labels I genuinely do love this light weight style of pant I’ve been wearing them for over 10years, being 5’3 I also know that I have to order (R) regular or (S) if I’m using them as dress pants and will be wearing boots. Recently ordered what was labeled as a Size 10 (regular) the pants that arrived although excellent quality were very visibly labeled as 10 (Long) as much as I’d love to return them that isn’t an option as this pair was literally replacing the only pair of work pants that fit me. So if you see me in public with my jeans cuffed at my ankles like a small child whose parent bought pants entirely too long, no you didn’t.

We’ll fit wide leg jeans Over the last year, my daughter has really gotten into super wide legged shorts (or jorts) and pants. I totally understand why though. At her age I loved super wide legged pants too because they’re cute and incredibly comfortable, not to mention they’re really in style. These are no different. These are made from a fairly light to mid-weight denim material. It’s soft to the touch and feels nice against the skin. Because they’re not thick you can wear them year round. My daughter already had a pair of shorts made by this company so I ordered the same size in these as I had in the jorts, which was size 4. The waist fits her perfectly. They’re not too baggy in the waist, they don’t slide down and she doesn’t need a belt but can wear one if she wants to to add some flare. These are definitely super baggy jeans as shown, but they’re supposed to be for this style. They really do look as pictured. These are a bit long on her, but that’s common because she’s on the shorter side. Overall, these seem to be well-made out of a comfortable light to mid-weight denim. According to my daughter, they are very comfortable and despite how baggy they are, they really do fit very well and the waist and they run true to size. I’ve ordered my daughter wide legged jeans shorts from this brand in the same size and these seem to fit very similarly. I feel like the $40 price tag is a little bit high but it’s not unheard of as long as the jeans hold up as well as the shorts from this company have I’ll get my money is worth. For a reference my daughter is 5‘2“, 115 pounds and we ordered the size 4 and they couldn’t fit more perfectly other than the length.

Inseam doesn’t match the size designation. I ordered the 28 Short expecting a 28" inseam, which is standard for petite sizing. However, the item arrived with a 30" inseam—more in line with a regular 28. Just a heads-up for anyone shopping petite: the inseam measurements may not match the label. A 28 Short should typically have a 28" inseam, while a regular 28 usually comes with a 30" inseam.

Perfect jeans to elongate legs, but color bleeds 4 stars instead of 5 because it is not colorfast. First pic is after 3-4 rinses in the sink. Second pic is after the 6th rinse. I would not wear this with anything white until it is run through the wash a few times. Otherwise, I love these jeans because they are very flattering to girls with short legs like me. I’m 5’1” and less than 108 lbs but am blessed with ample hips and short legs. If it’s too high waisted I look very bottom heavy. If too low rise my proportions look off. These have just the right amount of high rise. The dark wash, straight leg and positioning of the back pockets all do me favors. I will have to hem these as I do with almost all pants and will not be rolling the ankle because the inner color of fabric is light. Rolling the cuff will create a light band at the bottom that detracts from the elongating effect. Fabric does feel nice and soft yet sturdy. It’s not stiff and there is a slight stretch to accommodate ease of movement. Seams are all well done and pockets seem well reinforced. I expect these to last me many years. Amazon fashion purchases are always hit and miss, but if you are disciplined about returning items you don’t love, true bargains can be had. This is definitely a score despite the color bleeding.

Size discrepancy UPDATE: I *did* order the lighter wash (called Fascinating Fact) in a size 29 waist hoping for more room in the thigh, but in the image you can see the gap I circled in the back. it's pretty bad. All you old school Levi's gals, the ones who ordered that Supreme Curvy cut they used to have: these pants are not for you. When I returned the 726 style in store, the manager confirmed: it appears that an ample derriere is no longer in style, and they are not making pants for us right now. ORIGINAL REVIEW: What sadness! I got the 28x30L darker wash (Carbon Glow) and loved them (I measure 24 natural waist, 41 hips). But it seems like a miracle that this pair worked, because I then ordered the lighter wash and I have no idea how they’re the same size or even the same cut. Much less room in the bum area; you can see in this picture where I overlapped them how much of the dark pants sticks out. The crotch, the waist height, the thigh area - everything is so much smaller. I might order the 29 waist and see if they work, but this sizing inconsistency is so sad.

They are unwearable because it’s so noticeable and this isn’t the style you can cut off and make into shorts or whatever. Im so disappointed. Also, after washing the waist became very tight, which is fine because I am very thin and have a 25-26” waist, but if you have any padding in the midsection, I’m not here to judge, but they might be very uncomfortable because there is zero stretch. Great quality denim, but fit & length depend on height and styling This was my first time trying Amazon Essentials jeans, and I was genuinely impressed with the quality of the denim. The fabric is thick, structured, and feels durable, with just enough stretch to be comfortable without sagging. I’m 5’8”, 135 lbs, ordered a size 2, and the fit through the waist and hips felt comfortable and true to size. Although marketed as high-rise, these wear more like a mid-rise, hitting just below the belly button. The pintuck seams down the front and back look better in person than expected and add a nice design detail without feeling overdone. The main consideration is length and overall proportion. On me, they hit right at the top of the shoe. This actually works well with sneakers or high-top Converse, which is very on-trend right now, and in photos they don’t look bad at all. Where they fall short for me personally is with heels. I was picturing a wide-leg, elongated look with stilettos, and the combination of the shorter length and heavier cuff doesn’t create that long-leg effect. I also noticed that when trying to wear them higher on the waist to achieve a true high-rise fit, the jeans ride up uncomfortably through the crotch and seat, flattening the butt rather than accentuating it. Because there are no longer length options, this limits who these jeans will work best for, especially taller women or those wanting a more dramatic wide-leg silhouette. Overall, these are well-made, stylish jeans with excellent fabric and construction. They work best if you like the current cropped-at-the-shoe look and plan to style them with flats or sneakers. Had a hard time deciding between 3 or 4 stars because the quality is there, but fit, rise, and length won’t be ideal for every body type or styling preference.

Too short!! Comfy and true to size otherwise. Too short!!! What a bummer. I’m 5’5”, 137 lbs. I ordered size 4, my normal size. They fit fine but they are just too short. I often have this issue with pants, to be fair. At 5’5”, I’m not a tall gal, but I must have long legs because I always need to buy the tall versions of pants. I like my pants to touch the ground after I have shoes on. And I’m always wearing heels. The shoes I’m wearing in the photo are just my flat slippers. I also felt like the bum pockets were too little, it didn’t look right on me. I’ll include some photos to show the length on me. Other than that, these seem like a nice quality pair of jeans. They were comfortable on and had some stretchiness to them. I liked the color too.

I think what makes these so flattering on me is the high waist, the front flat felled seam and the length. They are super long on me but I think I can wear them with some high heels and make them work. I ordered a small and I'm 5'2", 115lbs and short waisted. I'm not sure what they mean about "wide leg jeans... straight leg pants" but here we are. I am glad I got the white pair because each of the blue variations had that weird distressed front look that I think is way too trendy to last long in my jean rotation. And I think $40 is relatively low priced for jeans, no? I will say that if they were not long enough to wear with heels, I might not like them as much, so if you are taller or have longer legs you may want to take that into consideration.

Wide leg, comfy & no shrinkage in hottest water ⭐️: These jeans in the darker wash and wide legs with the vertical front seam are giving me a sprinkle of 70s retro vibes, with a tiny splash of 90s bagginess. They’re fun. :) 👍: The fabric is surprisingly comfortable, although there are a few “raw” seam edges inside at the top waist which are irritating to my sensitive skin. 👖👍: The length on my 5’4” frame goes almost to the floor and based on the item’s product size chart and actual item received, the jeans I got run true to size. ➡️: SIDE NOTE: do wish these came in a smaller size, as they are baggy on my 26” waist, so they aren’t the most flattering in the booty area. But they don’t gap out in the back as many jeans tend to do on me! ⬅️ 🧺 For those needing a smaller size other than a 4 or just wondering about shrinkage in the wash– I did launder and dry a couple times on the hottest setting to try and shrink but they did NOT shrink up at all. 🤪 I would have given a 5 star rating if the seams inside were smoother, but overall these are priced really well at $40 for a fun, on-trend and comfy pair of jeans! Might as well as try ‘em out if you are curious. See my Video for fit.

Accurate Size Chart and Comfortable, Flattering Jeans! These jeans were a pleasant surprise. I’m a standard size 4, and they fit true to size - hallelujah for an accurate size chart! The Trendy Queen chart was spot-on for me. The denim is incredibly soft, and the bit of spandex makes these very comfortable. They stretch just enough to move with you while still holding their shape - no pinching, no feeling “stuffed into” your jeans by midday. Comfortable and flattering? Yes, please. And the price is a steal for on-trend denim! The length hits right at the ankle on me (I’m 5'6"), which I expected. These are slightly cropped, so if you’re looking for a full-length wide-leg jean, this may not be the pair - but for an ankle-length, relaxed fit, they’re perfect. I ordered the Icelandic Blue, and it’s even lighter in person than shown online, with less of the distressed “wear” look from the photos - which I actually prefer. Clean, fresh, and super easy to style. Soft, stretchy, flattering, affordable, and true to size - these are an easy 5-star addition to my denim rotation!

They stretch just enough to move with you while still holding their shape - no pinching, no feeling “stuffed into” your jeans by midday. Comfortable and flattering? Yes, please. And the price is a steal for on-trend denim! The length hits right at the ankle on me (I’m 5'6"), which I expected. These are slightly cropped, so if you’re looking for a full-length wide-leg jean, this may not be the pair - but for an ankle-length, relaxed fit, they’re perfect. I ordered the Icelandic Blue, and it’s even lighter in person than shown online, with less of the distressed “wear” look from the photos - which I actually prefer. Clean, fresh, and super easy to style. Soft, stretchy, flattering, affordable, and true to size - these are an easy 5-star addition to my denim rotation!

Flattering and fabulous fit! Love, love, love these jeans. They’re not my usual fit I wear, but I’m sold. These jeans fit really well and are extremely flattering. The high waist hugs in the right places, and the wide-leg style gives them a polished, on-trend look. They’re comfortable enough to wear all day and easy to dress up or down. Every time I wear them, I get so many compliments. Definitely a staple piece and worth the purchase. For the for, the size guide was spot on. I’m 5’6, 170 lbs. and I got the medium. They fit great and I could size down for a more snug fit. Get you a pair!

Not True Jeans – More Like Jeggings, Sizing Runs Small These are definitely not jeans—they are jeggings. The material is very stretchy, which I personally like, but if you’re looking for a true denim feel, these are not it. I’m also curious to see how they hold up after several hours of wear, whether they’ll keep their fit or start to sag. I ordered the ripped 1010 light blue, and the pair I received does not match the picture. The color is different, and the placement of the rips and bleach marks are off. At 153 lbs, fit build, I usually wear a medium, but I ordered a large because I wanted them to sit lower on the hips and fit loose through the thighs and legs. Instead, the large fit me perfectly—not baggy at all—so I’ll be exchanging them for an extra-large to get the fit I originally wanted. Overall, if you like stretchy jeggings, these could work for you. But if you’re expecting solid denim that looks exactly like the listing photos, you may want to reconsider, especially for the price. I’ll update with another review once I receive the extra-large.

Didn’t want to like them this much I ordered 2 pairs of these jeans(the 1010 and the 1009 variations), thinking one looked looser and one a little tighter in the pics and planned to keep only one (if any). While I’m still not entirely sure the differences between the 2, one has slight paint splatter, I’d say they fit the same. And I’m keeping both!! They have an incredible amount of stretch in them without looking like jeggings lol. They feel like that super comfortable broken in pair you have right before you need to wash them because they’re looking too stretched out, except that despite feeling that way these don’t look it. So far they look and feel the same from the start to end of the day. I’m 5’1”, 107# and ordered the XS, if there was an XXS, I might have tried it since I am pretty small, I could do with a slightly shorter rise but overall I say these Amazon jeans are a winner..

Absolutely Perfect! Purchased these in a medium and they are my go to jeans! They are denim material but soft and stretchy like leggings! They are perfection! I love the design. If you’re in between sizes I would recommend sizing down because they are crazy stretchy which is amazing! For reference I’m 5’5, curvy body with blessed with a booty. My weight fluctuates a crazy amount due to some health issues. In the picture I’m probably 175lbs. I am ordering a 2nd pair in a small because when I lose weight these are too baggy with the amount of stretch, around 160lbs. The paint splatter is cute and these make me feel younger than my 42 year old body says I am. My kids love them on me too which is a plus obviously! If I’m not living in leggings I have these on. They hit right below my belly button, which is a great height, usually I wear high waisted but these were a great compromise.

My favorite jeans! These are perfect! I am 210, 5’4 (apple shape). They have a nice stretch and fit around my waist comfortably and are not so big in the leg that I look like I’m in balloon pants. I ordered both 2x and 3x and did not notice much of a difference, so I am keeping both.

Color is way off, but comfort fit. Comfort and fit 5⭐️ but the color is not a match to 1009, which looks like a distressed acid wash. These are straight up a regular mid blue. I’m 4’11” and 125 and fit is relaxed in Medium, not falling off. I’d say I’m between a small and medium and these are comfortable, soft stretch. Since they’re a bit loose, stretch isn’t really relevant, but they are really comfortable.

Finally, Jeans That Don’t Make Me Look Like a Potato Sack I’m 5’2” and usually buying jeans feels like choosing between tripping over extra fabric or looking like I accidentally borrowed a child’s pair. But these? The “short” length is actually short enough for me—no impromptu denim hemming project required. 🙌 The fabric is a little thicker than what I expected for stretch jeans, but honestly, I’ll take it. They hold everything in place without feeling like I’m wrapped in shrink-wrap. Let’s just say I can bend over without hearing the distant threatening rip sound that some other brands give me. Bonus: they’re well-made, comfy, and flattering. My husband said, “Nice jeans,” which in husband language is basically a standing ovation.

Keeping Them! This is my second pair of “Flamingals” jeans. They are really soft. The other style I got, I bought the medium and they’re a little big so this style I bought the small. (I’m a size 6/8 for reference) I really like this style, but the cuff is stitched and they’re a little shorter than I would like, therefore I gave them a 4 star review. I’m not that tall 5’7” but I’m all leg. I wish the cuff wasn’t stitched so I could cuff them myself. That said— I’m keeping them and the small— although not baggy—Fits well. The last pair stretched out as the day went on, so I’m guessing these will too.
