I wanted to love these I am 5ft 8.5" tall, at tje moment I have a 33" waist, 45" hip and 27" thigh. In the pictures the pants have been washed and tumble dried low. Before washing I was excited about these pants, after washing I was super bummed. I knew they would shrink because of the cotton content, but there was significant shrinking. The covers for the strings all but crumbled. I love the fabric of these pants, and the softness. They are a good weight for spring and fall. I would say they are good winter pants except my ankles would be freezing, lol. I love that the back has elastic and it has a string for tightening on the waist. I am someone who fluctuates in weight quite often, so these could fit multiple sizes without a belt. I was hoping for the oversized look, if that's what you are looking for I would suggest sizing up when factoring shrinking. I ordered my true size based on size chart (14). They fit perfectly (relaxed fit) before washing. Unfortunately I did not get photos before washing.

Khaki cargo pants, good material & quality, looks polished but feel casual Even though the listing calls them jeans, the khaki option clearly looks like a khaki pant in the photos - and that’s exactly what I was expecting and what I got. The material is solid and not at all thin, and the color matches the pictures perfectly. There’s a drawstring front plus some elastic in the back, which works well for my shape (wider hips, narrower waist). I also like that these come in numbered sizes rather than just small, medium, or large, which makes it easier to get a good fit. They hit right at the ankle. That’s not my favorite length because of how I grew up thinking “too short pants”, but it’s definitely in style today. My workaround is to cuff them slightly, which actually looks deliberate and holds well thanks to the sturdier fabric. They’re comfortable, versatile, and could work as casual or even a little bit business casual depending on how you style them. One small drawback is that there’s no back pocket. For me, with a rounder shape, that makes my backside feel more highlighted than I’d like, but that’s easily balanced out with the right top. The cargo-style pockets on the sides are functional and deep without feeling too young or out of place for me at age 50. Overall, I found these pants to be high-quality, true-to-size (I wear an 8 and the 8 fit well), and well worth the price. They feel more polished than other cargo pants but still just as comfortable. Pros Sturdy material, not thin True-to-size numbered sizing Elastic back and drawstring for easy fit Stylish ankle length that can be cuffed Functional pockets Comfortable for all-day wear Cons No back pockets, which may not flatter everyone Ankle length may not suit all preferences Product: Dokotoo Womens 2024 Fall Mid Waist Jeans Casual Cargo Pants Drawstring Elastic Waist Khaki Pants with Pockets

Comfortable and Stylish! I’ve worn these jeans on several occasions now, always paired with low heels, and they’ve quickly become one of my favorite pieces. They’re incredibly comfortable, the denim has just the right amount of stretch so you can move easily without feeling restricted, yet they still hold their shape beautifully throughout the day. The fit is very flattering, with a stylish wide/flared leg that feels trendy but still easy to dress up or down. The fabric is lightweight and soft, which makes them perfect for longer wear, and they look much more elevated than your typical casual jeans. Every time I’ve worn them, I’ve received compliments, which honestly says a lot. They strike a great balance between comfort and style, polished enough for going out, but comfortable enough to feel relaxed all day. Definitely the kind of jeans I keep reaching for again and again. For reference I am 5'3 and weight 137 lbs. Ordered a size 8

Decent pair of jeans These jeans have a great fit overall and definitely deliver on the “butt-lifting” effect — they’re flattering and shape nicely without feeling too tight. The stretch is just right, giving enough flexibility for comfort while still holding their form throughout the day. I also appreciate the length — at 5’2”, they hit perfectly at the ankle without needing to be hemmed, which is rare and a big plus. The fabric, however, could be a bit sturdier. It feels slightly thin compared to higher-end denim, and while the stretch is nice, I’d prefer a bit more structure for long-term wear and shape retention. Still, they look stylish, fit beautifully, and are comfortable enough for all-day wear. Overall, these are great jeans that flatter the figure and fit petite frames well — just wish the material felt a little more durable.

It feels slightly thin compared to higher-end denim, and while the stretch is nice, I’d prefer a bit more structure for long-term wear and shape retention. Still, they look stylish, fit beautifully, and are comfortable enough for all-day wear. Overall, these are great jeans that flatter the figure and fit petite frames well — just wish the material felt a little more durable.

Amazing quality pants I am a big fan of this company. I used to order their regular yoga pants from time to time. I had no idea that they had skinny jeans in stretchy material. So I was so happy to receive them because I know that their quality is amazing from previous purchases. And it was, when I opened it up the package , the material looks like jeans. It doesn’t look like a sublimation print of jeans, the fabric itself looks like jeans from a store. They are a little long on me. I am 5 foot 4 and 115 pounds. I know that you can cut them. I prefer not to cause I do like the hemmed look. Both the front and back have pockets that you can use. There’s no zipper or buttons to have have to deal with. And there are no belt loops. I think the quality is great and I think the price is also amazing. I couldn’t be more pleased with them.

These jeggings are excellent quality, very stretchy. These fit true to size. I am 5 feet 4 inches and around 118-120 I got an XS and they fit perfectly. If I wanted a looser fit I would size up. They look very jean-like and the material is different than the regular yoga pants, not as soft to the touch and more, well, jean-like. Stretchiness is just about as good as regular yoga pants though! I love them because I can wear them as jeans, leggings, or whatever. Very comfortable and good pockets. I would recommend them if you are looking for jeggings that are really high quality and look great. Excellent value because these are going to last a long time. I've already washed them three times over the last two weeks and had no fading (I wash them inside out.)

Super comfortable, Size down These are honestly some of the most comfortable jeans I’ve owned. The fabric is super soft and stretchy — maybe a little too stretchy for my personal taste. I’m used to denim that holds its shape more, so these don’t show the figure as much as I prefer. Fit-wise: I’m 5’4” and these are the perfect length for me — no hemming needed. I ordered a size Small in mid-blue, and I’m usually a US size 4. They fit well overall, but I do think an XS would have fit me better because of how much stretch they have. The color is great and looks high quality in person. For a barrel fit, I personally would have preferred something a bit more balloon-like, but these are still very nice and polished. Overall, super comfortable, great color, and easy to wear — perfect for a casual date or everyday outfit.

More comfortable than expected Alright, look. I’m a millennial mom trying to hang onto my skinny jeans for dear life. This new wide leg jeans trend is significantly less flattering for those of us with hips, unless you’re into elephant legs. Especially for the short hourglass girlies like me. I’m 5’2” and currently in my holiday-hibernating-storing-fat-for-the-winter era. If I can’t have my skinny jeans because they’re “out” then at least give me some comfortable wide legs jeans, ok? I reluctantly ordered these barrel leg jeans from Zesica. I’ll start by saying I do really like this brand. I have a quite a bit of Zesica clothing items in my closet now, though mostly dresses. This was my first jeans purchase from the brand. Here are my thoughts: 1. They ARE comfortable. I’ll give them that. They’re roomy and baggy. Don’t expect tummy control. They’re so loose in the waist that I could pull them pretty far out, and because of this they did make me look like I had a pooch. Not loving that so much, so tucking shirts into these jeans is probably out. I generally wear a 8-10 and could have fit into a M, but I sized up to a L to accommodate these hips that don’t lie. 2. Because of their barrel leg shape, they give “Canadian Mountie Oompa Loompa.” Not gonna lie, I don’t even hate it. Like I said, they’re crazy comfortable, and some days I’m too tired to care about anything beyond that. I could Oompa Loompa through all of my errands in these, but they feel a little too casual to count as dressy jeans for a night out. 3. The color looks like the listing photos but it also doesn’t. I can’t put my finger on how. I’m certain I wore this color jeans in the 90s. 4. The stretch. So much stretch. These jeans stretch harder than our moms did in 1992 to a Richard Simmons VHS tape. 5. They’re cut short, like the listing photos. Remember, I’m 5’2” so even ankle jeans are still grazing the tops of my feet. They’re high-waisted just like the description says. 6. Expect a very breathable and lightweight denim. They *almost* feel more like soft cotton than denim, like the jeans version of a favorite t-shirt. They’re most definitely true to size — I’m probably less than 5 lbs away from these being too big on me and would need to switch out for my regular M, even as a curvy girl. They’re very on-trend right now, so if you’re on the fence, I’d say go for it. While not the most flattering jeans for my particular body shape, I’ll still rock the Willy Wonka out of them.

Pretty good jeans! Weird pockets though. I’m 5’4” and currently around 220 lbs, usually wear an XL, but ordered an XXL based on the size chart and they fit very comfortably, especially over my postpartum belly flap. They’re baggy without looking sloppy and have a tapered leg, which I really like for winter since they don’t drag or catch snow. They’re comfortable to sit in and don’t press into my stomach, even while breastfeeding. I will say there is a bit of a weird thing going on with the front pockets where it's like there's not enough material between them and so the white part of the pockets ends up showing sometimes. These are not pants that you're gonna want to wear with a shorter shirt, or with your shirt tucked in like where you're showing the button fly of the pants. I'm not really comfortable showing that part of the pants so you'll have to just trust me about it. It's not flattering! The denim has good stretch, and I probably could have sized down to an XL and still been fine. I ordered the mid blue color and it’s a nice darker wash with a very 80s-style denim look. Overall, they’re flattering, comfortable, and easy to wear for everyday outings and I can overlook the pocket issue because there's so much I still like about them. I'm obviously not sure how they would fit for someone who is thinner, but for a person who is trying to figure out how to dress a newly plus size body (thanks babies!). These have definitely made me very happy and given me a solid option for my winter mom wardrobe.

Comfortable, but Barrel? So right off the bat, these are incredibly comfortable. It’s more like a denim pull up pants than actual jeans. It’s denim, but thin, and and light. The way they’re cut is like a comfy pant. Even the way their pockets are placed toward the front and loose. It’s great as an alternative to sweats or like a pant to wear around the house, but easily wear out and about. I was nervous about this barrel jeans trend, but i think these end up fitting more just like a wide leg jean than barrel. I ordered my usual size Medium (for reference, I’m 5’3” about 150-155 lbs. ). Maybe if I sized down, they’d seem more barrel, and now that I’ve been wearing them, they seemed to have stretched out too, even though the actual jeans aren’t stretchy. It feels really wide. The wash color was great dark, like pictured. And it’s good for short girls, but hits more like regular length, than ankle , like would on taller ladies.

It’s great as an alternative to sweats or like a pant to wear around the house, but easily wear out and about. I was nervous about this barrel jeans trend, but i think these end up fitting more just like a wide leg jean than barrel. I ordered my usual size Medium (for reference, I’m 5’3” about 150-155 lbs. ). Maybe if I sized down, they’d seem more barrel, and now that I’ve been wearing them, they seemed to have stretched out too, even though the actual jeans aren’t stretchy. It feels really wide. The wash color was great dark, like pictured. And it’s good for short girls, but hits more like regular length, than ankle , like would on taller ladies.

These are not real denim. They have ZERO cotton content in them. I prefer natural fabric content but these are working ok. I do like that the pockets are actually usefully. The drawstring works well for those bloated days. Taking into account the fact that the material is not even real denim, I do not find the price to be worth it. I would not purchases these in the future, not at that price. The length is good for a tall person (5’10”). The coloring is all right. Overall, (other than material content) I am happy with the pants.

Go up a size The sidefeel women's wide leg jeans, high waist are pretty comfortable. I'm 5'2 at 200lbs and usually wear a 14 in jeans. With the high waist, I was not sure how they'd fit so I ordered up a size and glad I did. They're baggy in the legs but fit great in the waist. Now at 5'2 they are a little long but nothing a pair of boots won't handle. The color is a dark blue and the pockets are deep. Speaking of pockets, they do come down on the thigh area a little more than I like. It's just a personal preference. The seams are well sown and the zipper stays up. Overall it's a good pair of jeans. At the current price, the quality is lower than expected for what you pay. Otherwise they're something I'd recommend for unimportant events or outside work.

These wide leg jeans are quite comfortable. The fabric is soft and feels good against my skin. The jeans have a good bit of stretch, and the waistband doesn't bind at all. The denim is soft, not stiff, and the wide legs hang/drape well. The weight of the denim is good, too: not too thin, not too heavy. They're a solid mid-weight that will be very versatile. I chose the "bluing" shade, and I like it a lot. It's a very traditional dark blue denim that goes with everything. The jeans are actually a good cut, to the point I admit I was surprised. The seams are straight, and the seat is fitted. There's no weird bunching, even around the pocket area-- and even when sitting down! The design is actually impressive and is really, REALLY good for the price point. These look like more expensive jeans, so I honestly feel they're a steal at this price. I got my usual size and think the fit is accurate. I'm just shy of 5'4", and the length is pretty good on me. I normally have to buy a petite to keep my jeans from puddling on the ground, but, with shoes, the length is appropriate on these. Another happy surprise! I'd been wanting to try the wide leg jeans that are in style right now, but I just couldn't quite bring myself to spring for a pricey pair of jeans to do so. These jeans allowed me to try out this style at a very fair price point, and I'm so pleased with the quality of these pants!

Longer and wider than expected but great if you are 5’5 or taller I like these jeans for how they fit in the waist. So many jeans on me are too wide in the waist and look silly. This style however, was too wide in the leg for me. (5’3) for my height they just looked big and silly. My daughter tried them on and liked them. She is about 5’5 and I to the baggier look in jeans. They are made well and comfortable. They look like they will hold up really well in the wash. My daughter liked that they will look dressy with heeled boots.

Great for short people. I’m 5 feet tall, so at the time I ordered these in size 11, I weighed 148 lbs. They felt snug compared to the size 11 I ordered in another shade. I’ve since lost more weight and the size 11 still fits fine. If your short like me, knowing that Wallflower still doesn’t make their regular jeans in petite sizes, if you roll the cuffs down on this particular style, they will fit fine like the jeans length. They came down around my ankles, if your okay with that. For some reason, I’m constantly having to pull my pants up which is annoying. I don’t need to wear a belt as these are snug. The material is medium to thick imo. Color is nice and dark. No bad smell noticed, nothing ripped yet. Oh, it also came with pet hair.

These came used !!! Washed and kept due to how I liked the product, not dirty, but... The item is great, love the pants. HOWEVER... these were sold to me as new, they had tags, BUT I found this in the pocket after I wore them to town. I kept them because I really like how they fit. There was no smell and they were not dirty, but they should not have been sold as new when they obviously were not. Less than one star for the seller and misrepresentation of the item being new. Check your pockets when you buy things! (I'm about 5.5" and 149 lb and size 11 fit perfectly... not loose and not tight).

Comfy and Adorable! I loved the first pair so I bought 2 more pairs in different colors! Haha! These are so cute, I got them to wear around the house when pajamas just felt too lazy in case someone came over.. The ruffles are super cute on these and the material is soft. There’s deep pockets and belt loops. Real zipper and button like jeans but no need, you can just pull them up. The size Small is perfect on my 5’4” frame. I’m around 125lbs now for reference and I think I could gain another 15 or lose 15 and still be ok in these. I do feel like the darker wash is a little roomier for some reason but still fit. I did a review for shoes and was wearing these pants so here’s a picture of at least the length standing and where they hit top of my foot in a pair of Mary Jane flats. Although I bought these for lounging at home, I wear them out since they are so cute! Looked adorable with red flats (also Amazon), white tee and a straw bag to the farmers market. Can’t beat the price either. Anthropologie is selling similar for $150 a pair.
Do I get a second pair? I’m short - 5’3 and 200 lbs and I love these!! They are longer on me but not too long. I got size 16 and they are a very comfortable fit!

Cute and fun jeans with a trendy look These Astylish wide leg jeans are such a cute and fun addition to my wardrobe. The ruffled hem really sets them apart and makes them versatile. You can dress them up with a blouse for an evening dinner date, or pair them with a casual T-shirt for a cozy, relaxed day of shopping. The denim is made from a thinner lightweight material, which makes these jeans very comfortable to wear. They have a slight stretch, but I didn’t find that they stretched out or lost shape throughout the day. Since these aren’t made from standard heavy denim, there isn’t a lot of tummy support which I personally would have liked, but that trade-off does add to the overall comfort. I will say that I struggled a bit pairing them with shoes as they didn’t quite lay right with some styles. After some tinkering I found they looked best with sandals or an above-ankle boot. Fit-wise, I’d say they are true to size. I ordered a size 8 in dark blue, and they fit perfectly for my 5’4” height and 150 lb weight. Overall, I personally love these jeans. They’re stylish, comfortable, unique and I would definitely recommend them if you’re looking to add something fun and trendy to your wardrobe.

Fabulous! Very stretchy and comfortable. I love these jeans!! So much I have two pair. I let my neighbor try them on and she bought two as well. I had back surgery and needed something loose around my back and this was the ticket. I needed a pair of jeans for winter. Tired of wearing workout pants all the time. I am 5'6 150 lbs 31" length and the medium fit me best. If my incisions on my back weren't so bad I might be able to get away with a small for sure. I have not had a problem with shrinkage. Medium thickness, SUPER stretchy. Great value for the cost.

Great Maternity Jeans These are super comfy and stretchy! Perfect for pregnancy and I’m sure it will be nice for postpartum too! I’m 5’5, 120lbs and 17 weeks pregnant but there’s plenty of stretch that I’ll be able to wear these the whole pregnancy. I’m not a fan of how the over the belly maternity pants look and feel so I was very happy to find these! The only downside is they’re a bit thin and can cause a slight camel toe :/ & I also wish they had a skinny jean option but the length is nice & the pockets are real!

Doesn't hold up Comfy, soft, stretchy, I love them. Bought more but wish the dark have a 33" inseam option. I absolutely love that you can pick different inseams! I am 5'7" & go for 33" and the back grazes the floor. I love them. Not too long, not too short! Update: I have room to move around in them, I do not exceed the size and I am not in denial. I walked by a mirror & noticed a color difference in a spot on the side. Looking I noticed that the stitching is coming apart showing the darker in under then I turned and the exact same spot on the other side is coming undone. I loved these and ended up buying another pair but now I am hoping the other doesn't do this and won't waste my money on more 😔

Functional pockets! I've gained some weight recently and regular jeans just don't work for me anymore. I find them to be too stiff or too tight or too restrictive so I've purchased a couple "jeans" from this brand before because I love how comfortable they are. This pair is no different even though the style is not exactly the same. These "jeans" are not the usual jeggings that are totally fake and look fake (jeans print). They actually look and feel like real jeans but only thinner, softer, and more stretchy. They are pull-on pants with the crossover wrap high waist like yoga pants and flare legs like bell-bottoms. I'm 5'1" and 130lbs and I usually get the shorter 29" inseam so I can wear them with sneakers. These are a little long for me (31" inseam), but I can wear them with boots or other heels. Also, all the pockets are functional and I'm a sucker for pockets!

Inseam not 31", comfy, more like leggings than jeans Update: At time of order, sizing included choosing inseam length. It's now changed to petite, standard and tall, and my review header shows "standard", which was definitely not the case and I have the invoice to prove it. Just FYI if you read my review- I'm not crazy, I ordered a 31" inseam. Maybe they've sorted out their inseam problem with the new sizing choices, idk. Original review: Fit/Sizing - I'm 5'8" tall, US women size 6/Small. Ordered Small w/ 31" inseam. Fit is comfy like leggings, but inseam is over an inch shorter than stated. Regular sizes are often too short on me (and tall sizes too long, ugh), but these are offered in different lengths, and I really wanted that length. Awkwardly short at high ankle. Stretch out slightly while wearing, tug them up occasionally. Materials/Design - I like the cotton/poly blend. Most of this is evident from product photos, but in case there's confusion, these are definitely leggings w/ a denim look, not to be mistaken for "stretchy flare jeans". Mostly because the waist is pull-on, no zipper or closure. The flare swishes looser like a legging than denim, which is sturdier. Wide crossover waist reminiscent of maternity wear. Not a high-end look but that's ok, still more put together than sweatpants and as long as the length works for you, fit is flattering, doesn't smash the rear or dig in at the waist. Rating details - I take time to write honest reviews that I would find . Previously ordered similar pants from diff. Amazon store, also had wrong inseam. But I liked the hybrid look for lounge wear that wasn't too frumpy, excited someone else was offering them, and they have the same problem! Fit details are important, it wastes time and effort ordering something that is wrongly described. Plus, there is a large obvious defect in the material. Value is subjective but if I saw these at T**get I'd expect about a $25 price tag.

Not my favorite, but cute! For sizing reference, I'm 5'3", currently 145 lbs with measurements about 40"-30"-40" (bust-waist-hip), and I'm wearing size 4-6 in jeans or a medium in bottoms in most brands right now. Another random note but I have a shorter torso with longer legs (proportionally), aka I rarely wear short length in pants and even mid-rise bottoms hit pretty high waisted on me. I purchased these jeans in a SIZE 29 in the colorway ACCENT OF AZALEA. As far as sizing goes, I went with the safe bet and ordered a 29, despite being a pretty solid 28/6 right now, and I think it was a good choice. I could have done a 28, but these are comfortable, so I’m fine with it as long as they don’t stretch out. Like most bootcut jeans, these are fitted through the waist, hips, and thighs, then looser in the calf area. I’ve been wanting something that isn’t a skinny or straight leg, but isn’t too big and baggy, and these did the trick. I can’t even remember the last time I owned bootleg jeans but I’m glad I tried again. They are a little long and drag a bit when I wear sneakers, but nothing too dramatic. If you actually wear boots (which I don’t), they’re probably perfect. For the material, these are a thinner stretchy denim. I doubt they would last through years of wear and tear, so if that’s an issue for you, maybe try a different style like the 501s. They do have a zip fly, thank goodness… The construction is also pretty good. The color of these is nice. They’re a lighter wash that doesn’t dip into looking bleached. It’s a nice change if you’re someone like me that mostly sticks with standard denim shades. Overall, I’d definitely recommend these if you’re looking for a more casual, thin pair of bootcut jeans.

Boyfriend gives stamp of approval (he noticed, haha) I'm in love with these jeans!! Love that they've got some stretch to them (they did bag out from some casual around the house wear, but nothing crazy.) They are certainly high waisted, after a meal I did have to undo a button for comfort because I prefer my size, if not a little smaller than my usual size because of the stretchy material. My boyfriend says the "fit like a glove!!" So I've won in that department. They feel sturdy, great value for quality and material has a good amount of give to it so I'd say they're more comfortable than the standard for of jeans, I did order 2 different colors / styles from above (I'm 182lbs, 5"4' and usually about a size 14. The dark pair fit perfectly on me in a size 32 x 32. For some reason I was sent the incorrect size in the ripped knee light wash pair so I'll be returning those unfortunately (I got sent an 18 L... not sure how that happened and kinda disappointed but I love this pair before so much I'll keep it at 5 stars.) The question is: are they real Levi's?! I thinkkkk so?! A bit hard to say but winter way I'd recommend the dark wash racer stripe pair. If you're between a 180-190 and between 5"4' to 5"6' don't size up or down, stick with the 32 x 32 !!

Stretchy, forgiving but slightly too long! I got the *Eternal Indigo 30 Regular*. For reference, I’m 5'5”, about 155 lbs, with shorter legs and a medium torso. I originally ordered the *short* length, but they were too short. The regular length is about two inches longer, which was a surprising jump in length, but they ended up working okish with heels or boots, still a little long for my personal preference.. may get hemmed. The fit is very flattering: slim and stretchy on top, with slightly more than a bootcut and a subtle flare at the bottom. They definitely have real stretch, so even if they feel a little tight at first, they’re forgiving and comfortable. I’m four months postpartum so a little extra water weight wasn’t a problem with the forgiving stretch of these jeans. They sit just *below* my belly button, so I wouldn’t personally call them high-waisted, but the overall fit is still good enough that I’m keeping them. One note: they did lose a bit of color after the first wash. I’d recommend washing cold on delicate and hang-drying to keep the dark indigo.

725 vs 726 I'm 5'8 with a long torso but I have short legs. I wear around a size 14/16, and I have wide hips and a larger lower stomach. I ordered a 34/30 based on the measurements listed and they fit fine, but I don't think these are the jeans for me. Here's a comparison review between the Levi's 725 (bootcut - color - is it denim) and Levi's 726 (flare - color - seams to work). I prefer jeans that have a higher rise and sit above my belly button to help lessen a Muffin top. The 725 sits a little higher above the belly button than the 726, but neither sit much higher, and it's like they sit right at/slightly above my bellybutton. The 725 have a medium/high stretch, while the 726 have a more rigid, mild stretch. For the 725, I could probably wear a 33, while a 34 is necessary for the 726. I prefer the look of the 726, but the 725 are more comfortable. The 30 inch inseam is great for my shorter legs. I've attached pics wearing both of these styles, and side by side comparison pics.

Very durable! I am 5'4" and have an hourglass figure, with a 40" hip and 28" waist; so it is difficult to find a pair of pants that fits well; but these pants were a pleasant surprise. I usually only buy curvy jeans; but I needed some that would not only fit me comfortably but would also last a long time. My curvy jeans fit me like a glove; but they are almost worn away after only a year. So I thought I would try these Levi's, at least to wear when doing chores around my family's farm. I bought the 27 Regular size. They were on sale for $50 instead of $70, otherwise I would never have gotten them. But I was paying $40 for my other jeans to only last a year. I figured that, if these Levi's last me a few years for only $10 more, it would be worth the difference. These jeans are a much better quality than my other jeans. They are just slightly large about the waist and a little snug around my behind. But that difference is totally doable, for me. Unfortunately, I cannot wear my backbrace underneath these pants (which I have to wear almost every day because of my broken back). It just doesn't fit. So I can only wear these pants when my back is feeling alright. These pants have very spacious pockets and did not have any shrinkage after the first wash. I would definitely recommend these jeans. They feel very durable and the stitching is very well done. The back pockets are a little oddly wide. When I put my phone in my pocket, it feels way too close to my hip to feel right. But that doesn't bother me enough for it to be an issue. These jeans look so good with boots! They fit so perfectly over my cowboy boots that I feel like I have never had a pair of actual bootcut jeans, even though that is the shape I have been getting for over a year. They are the perfect length and width to fit over my boots without getting in the way of the heel. This was a great purchase, for me! (edit) I took away a star because, after more wear, I noticed that the dye in the jeans would bleed onto my skin at the knees whenever I had to kneel down.

Love these .. I love the quality and feel of these jeans. A nice, heavy fabric, yet soft and comfortable. However they are LONG. I know I'm not tall (5'4") but very disappointed that the cute bottom edge will need to be cut off. I'm having to cut 6 inches off of these jeans. And although the description says "zipper closure" they are actually button up...really cute and I loved this feature in 1971 when my fingers weren't arthritic! However it is a REAL struggle to button these since my fingers just don't work like they did in the 70's!! Fortunately I can slip them on and off with only the top two buttons undone and hopefully it gets easier to fasten them. Great jeans - especially if you are nice and tall!

Nice heavyweight As a short 4’11” gal I like to get cropped style pants because a lot of times the hem fits me like a regular and saves me the pain of having them hemmed. I received the HUDSON Jeans Women's Rosie High Rise, Wide Leg Jean, Black Ash, 28. This size fits me great and it feels amazing. Hudson jeans are known for the quality and the denim is soft with a tiny bit of stretch. As soon as I put them in they felt amazing. They’re still a tad bit long for my short legs. I washed them in cold but did not put them in the dryer until I checked on shrinkage. They didn’t shrink much at all so I think I will run them through the dryer and see if they shrink a bit. They are quite nice and I think quite flattering on me.

Very Essential Pants! I saw the mauve pants while browsing at a bargain price. I when I tried them on, I didn’t want to take them off. They looked great, fit perfectly and were so comfortable that I felt like I was wearing PJs. I ordered them in size 0. I am just shy of 5’ and thin, so finding casual but dressy slacks that fit is difficult. The material is soft and a perfect thickness to wear all year, not too heavy but provides enough warmth for cooler days. Being cotton with a tad of stretch keeps them from wrinkling a lot and looking fresh. In fact I loved them so much that I went back and bought every color available. Nice to not wear jeans dominate my choices. I took a picture showing all the colors which I bought. Oh, I love the style with the simple zipper in the front and welting on the legs. Also a feature is the fact that the waist hits my real waist and makes me look taller. Kudos on these VERY Essential pants.

I’m a curvy size 12 and these trousers are a 12. I didn’t think the zip would do up when I first put them on but the trousers have a good amount of stretch in them and they fitted great. The only place they pulled a little was by the side pockets but the 14 would have been too big for me. So if you’re more an athletic 12 they will hang looser. I’m going to say they are true to size. They were very comfortable to sit in and loosened up again. They are 3/4 length on me being 5’7 but they are a cropped style so perfect for Summer and evening wear. Wash on warm. Made in Indonesia. Amazing value for money. I hope my video helps to compare body sizes and I’m happy to recommend ☺️

Need some chinos? How about some 3/4 lengths? These are the Good Threads Womens Stretch Chino Utility Pant, Made From 98% Cotton, 2% Elastane, Sizes 10 - 18 And Colours Black, Dark Olive, Ivory, Mauve, Navy, Camel Or Rust Orange. These chinos are thinner than normal chinos, they are slightly stretchy though. The length is 3/4 length, so almost like pedal pushers. They have 2 x front hip pockets, 2 x buttock pockets, so pretty standard jean layout with belt loopholes. The one thing that is odd, is the bizarre front zip, what is wrong with Traditional zips? The good thing is these are both machine wash and dryable, so no faffing around. But there will be colour bleed to begin with, so wash with similar colours. My eldest lives these, she is 5"11 and looks great in em with her long legs, and they fit superbly. She's happy. Overall I'd recommend these chinos, as they are very well made, albeit a tad pricey. Oh and overlook that bizarre front zip!

Okay for now I purchased these because I have a pair from Sanctuary that I LOVE, but I wanted a darker pair, so I figured I would give these a try. I tried on the pants and the fabric was heavier than I expected. It gets hot down here in Florida, so I like the light weight jeans. Also, the front pockets looked ‘off’, not symmetrical. When I took my Sanctuary pair out to compare them, the Sanctuary front pockets were much bigger, they had a decorative run of stitching toward the top, and of course, pockets were symmetrical. I posted photos, maybe you can see it. I got the medium as per Amazon’s suggestion. I’m 5’4, 138 lbs. they felt good enough so I washed them. I wore them out today and after a couple hours I noticed they were loose, my tucked shirt kept slipping out. I did like the length, I’m 5’4” with a short torso and long legs and they stopped above my ankle. I just ordered the small and hope they fit. It’s frustrating being between sizes. We’ll see how it goes and I’ll update

Great quality but sizing is inconsistent I’ve bought 3 or 4 of these in different sizes and colors. The fit is so flattering, with a kick flare cropped leg that hits above my ankle (I’m about 5’5.5”.) The rise is high, slightly above my belly button. The fabric is thick with medium stretch. The quality is good, with functioning metal zippers and well-sewn pockets. One pair snagged on something metal, but I latched it with one of those iron-on denim patches (applied behind the fabric), and it still looks good. My only issue is with sizing inconsistency. I have a dark and very light blue, as well as a black and washed black. Some are in Large and others in XL. One of the L pair is unwearable because it’s so tight (med blue - tight in waist and upper thighs), but another fits - albeit snugly (also med blue but a different shade). The XL pair in light blue is wearable but quite baggy. The washed black L fit pretty well but shrank slightly and now digs in quite a bit in the waist and crotch when I sit down. I would order more but am afraid to get a pair that’s wildly small or too baggy. Everyone on my office has bought these because they look great on so many different body types.

Inconsistent colors, sizes weird, fabric issues For context, I’m a 41 year old female, 5’4”, 175 lbs, recently lost 45 lbs and somewhat athletic build. I’ve had 4 c-sections and have a bit of a belly and the ol’ caboose is a little big (not overly though). Originally, I ordered the 13/14 (waist) x 30 (inseam) in the Dark Indigo. I’m short, but the 30 inch inseam was about 2 inches off the ground - higher if I bent or sat down. Too short for my taste. Legs were too tight for my liking throughout the thighs, calf and especially the knees. The waist was too tight as well. The first time I tried them on, the waist was okay-ish, but when I went to try them on again for a double check, I had eaten a small lunch and they were extremely uncomfortable/restrictive. Amazon allowed me to exchange them for the same pair in a different size, so I ordered and just received 15 (33 inch waist) x 32 (inseam) which luckily, was right before I was leaving to return the first pair (cuz these ones are going back with them). Fabric is that thinner, saggy-type stretch where if you wore them for an hour or two they’d start drooping. The first ones I ordered were a sturdier, stiffer jean material which I prefer over super stretchy droopy jean material. The waist line and lower abdomen or ‘Fupa’ area is too poochy. Length is better with the 32 inseam. A little longer than I’d prefer, but something I’d be fine with if the rest of the jeans were decent. One of the biggest issues is the color. The 15 x 32 are waaaay too light to be ‘Dark Indigo’. In the photos I provided, they are the pair on the left. I wouldn’t have kept them even if they fit because I don’t dig light pants on me, hence why ordered Dark Indigo. Definitely inaccurate description (photo on Amazon doesn’t match either). The 13/14 x 30 on the right, I wouldn’t necessarily call Dark Indigo but the wash is really flattering on my frame. Hope this helps.

Quality, comfortable and flattering high waisted jeans. Fit/Size: The product fits me well, I have a hard time finding pants, especially jeans, that fit me. I am 5'2 about 150lbs and have short legs and wide hips so it is very difficult to find pants that not only fit me length wise but that will also fit properly over my hips as well. Surprisingly these fit with no problems and are comfortable on my waist without being too loose or too tight. I would have preferred these jeans be a bit more form fitting around my derrière and then widen more because they feel loose throughout so I feel like I lose some shape, but considering they fit so well everywhere else it seems like a fair trade off, I am hoping a couple more washes will help fix this. I also notice they do end closer to my ankles so when I sit down they rise up higher which seems to be how they are meant to fit, I don't wear wide leg jeans so it bothers me a bit, but overall the look is flattering and the fit is comfortable, I only mention this so buyers are aware they will sit higher especially with heels. Color: I ordered Lakeside blue and I really love the color and it matches the online photos. Quality: These jeans are very well made, the material feels durable and soft so they are very comfortable to wear. I don't see any wonky, loose or poor stitching. The pockets are decently sized on both the front and back and feel like they will keep their shape well. Based off the quality I think these are a great value for a pair of jeans that will last me for a long time.

Could be cute but run short and small. Don't seem to complement curvy figures. I ordered these pants in black, because I was looking for a bit less contrast between the regular jean color and the flipped up color in the legs. I do think the contrast is less obvious, but I was disappointed in the overall fit. As background, I'm very tall at 5'11", but I have shortish legs, and I go between tall/long pants, which often only work well with heels, and regular length pants, which tend to ride up when I sit. I estimate these pants at maybe 1/2 in shorter than most regular length pants, so on me, they're too long to be high waters and too short to be regular pants. Even more upsetting for me, these pants are very straight from top to bottom with no flare at the end, while I'm on the curvier side. This means they're unflattering to my body shape, with no shape of their own to improve the look. And although I ordered a size up, since my size normally falls between the stated ranges, these ended up being too tight on me to be comfortable. Overall, I think these pants are just ok. The stretchy jean material is nice, the stitching seems solid, and the washed out black is an appealing color for casual dress. I'd say they run a little small and a little short, though. If you're an average sized or shorter person who's not too hippy, these might be cute. Unfortunately for me, they don't work.

converted cuffed jeans into tall sizes jeans This review is for the “ GRAPENT Wide Leg Jeans for Women Trendy High Waisted Cuffed Hem Stretchy Denim Trousers Pants Work Casual Jeans“ I ordered the white jeans plainly based on the look of the cuff. There are no inseam measurements state in the listing, but in the photos they looked long. I was lucky, I am 5’10, 156 lbs and ordered the Large. The fit and size are perfect on me, and when I opened the seam and let out the cuff the pants came to a 34” inseam, exactly what I need. There are so few sellers offering tall sizes that I had to become creative and find ways to convert regular jeans (like cuffed ones) into something in my length. I love the quality and the stretch of the 2% elasthane. The pants washed well and i stretched them lengthwise to get even a half more inch in length. he white is not see through, a great bonus! So, for all the tall ladies, if you see these, the cuff is not just a piece of cloth sewn on, it is actually the leg of the pants just folded up and sewn into a tall outside cuff. With some sharp scissors and a bit of patience you can take the cuff off easily. the price of $37 is a good deal for this nice pair of jeans. I would get this item/set again.

BEST JEANS! EVER! Over the years, I've gone from living stretchy jeans, to essentially trying to LIVE in super stretchy, jegging type jeans. I don't have a lot but I want more. I'd love to look good while feeling extremely comfy, so I took a chance on these. I am 5'3 and have a 32" inseam but these are almost perfect as they are. They have a cute fit. I'm not crazy about the little stitches on the back pockets (seems unnecessary) but the fit is just cute and flattering! Style wise, I love that these look like regular jeans as they have real zipper and button closure, real pockets, but have the comfort of Jennings or pajama denim. I want these in a million colors!

Give these jeans a try - You might be happy too! These are a soft denim with just the right amount of stretch to be comfortable, but not being all stretched out on you during the day to where you have to yank on them all the time I would totally give a five star rating for comfort and fit, but the denim coloring in the stock photo is way off of what you receive. The photo looks very attractive. What you get is a nice denim but it’s more of what you expect of every day faded look denim. The stock photo looks spectacular. You don’t get that color in actuality, but they’re amazingly comfortable. I love the side pocket in this particular variety from the company. because it fits your cell phone nicely and totally hides it away. I like that it’s a very loose fit after coming down from the skin tight skinny gene era. It’s nice to go back to comfort while not compromising style. It might be difficult for some to decide on sizing for these because general small, medium, large type sizing is kinda vague. Every person’s body style is different. So it’s very hard to say they fit “all” but these happen to fit me perfectly. I bought the medium I am 5’7” and I weigh 145-150 it fluctuates, I’m 64 with a little pooch and a flatter bottom 🤣

Very substantial jeans Nice jeans. I ordered the dark blue also. Only discrepancy was the dark blue didn’t have left pocket on leg. Not a problem with returning for. FYI I’m 5’6”, 120#. I ordered the small.

Check cuffs to see that they are even when you get them. Material is thick and stretchy. I am 5'1" tall and 145 lbs and purchased a large. I tried them on and they are at my ankles since I am short but that's okay. I haven't worn them yet but I washed them to get the stiffness out. When I was folding the cuffs back up after washing, I noticed that one cuff wasn't straight. I just thought it was ironed wrong but no...it's actually sewn crooked. I was very disappointed because there isn't a lot I can do about that. Since I washed them I didn't think that Amazon would take them back, and anyway who's to say if I would get another pair the same. One good thing is this defect is in the back cuff so I won't be able to see it. But, I would have expected better quality after I paid $30 for this pair. I never pay that much but I liked the cuffs. So...a little disappointed in them but will keep.

Comfy capris Great pair of capris. I’m 5’2” and weigh 203, I ordered a 2xl but had to return for an xl. With the stretch material and elastic waist, I was able to go down a size. I normally wear an 18. These feel good on and are very comfortable.

Comfy and Stretchy! Moves with You! WallFlower Fearless Curvy 17.5" Capri Super High-Rise Insta Vintage Jeans, SIZE 1, COLOR Evan: I'm 5'2" about 193lbs. I have a 39” Waist and 45” Hips, Upper Thighs 26-27” and Lower Thighs 20-22”. I normally wear a Large or 12/14. These size 11 fit me nicely. I don’t consider these ‘super high-rise’ by any means. I have room to breathe, sit, and squat. These jean capris are made with stretchy jean material. I consider these to be of good mid-weight quality. I did not notice any loose strings or seams. The darker color is flattering. I can see how these will stretch out throughout the day, but I haven’t found a pair of jeans that don’t. I also like how I can fold over the bottom to make them look more like bermuda shorts. Overall, I’m very happy with these. They are comfortable and run true to size, maybe a little larger than true to size, but I haven’t washed/dried these yet. Would recommend.

good quality I’m always a 6 in stretchy jeans and I got a 6 in these; being super high waisted they’re a little snug around the waist (I’m apple shaped). A size higher would be too loose on my skinny butt and legs though, and they’re not uncomfortable or anything. They do have stretch. Heavy denim. No warning label about washing them before wearing due to dyes used. I got them for $10 what a bargain! I like the cut of the legs, they’re cute, it’s a nice happy medium bw skinny and super loose. The black is like a grey black. 5’9, 165 carry weight up top.

Love them. The jeans are great! Not much stretch so they don’t get loose and saggy after a long day. They are not thin but a decent Jean material. For the price, I’m very happy! You would pay double for this pair at a boutique. I’m 5’3 and 180ish# and ordered the large. Perfect for and they sit high which I like best. Business casual at its best. I’d get more but I don’t love that the others are the same print just different colors. I would love to see other patterns or a nice trending blue jean color.

Ordered every color love them Love love love... Ordered a lot of different brands they were too short even though they said 31 inseam. Ordered first the grey blue in medium 5'6 160lbs they were slightly snug as I pulled them up to my waist. Decided to order the large to compare. Large was much better. Looks like the trend with the much baggier jeans but still snug enough to stay up on your waist. Went ahead ordered them in the black and the other blue color. I have the light blue in my basket for a little later in the month. Would highly recommend. They are so comfortable it is hard to put on my regular jeans after wearing. Note: order the light wash the first pair came with a defect obvious 2 dark lines down one leg. Ordered a second pair same type defect but one dark line down leg and my fault I forgot to hit the 31 inch inseam button and they were too short. Just ordered a third pair of the light wash because they are worth all the effort to find a pair with no defects in the fabric

No bulk at the waist; flattering I'm 5ft 6in, about 118lbs and got the XS with the 31" inseam. You can see from my pic that they are a tiny bit long (but I'm in bare feet). They are a perfect length with sneakers and boots. The waist is a little tight on me, but not enough to be uncomfortable. The small would have probably worked for me too, but I like the slight tummy compression the XS gives me. Straight out of the package, they look like pull-on pants vs actual denim jeans, but they pass for the real thing when wearing. Before putting them on, the wide leg looked a little clownish, but having them on, they look cute! Very light whiskering at the top of the thighs in the Dark Blue Denim color. Functional front and back pockets. Soft and comfortable to wear. Easy to machine wash in cold and hang to dry.

Not Bell Bottom, They Are Wide Leg I'm not sure if I was sent the wrong item, but these are NOT bell bottom or flair. They are tight around the waist, butt, and thigh... but then just continue down to a wide leg pant. If you're looking for true bell bottom, do not buy these, it's a misnomer. These fit more like wide legs pants. I'm also 5'6" and these are almost too short for me. I'll keep them because they are just for work, but other buyers beware.

Missing the flare I normally wear my jeans with a straight leg that are too small to take off with your shoes on. I liked the way these jeans looked in the ad. They look curvy with a flare at the bottom, and I thought I'd give them a try. These are a good-quality pair of jeans with five pockets. The front pockets are pretty deep, with a lining that feels soft and silky. The zipper is silver-colored, although it's a little stiff. Upon receiving them and seeing their shape, I could tell that they didn't have the flared or "bell bottom" style. I tried them on right away and then took them off even quicker. Being plus-size most of my life, I've always hated jeans like this. It's not just the bottom that's flared, but actually the whole leg. I've always felt that plus-size jeans were made with the thought that just because you have a larger waist, you must have legs the size of an elephant. A bell bottom I can handle, but not elephant legs. I used to wear the largest size I could find, but now I wear anywhere from a size 12 to 16 depending on the pants and the amount of stretch. I ordered these in size 16, and they are too big; plus, they are very stretchy on top of that. They're just too uncomfortably big without any kind of curvy shape to them. Additionally, they are too long for me, but I'm 5'5", and I usually run into this problem. Currently, 25% off is a fair price, and they're a good pair of jeans for someone; just not for me.

Great fit, color, and quality These jeans are more comfortable than the ribcage straight jeans imo. Love the button fly. It’s fitted up the waist, but the denim has a slight stretch so it doesn’t feel restrictive. I bought a size 30 waist for a looser fit (normally a size 28). The wash is just as pictured, love the fringed hems. Levi’s quality is so reliable, these jeans will last a really long time with proper care. Just what I wanted!

Bells & Whistle in size 29, read if you want size details I got these on sale for $39.00 and love them more than I expected. I know sizing of Levi’s can be complicated, but I will say if you have the original 501 Levi’s shorts the sizing is very similar. I’m almost 5’4, weight is currently between 147 to 151 (I’m size 6 or 8 depending on brand) and size 29 fits me great. If I drop to 145, size 28 would fit better, say that based on these fitting like my 501 shorts in size 28 & 29. I also purchased the indigo darker color in a 30 and they fit very close to 29 but a little looser in the waist. I only returned them because I don’t need more jeans. My favorite thing about these jeans is the waist hits right at my belly button so they aren’t too high waisted for me unlike many high waisted jeans. Pic doesn’t show much of jean but allows you to see cut of them if you are also 5’4 like me. Pic doesn’t show much of jean but allows you to see color in daylight (Bells & Whistle) plus the cut of the jean if you are also 5’4 like me.

Nice pair of jeans I bought 3 different colors,“leisure time”(black wash),“indigo”(dark blue wash) and “lunch plans”(lighter blue wash). All size 29. I am 30” waist, 42” hips. Give or take half an inch. As reference. The “lunch plans” color definitely felt more stretchy/give to it than the other two colors which felt a little more structured/less give. However they all are labeled as the same material %’s. I could probably do the size 30 for a more loose fit on the “leisure time” and “indigo” colors but I’m sure with daily wear they will loosen a tad. I only keep them bc my hip to waist ratio always tends to have the waist gap on pants. The gap on these is not bad as it does sit flush to my back probably bc I chose to size down. Otherwise they fit good overall. I did not notice any bad smells. Material thickness was good quality, medium weight. They sit right at/just below my belly button which I like when it comes to jeans. Lastly, I am not a fan of the length, so I cuffed them to look cuter. Mine came as 28” inseams so they sat right at my ankle.

Same Levi’s $20 less!!! This is me wearing a waist size 31” in the “All the Bells and Whistles” color. I’m 5’8”, about 150lbs, and pear-shaped. I bought these jeans in the “soft-black” color at Target for $70 about a month ago and love them. I don’t usually wear such light jeans but thought I should try something different. They are super comfy and made well. My pair from Target washed well and didn’t stretch out too much when wearing. These feel light the same product (just a different color). I say all this because I’ve read a lot of reviews on different Levis purchased through Amazon and was worried I’d get a “cheaper” version of my Target pair (as in made cheaply, not price). However, this was not the case at all for me. I saved more than $20 buying this pair from Amazon ($48.99) versus the pair I bought at Target!

SUPER STRETCHY AND COMFORTABLE AND LOOSE FIT! LOOK GREAT ON ! check sizes Straight Leg Hight waisted jeans loose fit Jeans. These are SUPER STRETCHY! ( Igo up and down about 4lbs ) so great jeans would absolutely recommend, just high waist has to sit high.

Love! Short girl pants, wish I had sized down. These jeans have a fantastic weight to them and the cut is fantastic! I’ve been wearing them on repeat and have washed them a handful of times they launder and dry beautifully. I do wish I had sized down. They contain enough stretch to fit my short waist when sitting, had a small smaller size been ordered they would not be a bit bulky around my waist. These are the perfect short girl pants as I’m 5 foot 3 inches and I have absolutely no need to hem them, they are perfect length for me.

My favorite pair of jeans!! I needed a new pair of jeans so decided to try on these Dokotoo jeans for size! This is the first pair of size 16 jeans that I've bought in probably 15 years, really crossing my fingers hoping that these fit. Very pleasantly surprised! The pork chop pockets are cute and functional and so are the back pockets. The cuff area is more straight and not flared or bell bottoms. I love the way these Dokotoo jeans fit and the way they look! There is stretch around the waist AND all through the hips and thighs. The waist is a traditional button fly design. Took photos of the jeans on and off plus front and back. I am an apple shape and carry most of my weight in the belly area if that helps as far as sizing. (I'm 210 and 5'8)

Cuter advertised than in person Overall these jeans fit according to size and are comfortable to wear. Maybe this style just doesn’t work for me but these jeans looked much better advertised than they do in person. On me they seem to look a bit loose and frumpy instead of chic (5’5” and 120lbs for reference). If you’re looking for a loose fit though these work well. The tie on the front doesn’t stay in place well, it’s constantly coming undone. These would work better with a crop top or shorter top of some sort.

I love the fit and the look of these jeans I ordered the Classic Blue cropped, wide-leg pull-on jeans in my normal size after consulting the size chart. I am very happy with the comfortable, relaxed fit. The hem is a couple of inches above my ankle just as I expected for cropped jeans. I confirmed the 26 inch inseam with my tape measure, and while I was at it, I checked the other dimensions. They all matched the size chart, so these jeans are true to size for X-Large at least. I like the flattering look of these jeans with their wide legs and simple fly-less front. The functional drawstring is small and understated, and it looks nice. The front pockets are deeper than I expected which was a pleasant surprise. I also like the lack of any distressing. One difference from the photos is the thread. All exposed stitching was done with classic gold thread, not white. I actually prefer the gold thread, so I'm OK with the difference. The medium-weight denim fabric feels good and is not coarse nor scratchy. The overall quality of construction is quite good.

So far, I'm happy with them Pretty satisfied with these for a number of reasons: 1. They are lightweight, not heavy or stiff 2. You can't tell they are pull on, so you can tuck a shirt in if you like 3. The wide leg isn't overly wide 4. Although they do not have tummy control they still look okay on this 71 year old person. 5. They are true to size, I'm usually a 2 and the xs fits fine. For reference I'm 5'2". I have not worn them yet, or washed them, since it is still winter where I live and I'm still wearing fleece lined jeans, so I am hoping they wash up okay and do not pill as other reviewers have mentioned. That would be a big disappointment. BTW the spots must be on my mirror (in the photo) - they are not on the jeans.

Soft but Fragile! I had high hopes for these sweatpant jeans—and in some ways, they delivered. The fabric is incredibly soft and comfortable, almost like wearing loungewear disguised as denim. I ordered a size small (I’m 5’4”, 128 lbs, with a 28” waist), and the fit was perfect—not too tight or loose, and very flattering. That said, the material is unfortunately quite flimsy. Within just a couple of hours of wear, I noticed threads starting to snag, and the overall durability just isn’t there. I was doing yardwork or something physically taxing, just walking my dog around the neighborhood. As comfortable as they are, it’s hard to justify the purchase when they don’t hold up well to everyday wear. Great idea, but the quality really needs improvement.

Great Jeans overall depending on body type These jeans are a catch 22 for me. If I buy them in my usual size they are tight in the thighs. If I buy them to fit my thighs, they are loose in the waist. I also would prefer longer. I bought Regular length as I'm only 5'6". Although I really liked these jeans, I'm afraid that with my hourglass body shape I won't be buying them any more. I'm sure they fit other body shapes better though and are actually a good jean. Just not the best fit for me. They did fit better than most other jeans though.

So Cute, So stretchy, So Adjustable, So Versatile These are such nice shorts. They aren't too short and have plenty of room, yet look stylish and cute for almost any woman. They must be sized smaller since I can fit into the size M. The hips are a bit too wide for me. I'm 5'5" and 110 lbs. with a waist of 27 1/2" and hips of 34" and purchased the size M's which fit comfortably except the extra material in the hips. What makes these shorts so versatile for most anyone are the adjustments: all throughout with the stretch, then in the waist with the wonderful elastic in the entire length of the waist, and then the drawstring you can tighten up in case the waist ends up being too loose on us thinner women. And the leg length is just long enough without being too dumpy looking, but still modest enough for older women. They are so cute. There's no wonder why these shorts cost a bit more for denim shorts at $25.59, with all the adjustable features. They've been distressed washed, so they are a very soft. These shorts get an A+ from me.

Roomy and comfortable well made shorts I’m loving these shorts and am going to have to keep an eye out for this brand! I would absolutely buy these again in all the colors.

I will update my post after washing and if anything changes. Update: bunching on knee actually improved (is less) after washing!

Cute jeans but don’t fit right. I don’t normally get jeans online, but the reviews weren’t bad so I decided to give it a try. I’m 5’7 with wider hips and a bit of a butt. I went with the XL based on the reviews and they fit snug around my “ problem” areas, but I like oversized sweaters and shirts so I figured I could hide it well. The jeans are comfortable and have a nice stretch to them, but they fit weird around the rips. They kind of pucker and stick out, which I don’t like at all. Also, if I wear them with a bit of a heel they are too short. I’d have to wear them with flats for the length to work. Sadly, I’ll be returning them. I don’t love them enough to say they’re worth the money.

Comfy....but VERY loose in the behind and thighs! I bought the recommended size but these were just TOO baggy..literal fistfuls of fabric around the bottom and upper thighs. I am 5'7" and 143 lbs. I was so excited for these pants, but the fit was nothing like the picture. If the sizing and fit were right I would’ve loved these pants. The waist is comfortable, not restricting; and the material is not too thick or too thin. Unfortunately these were way too big for me. I ordered the XS (I’m 5’3” and 110lbs) and they were too long and too baggy.

Heavy denim stretch waist look at model closely 5’7” 195- 200lbs Size 18 is too big because I thought the regular size 16 would be too small. Should have bought regular size. I liked: * Heavy weight denim 70% cotton. * Stretch 28.5% polyester and 1.5 % elastic-elastine * Loose fit in belly and legs * Not tight on ankles * Real pocket hold a cellphone iPhone 13 Pro * Not too hot - not too cold * Very comfortable on the skin after washing. I did not like the length. I know they are supposed to be at the ankle. They can be rolled up to look stylish. This is great for business casual days at work and for nurses who wear regular clothes but still need to do patient care. Nice to not burst out of pants while bending. I purchased mine too large. I was worried that the belly would be too tight. The elastine lets the waist stretch without being granny style elastic. I recommend offering your actual size. I would buy again. I will keep these because they are not jeggings and can still be worn baggy style. I recommend a figure defining top to give you a shape because the pants are baggy. I will keep these size 18 and order another pair sized to my regular size 16 and then style like the model. The first pair will become lounge jeans and work around the house jeans because they really are comfortable without being jeggings. I recommend them but - let the model photos be your guide to styling.

Awesome jeans if you dislike "hard pants" but still want to wear jeans I'm 5'2", 115lbs and got a size 4. I'm 36 and my proportions have gotten odd the last few years - I have pretty big hips/butt but a smaller waist, so finding comfortable denim with a flattering fit that doesn't ride in the crotch or feel too tight has become a nightmare for me. These are PERFECT for my body type. I'm pretty outdoorsy and tend to go for a more relaxed and sometimes almost "streetwear" style of clothing, and these have really checked all the boxes. Very comfortable material to the point I can WFH in them and not feel too constricted. Definitely a nice, effortless fashion piece that can be paired with just about any top and worth buying in a few different colors.

FINALLY! Short and curvy girl friendly! Cute and really stretchy. I originally ordered a 12 but a 10 fits better. Fits good! Not too long for me. Something I’ll often run into the trouble of, is for whatever reason pants manufacturers seem to think wide hips mean long legs. I know so many narrow-hipped women with long legs, and wide-hipped women with “short” or average sized legs and a big bottom. I’m in the latter category. For whatever reason, it is often hard for me to find pants where the waistband will go around my hips, but the bottom of the leg won’t go past my ankles. I’ve gotten good at hemming the bottom of pants over the years because of this, but, luckily, I don’t have to with these! In fact, I often look for “boyfriend cut” jeans for exactly that reason, because they tend to fall just above or just under my ankles, while being the correct size for my hips and waist according to the sizing. These end just below my ankles, and won’t trip me over. (I’m 5’4” for reference). As much as I’d love to be able to rock the flattering boot-cut look, I can only pull it off on days where I’m wearing high-heel boots, and even then, I sometimes have to fix the bottom so the denim doesn’t go past my high heels. On a day to day basis where I wanna wear flip-flops or sneakers in the summer, it’s gotta be a boyfriend cut jean, so that it has the right shape and length, without being too circulation-cutting tight like skinny jeans would be. These pants fit PERFECT. The last boyfriend cut jeans I bought were from Levi-Strauss but they no longer have the boyfriend cut on here for that brand as far as I can tell. So I decided to take a chance on the Lee Indigo Riders series, and I’m so glad I did. It seems lightweight but durable, as long as you buy the correct size for yourself. These are not hugging my thighs (they’re not supposed to, it’s called “boyfriend” cut for a reason). But, they do make my butt look nice! And they hide my belly pouch reasonably well. If you’re looking for tighter jeans, instead of sizing down and breaking these like some reviewers complained of, these are *not* oversized, they’re boyfriend cut. Understand what that style means, and go buy yourself some skinny jeans if you want to wear tighter pants without ripping the seams with your thighs. These are not built to be super form fitting, they’re built to hug in a few flattering places and be slightly lax slacks type fit in other places. That’s exactly what I wanted, and that’s exactly what I got. Flattering but not “too sexy” and professional looking, kosher to wear around older family members, etc. I recommend these!

Great jeans! Love them! 160 I love these and own them in every color and in 3 sizes! 8,10 & 12. My weight fluctuates due to a thyroid issue. Anyway, I have trouble with jeans/capris because I have a small waist, thicker thighs and a booty! I'm 5'3 currently 160lb. Can weigh 175. I do have to roll these one time but that's just where I like them to hit at my ankle. The fit perfectly in my waist, thighs and butt. They are not high rise. They hit at my belly button and they do not sag in my crotch. I do not have a long torso just average. These have a little stretch however, you can wear them all day and if you had to one more time before washing & drying and they will not be all stretched out and baggy. They are true to size. They are slightly distressed. They wash and dry nicely.

The fit was nice, comfortable. I am 5’6”, 153 lbs. and I ordered the 8. The material is the kind to stretch out after you wear it a while. I kind of kept pulling them up at the back. I think I would like to have tried a size 6. But, I really like them.

These jeans are so comfortable and they sit on my hips without sagging in the crotch or bottom. I like my comfortable pants to sit below my big belly... that usually causes the butt to sag. These jeans feel just right and I like the look except for the slight poochyness on the sides right below the front pockets. I am 5ft5, 149 and ordered the 12.

These jeans are very baggy, even for boyfriend style. I would also say they are more mid rise than high waisted. I am 5'5" and 110 lbs and I ordered the size 2. I am very familiar with this brand and have some other denim by Cicy Bell, and size 2 has always fit like a glove, but I think these run quite large, and I wish they were a little more fitted through the thigh. A high waist of 28" is not a size 2, it is more like a typical size 4-6. I have not washed them yet, but I am hoping they shrink substantially. They are a good boyfriend fit style, but unless you wear a very fitted top, these are not all that flattering. I would recommend sizing down a size from your usual, if possible, for the most flattering fit.

Very nice soft material! These are super nice jeans, very very soft material and look to be well made. The size is a little snug on me but it could be because I just got off a cruise ship and may have eaten more than normal lol. The legs are wide and the waist area is clearly defined on the body. Sometimes when jeans are big on the bottom they are too wide in the waist area, not with these. Only other issue I had was that they were super long. I will need to cut off about 5 inches but then again I am only 5'2 so perhaps they are not made for petite people

Cute Fit For My Teen I got these Cicy Bell Jeans for my 13 yr old in size 2. She’s 5’1” tall and 95 lbs. The size 2 is just slightly too baggy in the waist for her. She must wear a belt to keep them from sagging. The length is a bit long, but not so much that she’s tripping on them. The baby blue color looks very trendy and cute on her. At first she thought they were too baggy, but I think that’s just because I should’ve gotten a smaller size. The only reason I took off a star is because the denim is stiff and not the most comfortable to wear. I’m hoping they soften up in the wash. The quality seems good; no loose threads or chemical smells. I think they are an ok value at $35.99 at the time of this review.

Loose and comfortable These pants are very comfy! I am short, 5'1", and these fit longer on me (as most pants do). They run a little big and have some stretch. I got the size 2, but wish they came in a zero. They are reqlly cute, I like a looser fit. They wash well and I'm sure I can shrink them a bit on high in the dryer. They look great with boots and I don't need to tailor them as long as I have a slight heel. Overall, these pants are flattering and very comfortable. The quality is nice and though the material is thin they seem like they will last. For the price, these are a nice pair of jeans that seem like they would fit a variety of sizes since they stretch.

Love these jeans, but size chart is off I really love these jeans, the reason for 4 stars is the sizing chart is off. Based on the waist measurement I ordered a size 10 which is my regular size in this brand (5’7, 150), but the waist is uncomfortably tight - it’s like a pinch waist. I need at least 1 size larger. At 5’7, I am really pleased with the length. These jeans are a nice light weight, almost flowy fabric which I love. Since I live in a warmer climate I appreciate the lighter weight. I am trying to lose some weight, so hopefully these will be more comfortable in the next few months. I would recommend these jeans, just be aware they would probably work better if you have an hourglass shape - and size up because I find the waist measurement to be small.

Good general-wear jeans, but not great. My daughter wears these jeans--she's quite tall and slender (5'9", size 4), so take that into account with regard to the following comments. She says they're okay and she'll happily wear them, but they're not her favorite jeans. The length is just right, but they'd be too long on women of regular height. Also, for "baggy" jeans, they were still a bit too fitted in the thigh. Not tight, but not roomy like baggy jeans should be either. Color and style are good. Waist fits well. Fine of general purpose wearing but she won't dress them up for work or going out. For the price, though, they're not bad. You could easily pay twice as much for equally serviceable (but not great) jeans.

I'm buying 2 more... 5'7", 211lbs - size 14First off.... I HATE going jean shopping with a firey burning passion. It takes forever and more often than not makes me feel very fat. And skinny jeans are even more difficult because they fit different and I normally wear bootcut and boots.. That being said, I bought these bc income tax came in, so if I didn'tlike them, I could give them to my daughter without feeling like I was blowing $ I should be using on groceries... I did my due diligence and read the reviews... everyone said they were true to size..... they were right. I love these jeans. The fabric feels great. I can sit with minimal muffin top (this is a normal occurrence for me these days, since having the kiddos) and they are super cute. I love that the tears are minimal and high on the leg... it makes them very fashion-boot friendly. You can dress them up or down and they're still adorable. Last but not least, they are affordable. Love. Love them! I can't wait for my backups to come in the mail!

Super comfortable/Great fit I am 5’2” and 160 pounds. Ordered the dark blue distressed in size 12 and the lighter blue with less distressing in size 14. Both were comfortable with my 37.5 inch waist and 44” hips but I decided to stick with the 14 for more comfortable sitting. The length was not too bad but I feel like a couple inches could be taken off for a better fit but that is always to be expected for us short ladies. I love the mid waisted jeans for front tucking tops. The stretch is perfect and the jeans fit very comfortably. My only dislike is that I wish the whiskering/shadowing was higher toward the top. I am a little self conscious about the lines going across my thighs, but other than that these are great, inexpensive, trendy jeans for a mom on a tight budget that hates spending high prices on herself.

Old school denim that fits well on curvy ladies! These jeans are thick denim that have a sturdy feel. They are 99% cotton so there isn’t much stretch, but they have a relaxed fit and run true to size. I’m 5’7” with a 28” waist and 40” hips. I have an athletic build with thick thighs and hips. I ordered a size 28 long and the waist and inseam are perfect for my height and measurements. The waist doesn’t gap at all and sits about 1” below my navel. The bottom of the legs hits between my ankle and foot so they won’t drag on the ground or look like high waters. They are loose enough in the hips and thighs to move freely and feel comfortable while sitting and doing activities. I purchased the green ivy color and it’s a beautiful shade that will go with most colors.

Let's Talk About Sizing... I have several pairs of these jeans & I love them all. Never returned any. I am 5'2", 110lbs and I have always gotten size 3. This time I got a 1 based on amazon's reccomendation. I think I'll stick with the 3 moving forward as these are a bit tight. I feel a little self conscious wearing these since they fit like 2nd skin and I coyluld use a butt lift @ 52. They are comfortable though as there is plenty of give. If you are purchsing these for the 1st time or are unsure of what size to choose, I would definitely say NOT to go up too many sizes. I see people who say differently, but I think those people are not being realistic or don't actually know what their REAL size is. These measurements are 100% accurate. I have checked each pair that I own against my measurements- every one is dead on.

Style 92000 is definitely boot cut/straight NOT wide leg. There seems to be a lot of confusion as to the style being sold. I believe this is possibly because a few different styles are being sold under one listing. I could be wrong but I think if you focus on the number listed as the "Style" instead of color it may help. I ordered "92000" in dark blue (see pictures) and the legs are not wide at all. I would even consider them almost a straight leg rather than bootleg. I am short (5 ft 1 in) and average around 100 lbs. I bought a pair of Wax jeans size 1 (different listing/style) last year and while I really like them I have found they have really loosened up over time and are almost too big now. I went with a size 0 this time and while quite snug on me they are not uncomfortably tight and given how much my last pair stretched with wear I'm pretty sure these will as well. The size 1 was way too long (easily fixed; when you are short you get used to everything being too long) but the size 0 is only 1 or 2 inches too long. In summary: Style 92000 is NOT wide leg. Size 0 fits a 90-105 lb body well, though will be a bit snug for anyone over 100 lbs and is only slightly too long for someone around 5 feet tall. Very nice looking pair of jeans. Recommended for anyone who doesn't like skinny jeans yet wants comfort and style.

Sizing is interesting Sizing on these was really difficult. It took a few tries to figure it out so hopefully I can help someone. After reading about how raw denim works, it is best to choose a size that is slightly tight along your body.Because as you wear them, it will conform to your body.And stretch about a half an inch. So keep that in mind when you are looking at the size chart.You may want to go smaller I am 3 month postpartum looking for pants, leaving me somewhat squishy in the middle leaving me at a 31 waist and 42 hip. When I tried the size 11 to my measurements they where waaay too big. I tried the 9, still not good. Left crotch saggy. So I decided to skip the seven and went to the 5. I like the fit in the legs and honestly I think I will get them to fit great after constant wears, just a lil tight in the waist but I think if I get them wet amd wear them around I can fix that. I think the moral of the story is I woild size down 1 or even 2 sizes if you like your pants to fit tighter and not have a baggy crotch. This is coming from someone tall without alot of booty so If you are more curvy you may have a different experience but if u look like me size down. In the end u really like them and I think they will last me a long time

Not the color I ordered. Update please read I like the way these jeans fit they are made of cotton and they do not have any stretch. They are like the kind I wore back in the day. They are made to last.. But I ordered the lite color and obviously I was sent the wrong color these are much darker. I really wanted the lighter color for an event I was going to but now it’s too late. I’m not sure if I want to send them back or not because these kind of pants are hard to find. Plus who knows if I would get the color I want even though I couldn’t get them in time when I needed them. I took too stars off because I was sent the wrong color. Other than that I really like the jeans. For reference I’m about 5”9 and weigh 130 pounds I ordered the size 3-36 and they fit perfectly. Update… I decided to keep the one that they sent me that was the Wong color. I tried again and I got the right color this time 😁😁 but these don’t fit the same as the darker ones. They are a little bit big in the behind. The length is great it’s hard to find jeans that are long enough. I have washed them 2 times in hot water hoping they would shrink just a little (I have lost 8 pounds since I posted my review on the darker!(I wish it was more) I’m happy they didn’t shrink in the length.The bottom of the pants keep rolling up I’m not sure why. I’m Trying to gain some weight so maybe they will fit better. Overall I like the pants if I gain too much weight I might need some that are bigger and I will definitely come back and buy more of these pants.
